eng: and get your fields ready; after that, build your house. Do not testify against your neighbor without cause—
imn: liuc leiz ziangx meih nyei lingh deic, nqa'haav cingx daaih gomv biauv. Maiv dungx zoux maiv maaih gorn-baengx nyei zorng-zengx hoic juangc laangz mienh,

eng: would you use your lips to mislead? Do not say, “I’ll do to them as they have done to me;
imn: yaac maiv dungx longc meih nyei nzuih nduov mienh. Maiv dungx gorngv, “Yie oix zoux bun ninh hnangv ninh zoux bun yie.

eng: I’ll pay them back for what they did.” I went past the field of a sluggard,
imn: Ziux ninh zoux nyei yie oix winh bun ninh.” Yie jiex mienh lueic mienh nyei liangx-ndeic,

eng: past the vineyard of someone who has no sense; thorns had come up everywhere,
imn: jiex mienh hngongx mienh nyei a'ngunc huingx, mangc maah! Maaih njimv cuotv daaih buangv ndau-beih,

eng: the ground was covered with weeds, and the stone wall was in ruins. I applied my heart to what I observed
imn: maaih njimv nyei miev mbiorngz buangv, weih jienv nyei la'bieiv-laatc yaac mbaang. Yie yietc binc mangc, yietc binc hnamv,

eng: and learned a lesson from what I saw: A little sleep, a little slumber,
imn: yie mangc liuz ziouc duqv hoqc hiuv yietc nyungc aqv, aengx bueix dangh, m'njormh dangh,

eng: a little folding of the hands to rest— and poverty will come on you like a thief
imn: qopv jienv buoz m'njormh da'hngatv dangh, jomc nyei jauv ziouc liemh zeih taux hnangv janx-zaqc.

eng: and scarcity like an armed man.
imn: Dangx-donx nyei jauv oix daaih hnangv mienh dorh jienv wuoqc ginc daaih taux.

eng: These are more proverbs of Solomon, compiled by the men of Hezekiah king of Judah: It is the glory of God to conceal a matter;
imn: Yiem naaiv mingh yaac benx Saa^lo^morn nyei cong-mengh waac, dongh Yu^ndaa hungh, He^se^ki^yaa, nyei mienh ceu daaih. Zorqv sic gem jienv benx Tin-Hungh nyei njang-laangc,

eng: to search out a matter is the glory of kings. As the heavens are high and the earth is deep,
imn: mv baac hungh diex nyei njang-laangc se ninh zorqv sic zaah cuotv daaih. Lungh ndongc haaix hlang, ndau ndongc haaix ndo,

eng: so the hearts of kings are unsearchable. Remove the dross from the silver,
imn: hungh diex nyei hnyouv yaac hnangv wuov nor zaah maiv toux. Zorqv cuotv nyaanh nqaiv,

eng: and a silversmith can produce a vessel; remove wicked officials from the king’s presence,
imn: nyaanh zaangc ziouc duqv nyaanh daav cuotv ga'naaiv. Zorqv nqoi yiem hungh diex nyei nza'hmien nyei orqv mienh,

eng: and his throne will be established through righteousness. Do not exalt yourself in the king’s presence,
imn: ninh nyei hungh weic ziouc bangc baengh fim nyei jauv liepc gorn-ndoqv wuonv. Yiem hungh diex nyei nza'hmien maiv dungx taaih ganh hlo,

eng: and do not claim a place among his great men; it is better for him to say to you, “Come up here,”
imn: fai souv jienv zoux hlo nyei mienh nyei dorngx, weic zuqc maaih mienh gorngv, “Faaux naaiv oc,”

eng: than for him to humiliate you before his nobles. What you have seen with your eyes do not bring hastily to court,
imn: se gauh longx dorng jienv zoux hlo nyei mienh zuqc mienh heuc meih tuix njiec. Meih nyei m'zing buatc nyei

eng: for what will you do in the end if your neighbor puts you to shame? If you take your neighbor to court,
imn: maiv dungx siepv-siepv nyei dorh mingh wuov sic dorngh. Nziex maaih ganh nyei juangc laangz mienh daaih bun meih zuqc nyaiv, setv mueiz meih hnangv haaix nor zoux? Se gorngv meih caux dauh juangc laangz mienh nzaeng,

eng: do not betray another’s confidence, or the one who hears it may shame you
imn: oix zuqc caux ninh mbenc sung, yaac maiv dungx dorh ninh gem jienv nyei sic mingh gorngv. Nziex muangx haiz nyei mienh bun meih zuqc nyaiv.

eng: and the charge against you will stand. Like apples of gold in settings of silver
imn: Meih waaic nyei mengh dauh yaac maiv haih longx aqv. Gorngv yietc joux waac puix duqv horpc ziangh hoc,

eng: is a ruling rightly given. Like an earring of gold or an ornament of fine gold
imn: se hnangv norm longc jiem zoux daaih nyei biouv lorqc jienv nyaanh lorqc. Cong-mengh mienh kuinx maaih m'normh oix muangx nyei mienh

eng: is the rebuke of a wise judge to a listening ear. Like a snow-cooled drink at harvest time
imn: se hnangv jiem-buoz-ndoqv-nzaeng fai zien jiem zoux daaih nyei siou-setv. Ziepc zuoqv nyei douc fienx mienh

eng: is a trustworthy messenger to the one who sends him; he refreshes the spirit of his master. Like clouds and wind without rain
imn: bun paaiv ninh wuov deix ziouv nyei hnyouv fingv, hnangv siou gaeng-zuangx nyei ziangh hoc maaih sorng tengx laangh. Mienh bungx waac-maux gorngv oix fungx zingh nyeic mv baac maiv fungx,

eng: is one who boasts of gifts never given. Through patience a ruler can be persuaded,
imn: se hnangv maaih mbuonx maaih nziaaux yaac maiv maaih mbiungc. Longc noic yuoqc duqv zoux hlo nyei mienh.

eng: and a gentle tongue can break a bone. If you find honey, eat just enough—
imn: Gorngv waac suonc zungv haih bun ninh nyei hnyouv mau njiec. Se gorngv meih duqv mueiz-dorngh oix zuqc nyanc horpc.

eng: too much of it, and you will vomit. Seldom set foot in your neighbor’s house—
imn: Nyanc camv jiex jaax nor, nziex haih lov cuotv. Meih oix zuqc lauh lauh mingh nzunc meih nyei juangc laangz mienh nyei biauv hnangv.

eng: too much of you, and they will hate you. Like a club or a sword or a sharp arrow
imn: Maiv zei aeqv, nziex meih bun ninh haiz kouv ninh ziouc nzorng meih aqv. Zoux jaav zorng-zengx hoic juangc laangz mienh wuov dauh

eng: is one who gives false testimony against a neighbor. Like a broken tooth or a lame foot
imn: se hnangv mborqv jaax nyei ndiangx-ndonx fai nzuqc ndaauv fai forng-cang laic. Zuqc zeqc naanc nyei ziangh hoc kaux maiv ziepc zuoqv nyei mienh

eng: is reliance on the unfaithful in a time of trouble. Like one who takes away a garment on a cold day,
imn: se hnangv nyaah nquaqv fai zaux waaic. Haaix dauh baaux nzung bun hnyouv mun hnyouv nzauh nyei mienh muangx,

eng: or like vinegar poured on a wound, is one who sings songs to a heavy heart. If your enemy is hungry, give him food to eat;
imn: se hnangv juangv nyei hnoi jaiv lui, yaac hnangv zorqv wuom-sui dox njiec zuqc mun nyei dorngx. Se gorngv meih nyei win-wangv hnyouv sie, oix zuqc bun hnaangx ninh nyanc.

eng: if he is thirsty, give him water to drink. In doing this, you will heap burning coals on his head,
imn: Se gorngv ninh jaang nqaatv, oix zuqc bun wuom ninh hopv. Meih hnangv naaiv zoux ninh ziouc nyaiv aqv,

eng: and the Lord will reward you. Like a north wind that brings unexpected rain
imn: Ziouv yaac oix bun zingh nyeic meih. Baqv bung nyei nziaaux dorh mbiungc daaih,

eng: is a sly tongue—which provokes a horrified look. Better to live on a corner of the roof
imn: mienh gorngv waac-huv yaac cuoqv mienh qiex jiez. Yiem biauv-ngorh gu'nguaaic nyei gorqv

eng: than share a house with a quarrelsome wife. Like cold water to a weary soul
imn: corc gauh longx caux zanc-zanc nzaeng jaax nyei m'sieqv dorn juangc biauv yiem. Maaih longx nyei fienx yiem go nyei deic-bung daaih,

eng: is good news from a distant land. Like a muddied spring or a polluted well
imn: se hnangv jaang nqaatv nyei ziangh hoc hopv wuom-namx nor. Kuv mienh nyiemc suei bun orqv mienh

eng: are the righteous who give way to the wicked. It is not good to eat too much honey,
imn: se hnangv njoqc nyei wuom-nyuonh fai laih hlopv nyei wuom-zingv. Nyanc mueiz-dorngh camv se maiv yaauc.

eng: nor is it honorable to search out matters that are too deep. Like a city whose walls are broken through
imn: Lorz mienh taaih ganh yaac maiv benx. Mienh maiv haih gunv maiv duqv ganh,

eng: is a person who lacks self-control.
imn: se hnangv mbaang waaic nyei zingh maiv maaih zingh laatc.

eng: Like snow in summer or rain in harvest, honor is not fitting for a fool. Like a fluttering sparrow or a darting swallow,
imn: Ceng mienh hngongx mienh se maiv puix, hnangv nqaai gorng duih sorng, fai siou gaeng-zuangx nyei ziangh hoc duih mbiungc. Zioux mienh yaac maiv maaih gorn-baengx yaac zioux maiv zuqc,

eng: an undeserved curse does not come to rest. A whip for the horse, a bridle for the donkey,
imn: hnangv norqc meix fai norqc mba'hinx ndaix mingh ndaix daaih yaac maiv nzopc nor. Biaav-bin longc mborqv maaz, hanh kouv fapv lorh nyei nzuih baengx,

eng: and a rod for the backs of fools! Do not answer a fool according to his folly,
imn: biaav longc mborqv mienh hngongx mienh nyei diqc daanz. Maiv dungx dau mienh hngongx mienh ziux ninh hngongx nyei waac,

eng: or you yourself will be just like him. Answer a fool according to his folly,
imn: nziex meih ganh ziouc caux ninh fih hnangv. Oix zuqc dau mienh hngongx mienh ziux ninh hngongx nyei waac,

eng: or he will be wise in his own eyes. Sending a message by the hands of a fool
imn: nziex ei ninh nyei m'zing mangc, ninh funx ninh ganh maaih cong-mengh. Haaix dauh bangc mienh hngongx mienh fungx fienx,

eng: is like cutting off one’s feet or drinking poison. Like the useless legs of one who is lame
imn: se hnangv hngaqv ganh nyei zaux ndutv ziouc hoic ganh. Cong-mengh waac yiem mienh hngongx mienh nyei nzuih se maiv lamh longc,

eng: is a proverb in the mouth of a fool. Like tying a stone in a sling
imn: hnangv zaux mbai mienh nyei zaux maiv lamh longc. Ceng mienh hngongx mienh

eng: is the giving of honor to a fool. Like a thornbush in a drunkard’s hand
imn: se hnangv zorqv la'bieiv hietv jienv ndopv-hlaang. Cong-mengh waac yiem mienh hngongx mienh nyei nzuih,

eng: is a proverb in the mouth of a fool. Like an archer who wounds at random
imn: hnangv njimv yiem diuv nquin mienh nyei buoz. Haaix dauh cingv jiex jauv nyei mienh hngongx mienh fai diuv nquin mienh,

eng: is one who hires a fool or any passer-by. As a dog returns to its vomit,
imn: se hnangv buonv juang-zinx nyei mienh la'guaih buonv bun zuangx mienh zuqc mun. Mienh hngongx mienh zoux hngongx nyei sic, zoux liuz aengx zoux,

eng: so fools repeat their folly. Do you see a person wise in their own eyes?
imn: se hnangv juv nzuonx mingh nyanc ninh lov nyei ga'naaiv. Meih buatc mienh funx ninh ganh maaih cong-mengh fai?

eng: There is more hope for a fool than for them. A sluggard says, “There’s a lion in the road,
imn: Mienh hngongx mienh zungv maaih lamh hnamv gauh camv ninh. Mienh lueic mienh gorngv, “Maaih sienh yiem wuov jauv!

eng: a fierce lion roaming the streets!” As a door turns on its hinges,
imn: Maaih dauh sienh yiem wuov jaai-horngc.” Gaengh naapc hnangv haaix nor bun gaengh koi mingh koi daaih,

eng: so a sluggard turns on his bed. A sluggard buries his hand in the dish;
imn: mienh lueic mienh bueix jienv coux hnangv wuov nor mbienv mingh mbienv daaih. Mienh lueic mienh nyei buoz dapv bieqc nzormc,

eng: he is too lazy to bring it back to his mouth. A sluggard is wiser in his own eyes
imn: bouh faaux ganh nyei nzuih ninh zungv nqemh kouv haic. Mienh lueic mienh ziux ganh nyei m'zing mangc ninh maaih cong-mengh,

eng: than seven people who answer discreetly. Like one who grabs a stray dog by the ears
imn: gauh camv siec dauh dau duqv horpc nyei mienh. Mienh nzaeng jaax nyei sic maiv guen zuqc mbuo, mbuo yaac mingh la'nyauv,

eng: is someone who rushes into a quarrel not their own. Like a maniac shooting
imn: se hnangv juv jiex jauv, mienh la'guaih baeng juv nyei m'normh. Haaix dauh nduov ninh nyei juangc laangz mienh

eng: without a gossip a quarrel dies down. As charcoal to embers and as wood to fire,
imn: Maiv maaih zaangh, douz ziouc daic mi'aqv. Haaix ndau maiv maaih mienh gorngv waac-sapv waac-huv, nzaeng jaax nyei jauv ziouc dingh aqv. Nzaeng jaax nyei mienh nzaeng jaax bun sic cuotv,

eng: so is a quarrelsome person for kindling strife. The words of a gossip are like choice morsels;
imn: hnangv douz-taanx jaa jienv douz caux zaangh dapv jienv zieqc jienv douz nyei douz-nzauc. Gorngv waac-sapv daanh mienh nyei waac,

eng: they go down to the inmost parts. Like a coating of silver dross on earthenware
imn: se hnangv duqv nyanc aax kuv nyei ga'naaiv, naqv njiec taux ga'sie. Nzuih gaam hnyouv ciouv,

eng: are fervent lips with an evil heart. Enemies disguise themselves with their lips,
imn: se hnangv nie-mor nzaatv jienv liuc nor. Mienh nzorng mienh yaac longc nzuih ganh nyungc gorngv.

eng: but in their hearts they harbor deceit. Though their speech is charming, do not believe them,
imn: Yiem hnyouv ninh kungx nduov mienh hnangv. Ninh gorngv kuh muangx nyei waac meih maiv dungx sienx ninh,

eng: for seven abominations fill their hearts. Their malice may be concealed by deception,
imn: weic zuqc yiem ninh nyei hnyouv maaih siec nyungc gamh nziev nyei sic. Maiv gunv ninh longc guv guaix nyei za'eix buang jienv ninh nzaeng nyei jauv,

eng: but their wickedness will be exposed in the assembly. Whoever digs a pit will fall into it;
imn: ninh nyei orqv sic oix yiem mienh camv gapv zunv nyei dorngx cuotv daaih. Haaix dauh wetv kuotv, ganh ziouc ndortv bieqc kuotv gu'nyuoz.

eng: if someone rolls a stone, it will roll back on them. A lying tongue hates those it hurts,
imn: Haaix dauh fongv la'bieiv, la'bieiv aengx oix daaux nqaang ndapv ninh. Mienh nyei nzuih gorngv-baeqc hoic ninh nzorng nyei mienh.

eng: and a flattering mouth works ruin.
imn: Longc nzuih la'guaih ceng mienh yaac baaic waaic mienh.

eng: Do not boast about tomorrow, for you do not know what a day may bring. Let someone else praise you, and not your own mouth;
imn: Maiv dungx bungx waac-maux gorngv njang hnoi nyei sic, weic zuqc meih maiv hiuv duqv yietc hnoi yietc hnoi oix maaih haaix nyungc sic cuotv. Bun ganh dauh ceng meih, maiv zeiz longc meih ganh nyei nzuih.

eng: an outsider, and not your own lips. Stone is heavy and sand a burden,
imn: Bun nyiec nyei mienh ceng meih, maiv zeiz ganh nyei nzuih ceng. La'bieiv se hniev, saa-ziou yaac maaih hniev-daauh,

eng: but a fool’s provocation is heavier than both. Anger is cruel and fury overwhelming,
imn: mv baac mienh hngongx mienh cuoqv mienh zungv gauh hniev naaiv deix i nyungc. Mienh nouz se ciouv, mienh qiex jiez haih baaic waaic,

eng: but who can stand before jealousy? Better is open rebuke
imn: mv baac mienh mueic jieqv, haaix dauh haih ngaengc duqv jienv? Dorng jienv mienh gorngv-hemx,

eng: than hidden love. Wounds from a friend can be trusted,
imn: se gauh longx maiv bun mienh hiuv duqv meih hnamv ninh. Loz-gaeng doic bun meih zuqc mun se ziepc zuoqv nyei zoux,

eng: but an enemy multiplies kisses. One who is full loathes honey from the comb,
imn: mv baac win-wangv zom hmien jiex ndaangc zien nyei jauv. Nyanc beuv nyei mienh ziouc youx mueiz-dorngh,

eng: but to the hungry even what is bitter tastes sweet. Like a bird that flees its nest
imn: mv baac hnyouv sie nyei mienh, liemh im nyei ga'naaiv yaac gaam nyei. Mienh leih nqoi ganh nyei biauv,

eng: is anyone who flees from home. Perfume and incense bring joy to the heart,
imn: se hnangv norm norqc leih nqoi ninh nyei lauz. Youh ndaang caux wuom-ndaang bun hnyouv a'hneiv.

eng: and the pleasantness of a friend springs from their heartfelt advice. Do not forsake your friend or a friend of your family,
imn: Loz-gaeng doic longc hnyouv kuinx bun hnyouv orn-lorqc. Meih maiv dungx guangc meih nyei loz-gaeng doic caux meih nyei die nyei loz-gaeng doic.

eng: and do not go to your relative’s house when disaster strikes you— better a neighbor nearby than a relative far away. Be wise, my son, and bring joy to my heart;
imn: Zuqc zeqc naanc nyei hnoi yaac maiv dungx mingh meih nyei muoz-doic nyei biauv. Yiem fatv nyei juangc laangz mienh se gauh longx yiem go nyei muoz-doic. Yie nyei dorn aac, meih oix zuqc zoux cong-mengh mienh,

eng: then I can answer anyone who treats me with contempt. The prudent see danger and take refuge,
imn: yaac bun yie nyei hnyouv njien-youh. Haaix dauh daanh yie, yie ziouc maaih waac dau. Mienh guai mienh buatc haih maaih sic cuotv ziouc simv nqoi,

eng: but the simple keep going and pay the penalty. Take the garment of one who puts up security for a stranger;
imn: mv baac maiv hiuv haaix nyungc nyei mienh hungx jienv mingh ziouc zuqc kouv aqv. Se gorngv mienh beu ga'nyiec nyei mienh,

eng: hold it in pledge if it is done for an outsider. If anyone loudly blesses their neighbor early in the morning,
imn: oix zuqc zorqv laengz beu nyei mienh nyei lui daaih dorngx. Se gorngv ninh beu ganh fingx mienh, zungv oix zuqc zorqv ninh nyei lui dorngx jienv. Haaix dauh jiez sin nziouv,

eng: it will be taken as a curse. A quarrelsome wife is like the dripping
imn: qiex hlo nyei heuc jienv buang waac bun juangc laangz mienh, daaux nzuonx mienh funx benx zioux nyei waac. Zanc-zanc nzaeng jaax nyei m'sieqv dorn,

eng: of a leaky roof in a rainstorm; restraining her is like restraining the wind
imn: se hnangv duih mbiungc nyei hnoi maaih mbiungc zanc-zanc ndiepv njiec, maiv dingh. Dangv jienv ninh se hnangv dangv jienv nziaaux,

eng: or grasping oil with the hand. As iron sharpens iron,
imn: fai longc mbiaauc jieqv buoz nanv youh. Hnangv hlieqv nzioux hlieqv laic daaih,

eng: so one person sharpens another. The one who guards a fig tree will eat its fruit,
imn: laanh caux laanh yaac hnangv wuov nor. Haaix dauh goux ngongh nyorx ndiangx ziouc duqv nyanc ndiangx nyei biouv.

eng: and whoever protects their master will be honored. As water reflects the face,
imn: Haaix dauh goux ninh nyei ziouv, wuov dauh ziouc duqv mienh taaih. Mangc wuom buatc ganh nyei hmien,

eng: so one’s life reflects the heart. Death and Destruction are never satisfied,
imn: mienh nyei hnyouv fih hnangv nyei bun cing mienh hnangv haaix nor. Yiemh gen caux zuqc mietc nyei mienh yiem nyei dorngx, yietc liuz maiv buangv.

eng: and neither are human eyes. The crucible for silver and the furnace for gold,
imn: Mienh nyei m'zing buatc oix duqv nyei, yietc liuz yaac maiv gaux. Nyaanh korqv se weic sinx nyaanh, louh se weic sinx jiem,

eng: but people are tested by their praise. Though you grind a fool in a mortar,
imn: ceng nyei waac yaac haih seix duqv cuotv mienh. Maiv gunv zorqv mienh hngongx mienh caux zong muonc nyei mbiauh

eng: grinding them like grain with a pestle, you will not remove their folly from them. Be sure you know the condition of your flocks,
imn: dapv jienv da'komv aengx longc da'juih zong, mienh hngongx mienh m'daaih hnangv naaic hngongx aqv. Meih oix zuqc hiuv duqv meih nyei ba'gi yungh guanh hnangv haaix nor,

eng: give careful attention to your herds; for riches do not endure forever,
imn: yaac mangc longx meih nyei ngongh guanh, weic zuqc zinh zoih maiv haih yietc liuz maaih.

eng: and a crown is not secure for all generations. When the hay is removed and new growth appears
imn: Hungh ningv yaac haih yiem taux maanc gouv maanc doic fai? Gaatv miev-nqaai mingh,

eng: and the grass from the hills is gathered in, the lambs will provide you with clothing,
imn: miev-lunx aengx cuotv daaih, mbong zaangc nyei lai-miev yaac siou nzengc, wuov zanc meih ziouc duqv ba'gi yungh dorn nyei biei

eng: and the goats with the price of a field. You will have plenty of goats’ milk to feed your family
imn: weic zoux meih nyei lui-houx. Yungh yaac benx lingh deic nyei jaax-zinh. Maaih yungh nyorx gaux zoux meih nyei nyanc hopv,

eng: and to nourish your female servants.
imn: yaac gaux meih nyei yietc biauv mienh, liemh meih nyei m'sieqv bou.

eng: The wicked flee though no one pursues, but the righteous are as bold as a lion. When a country is rebellious, it has many rulers,
imn: Orqv mienh biaux yaac maiv maaih haaix dauh zunc, mv baac kuv mienh daamv hlo hnangv sienh. Guoqv zaangc mienh baamz zuiz, guoqv maaih zoux hlo gunv nyei mienh camv.

eng: but a ruler with discernment and knowledge maintains order. A ruler who oppresses the poor
imn: Mv baac maaih haih mengh baeqc nyei mienh caux maaih wuonh zaang nyei mienh nor, guoqv zaangc ziouc liepc duqv wuonv lauh. Zoux hlo nyei mienh zeix mienh jomc mienh,

eng: is like a driving rain that leaves no crops. Those who forsake instruction praise the wicked,
imn: hnangv domh mbiungc mborqv gaeng-zuangx waaic. Guangc leiz-latc nyei mienh ceng orqv mienh,

eng: but those who heed it resist them. Evildoers do not understand what is right,
imn: mv baac gan leiz-latc nyei mienh caux orqv mienh doix-dekc. Orqv mienh maiv mengh baeqc baengh fim nyei jauv,

eng: but those who seek the Lord understand it fully. Better the poor whose walk is blameless
imn: mv baac taaih Ziouv wuov deix mengh baeqc nzengc. Mienh jomc mienh hnyouv zingx nyei yiem

eng: than the rich whose ways are perverse. A discerning son heeds instruction,
imn: se gauh longx buv longh buv dangv nyei butv-zoih mienh. Gan leiz-latc nyei mienh benx cong-mengh nyei dorn,

eng: but a companion of gluttons disgraces his father. Whoever increases wealth by taking interest or profit from the poor
imn: mv baac mienh kaux mauv nyanc mauv hopv nyei mienh zoux doic, ziouc bun ninh nyei die zuqc nyaiv. Mienh longc leic zinh hlang weic duqv zinh zoih camv jienv faaux,

eng: amasses it for another, who will be kind to the poor. If anyone turns a deaf ear to my instruction,
imn: ninh siou daaih weic bun haih korv-lienh mienh jomc mienh wuov nyungc mienh. Haaix dauh maiv muangx leiz-latc,

eng: even their prayers are detestable. Whoever leads the upright along an evil path
imn: liemh ninh daux gaux nyei waac benx Tin-Hungh funx youx haic, gamh nziev haic nyei sic. Haaix dauh nduov hnyouv zingx nyei mienh yangh orqv nyei jauv,

eng: will fall into their own trap, but the blameless will receive a good inheritance. The rich are wise in their own eyes;
imn: wuov dauh ziouc ndortv njiec ninh ganh nyei kuotv, mv baac maiv maaih dorngx nqemh nyei mienh duqv nzipc longx nyei buonc. Butv-zoih mienh ziux ninh ganh mangc maaih cong-mengh,

eng: one who is poor and discerning sees how deluded they are. When the righteous triumph, there is great elation;
imn: mv baac haih samx duqv cuotv nyei mienh jomc mienh haih mangc duqv toux. Kuv mienh duqv hingh, mienh zoux jorm, nauc nyitc,

eng: but when the wicked rise to power, people go into hiding. Whoever conceals their sins does not prosper,
imn: mv baac orqv mienh henv faaux, mienh ziouc bingx. Haaix dauh buang jienv ninh ganh nyei zuiz maiv haih duqv longx,

eng: but the one who confesses and renounces them finds mercy. Blessed is the one who always trembles before God,
imn: mv baac haaix dauh nyiemc ganh zoux dorngc nyei zuiz aengx guangc nqoi, ziouc duqv Tin-Hungh korv-lienh. Mienh zanc-zanc taaih Ziouv ziouc duqv orn-lorqc,

eng: but whoever hardens their heart falls into trouble. Like a roaring lion or a charging bear
imn: mv baac zoux hnyouv ngaengc nyei mienh zuqc zeqc naanc hoic. Zoux hlo nyei orqv mienh gunv mienh jomc mienh

eng: is a wicked ruler over a helpless people. A tyrannical ruler practices extortion,
imn: se hnangv sienh njunh nor, fai hnangv jiepv bieqc daaih borngz. Zoux hlo gunv mienh nyei mienh maiv bieqc hnyouv,

eng: but one who hates ill-gotten gain will enjoy a long reign. Anyone tormented by the guilt of murder
imn: ninh ziouc zoux doqc, zeix mienh. Mv baac haaix dauh youx maiv baengh fim nyei leic zinh ziouc bun ninh ziangh nyei hnoi-nyieqc ndaauv. Haaix dauh daix mienh bun nziaamv liouc cuotv ziouc dorngc zuiz,

eng: will seek refuge in the grave; let no one hold them back. The one whose walk is blameless is kept safe,
imn: ninh oix zuqc bingx jienv biaux taux daic, maiv dungx bun haaix dauh tengx ninh. Zoux duqv zingx nyei mienh duqv yiem longx,

eng: but the one whose ways are perverse will fall into the pit. Those who work their land will have abundant food,
imn: mv baac haaix dauh zoux sic ngau njuotv ziouc liemh zeih ndortv njiec kuotv. Haaix dauh laih ganh nyei lingh deic duqv laangh ziqc nyanc beuv,

eng: but those who chase fantasies will have their fill of poverty. A faithful person will be richly blessed,
imn: mv baac haaix dauh gan maiv lamh longc nyei jauv ziouc za'gengh jomc aqv. Ziepc zuoqv nyei mienh duqv fuqv camv,

eng: but one eager to get rich will not go unpunished. To show partiality is not good—
imn: mv baac oix siepv-siepv nyei butv zoih wuov nyungc mienh, ndongc haaix zungv oix zuqc dingc zuiz. Mangc mienh nyei hmien mbenc sic se maiv benx,

eng: yet a person will do wrong for a piece of bread. The stingy are eager to get rich
imn: mv baac weic duqv aax hnaangx nziex mienh haih zoux dorngc. Mienh njapv mienh oix siepv-siepv nyei butv zoih,

eng: and are unaware that poverty awaits them. Whoever rebukes a person will in the end gain favor
imn: yaac maiv hiuv duqv ninh ganh oix zuqc jomc. Haaix dauh gorngv-hemx mienh, nqa'haav duqv mienh a'hneiv

eng: rather than one who has a flattering tongue. Whoever robs their father or mother
imn: gauh camv nzuih la'guaih ceng nyei mienh. Haaix dauh nimc ninh nyei diex maac nyei ga'naaiv

eng: and says, “It’s not wrong,” is partner to one who destroys. The greedy stir up conflict,
imn: yaac gorngv, “Hnangv naaic se maiv dorngc,” ninh se caux zoux mietc nyei mienh zoux doic. Cei nyei mienh coqv cuotv nzaeng jaax nyei sic,

eng: but those who trust in the Lord will prosper. Those who trust in themselves are fools,
imn: mv baac haaix dauh kaux Ziouv ziouc duqv longx. Haaix dauh kaux ninh ganh nyei hnyouv se hngongx,

eng: but those who walk in wisdom are kept safe. Those who give to the poor will lack nothing,
imn: mv baac haaix dauh gan cong-mengh nyei jauv ziouc duqv njoux. Haaix dauh nzie mienh jomc mienh,

eng: but those who close their eyes to them receive many curses. When the wicked rise to power, people go into hiding;
imn: wuov dauh ziouc maiv caa haaix nyungc, mv baac haaix dauh buang jienv m'zing hnangv maiv buatc nor, ziouc zuqc mienh camv nyei zioux. Orqv mienh henv faaux, mienh ziouc bingx,

eng: but when the wicked perish, the righteous thrive.
imn: mv baac orqv mienh zuqc mietc, kuv mienh hiaangx camv faaux.

eng: Whoever remains stiff-necked after many rebukes will suddenly be destroyed—without remedy. When the righteous thrive, the people rejoice;
imn: Haaix dauh zanc-zanc zuqc gorngv-hemx corc hnyouv ngaengc, wuov dauh ziouc liemh zeih zuqc mietc, maiv maaih za'eix goiv duqv nzuonx. Kuv mienh maaih hatc maaz camv jienv faaux, baeqc fingx njien-youh,

eng: when the wicked rule, the people groan. A man who loves wisdom brings joy to his father,
imn: mv baac orqv mienh zoux hlo gunv mienh, baeqc fingx njunh jienv yiem. Haaix dauh hnamv cong-mengh, wuov dauh bun ninh nyei die a'hneiv,

eng: but a companion of prostitutes squanders his wealth. By justice a king gives a country stability,
imn: mv baac haaix dauh caux maaic sin nyei m'sieqv dorn jiu tong, wuov dauh zaaux nzengc ninh maaih nyei yietc zungv ga'naaiv. Hungh diex longc baengh fim nyei jauv,

eng: but those who are greedy for bribes tear it down. Those who flatter their neighbors
imn: guoqv ziouc liepc duqv wuonv, mv baac hungh diex aapv baeqc fingx cuotv nyaanh hmuangx ziouc bun guoqv zuqc baaic. Mienh la'guaih ceng ninh nyei juangc laangz mienh

eng: are spreading nets for their feet. Evildoers are snared by their own sin,
imn: se hnangv zaeng mungz nzenc ninh ganh nyei zaux. Orqv mienh baamz zuiz se hnangv zuqc ganh nyei zuiz zoux koux hlopv jienv,

eng: but the righteous shout for joy and are glad. The righteous care about justice for the poor,
imn: mv baac kuv mienh njien-youh, baaux nzung. Kuv mienh hiuv duqv mienh jomc mienh horpc zuqc duqv nyei leiz,

eng: but the wicked have no such concern. Mockers stir up a city,
imn: mv baac orqv mienh maiv bieqc hnyouv naaiv nyungc jauv. Zoux huotv mienh nyei mienh coqv bun zingh nyei mienh lunc nzengc,

eng: but the wise turn away anger. If a wise person goes to court with a fool,
imn: mv baac cong-mengh mienh haih donv duqv qiex jiez nyei jauv. Se gorngv cong-mengh mienh caux mienh hngongx mienh nzaeng,

eng: the fool rages and scoffs, and there is no peace. The bloodthirsty hate a person of integrity
imn: mienh hngongx mienh youc qiex jiez youc jatv, zungv maiv haih sekv. Oix daix mienh nyei mienh hanc haic maiv maaih dorngx nqemh nyei mienh.

eng: and seek to kill the upright. Fools give full vent to their rage,
imn: Orqv mienh lorz hnyouv zingx nyei mienh nyei maengc. Mienh hngongx mienh bun ninh nyei ga'qiex baetv cuotv nzengc,

eng: but the wise bring calm in the end. If a ruler listens to lies,
imn: mv baac cong-mengh mienh longc noic nyienz jienv. Se gorngv zoux hlo nyei mienh muangx gorngv-baeqc nyei waac,

eng: all his officials become wicked. The poor and the oppressor have this in common:
imn: ninh nyei yietc zungv jien ziouc waaic nzengc. Mienh jomc mienh caux zeix mienh nyei mienh maaih yietc diuc fih hnangv,

eng: The Lord gives sight to the eyes of both. If a king judges the poor with fairness,
imn: se Ziouv bun ninh mbuo i dauh nyei m'zing njang. Se gorngv hungh diex baengh fim nyei siemv mienh jomc mienh,

eng: his throne will be established forever. A rod and a reprimand impart wisdom,
imn: ninh nyei hungh weic yietc liuz liepc wuonv. Longc biaav mborqv njaaux yaac gorngv-njaaux, fu'jueiv ziouc jaa cong-mengh,

eng: but a child left undisciplined disgraces its mother. When the wicked thrive, so does sin,
imn: mv baac se gorngv sueih fu'jueiv ei ganh nyei hnyouv zoux, ziouc bun maa zuqc nyaiv. Orqv mienh duqv lingc gunv, zuiz nyei jauv jaa camv,

eng: but the righteous will see their downfall. Discipline your children, and they will give you peace;
imn: mv baac kuv mienh duqv buatc ninh mbuo zuqc baaic waaic nzengc. Oix zuqc mborqv njaaux meih nyei dorn,

eng: they will bring you the delights you desire. Where there is no revelation, people cast off restraint;
imn: ninh ziouc bun meih duqv hitv kuonx, ninh yaac oix bun meih nyei hnyouv a'hneiv. Haaix norm dorngx maiv maaih laauc yaangh nyei jauv,

eng: but blessed is the one who heeds wisdom’s instruction. Servants cannot be corrected by mere words;
imn: baeqc fingx ziouc maiv muangx leiz gunv, mv baac gan longx leiz-latc nyei mienh duqv orn-lorqc. Kungx longc waac gorngv-njaaux bou hnangv se maiv gaux.

eng: though they understand, they will not respond. Do you see someone who speaks in haste?
imn: Maiv gunv ninh mengh baeqc ninh maiv ei jienv zoux. Meih buatc nzuih siepv nyei mienh fai?

eng: There is more hope for a fool than for them. A servant pampered from youth
imn: Mienh hngongx mienh zungv maaih lamh hnamv gauh camv ninh. Bou faix nyei ziangh hoc meih ei ninh nyei hnyouv bun ga'naaiv ninh,

eng: will turn out to be insolent. An angry person stirs up conflict,
imn: setv mueiz ninh zorqv nzengc meih maaih nyei yietc zungv ga'naaiv. Ga'qiex beqv nyei mienh coqv cuotv nzaeng jaax nyei sic.

eng: and a hot-tempered person commits many sins. Pride brings a person low,
imn: Hungh hec qiex jiez nyei mienh baamz zuiz camv. Mienh zoux maux ninh ziouc ndortv njiec aiv,

eng: but the lowly in spirit gain honor. The accomplices of thieves are their own enemies;
imn: mv baac mienh nyiemc ganh zoux faix ziouc duqv mienh taaih. Haaix dauh caux janx-zaqc maaih buonc ziouc hanc ganh nyei maengc.

eng: they are put under oath and dare not testify. Fear of man will prove to be a snare,
imn: Ninh haiz mienh houv nyei waac yaac maiv gorngv haaix nyungc. Mienh gamh nziex mienh se benx koux hlopv,

eng: but whoever trusts in the Lord is kept safe. Many seek an audience with a ruler,
imn: mv baac haaix dauh kaux Ziouv duqv yiem longx. Mienh camv nyei lorz zoux hlo nyei mienh korv-lienh,

eng: but it is from the Lord that one gets justice. The righteous detest the dishonest;
imn: mv baac se Ziouv bun mienh duqv baengh fim. Hnyouv maiv zingx nyei mienh zuqc kuv mienh youx,

eng: the wicked detest the upright.
imn: hnyouv zingx nyei mienh zuqc orqv mienh youx.

eng: The sayings of Agur son of Jakeh—an inspired utterance. This man’s utterance to Ithiel: “I am weary, God, but I can prevail. Surely I am only a brute, not a man;
imn: Naaiv se Yaa^ke nyei dorn, Aagu, douc nyei waac. Naaiv laanh mienh gorngv mbuox I^ti^en, se mbuox I^ti^en caux Ukaan, “Beiv zuangx mienh yie gauh hngongx, zungv maiv funx benx baamh mienh.

eng: I do not have human understanding. I have not learned wisdom,
imn: Yie yaac maiv bieqc hnyouv hnangv baamh mienh bieqc hnyouv. Yie maiv hoqc jiex cong-mengh nyei jauv.

eng: nor have I attained to the knowledge of the Holy One. Who has gone up to heaven and come down?
imn: Yie yaac maiv maaih wuonh zaang hiuv duqv Cing-Nzengc Wuov Dauh. Haaix dauh faaux lungh aengx njiec daaih?

eng: he is a shield to those who take refuge in him. Do not add to his words,
imn: Ninh zoux torngv-baaih beu ndaauh kaux ninh nyei mienh. Maiv dungx jaa tipv haaix nyungc bieqc ninh nyei waac.

eng: or he will rebuke you and prove you a liar. “Two things I ask of you, Lord ;
imn: Nziex ninh oix gorngv-hemx meih yaac buatc meih benx gorngv-baeqc nyei mienh. “Tin-Hungh aac, yie oix tov i nyungc meih.

eng: do not refuse me before I die: Keep falsehood and lies far from me;
imn: Yie maiv gaengh daic, naaiv deix i nyungc tov maiv dungx ngaengc, maiv bun yie. Tov bun gorngv-baeqc caux jaav nyei waac leih go yie.

eng: give me neither poverty nor riches, but give me only my daily bread. Otherwise, I may have too much and disown you
imn: Tov maiv dungx bun yie ba'laqc jomc fai butv zoih. Tov bun yie gaux nyanc gaux hopv hnangv. Nziex yie duqv nyanc beuv yaac maiv nyiemc Ziouv ziouc gorngv,

eng: and say, ‘Who is the Lord ?’ Or I may become poor and steal, and so dishonor the name of my God. “Do not slander a servant to their master,
imn: ‘ Ziouv se haaix dauh?’ Fai nziex yie jomc daaih ziouc nimc ga'naaiv, bun yie nyei Tin-Hungh nyei mengh maaih uix. “Maiv dungx daanh bou bun ninh nyei ziouv muangx,

eng: or they will curse you, and you will pay for it. “There are those who curse their fathers
imn: nziex ninh zioux meih, funx meih dorngc zuiz aqv. “Maaih deix mienh zioux ninh mbuo nyei die

eng: and do not bless their mothers; those who are pure in their own eyes
imn: yaac maiv buang waac bun maa. Maaih deix mienh ziux ganh nyei m'zing mangc, funx ganh cing-nzengc,

eng: and yet are not cleansed of their filth; those whose eyes are ever so haughty,
imn: mv baac ganh laih hlopv yaac maiv nzaaux nzengc. Maaih deix mienh zoux maux, m'zing hlang,

eng: whose glances are so disdainful; those whose teeth are swords
imn: ninh mbuo m'zing-jaax za'gengh hlang haic. Maaih deix mienh maaih nyaah hnangv nzuqc ndaauv,

eng: and whose jaws are set with knives to devour the poor from the earth and the needy from among mankind. “The leech has two daughters.
imn: maaih nyaah hnangv nzuqc, ziouc naqv nzengc ndau-beih nyei mienh jomc mienh, se yiem zuangx mienh mbu'ndongx nyei dangx-donx mienh. “Wuom-biom maaih i dauh sieqv,

eng: ‘Give! Give!’ they cry. “There are three things that are never satisfied, four that never say, ‘Enough!’: the grave, the barren womb,
imn: ninh mbuo zanc-zanc heuc jienv gorngv, ‘Bun aqv! Bun aqv!’ “Yietc liuz maiv haih gaux maaih buo nyungc, yietc liuz maiv gorngv, ‘Gaux aqv,’ maaih biei nyungc. Se yiemh gen caux maiv haih maaih saeng-yungz nyei m'sieqv dorn,

eng: land, which is never satisfied with water, and fire, which never says, ‘Enough!’ “The eye that mocks a father,
imn: caux ndau-beih nqaai, maiv duqv wuom gaux, caux douz yietc liuz maiv gorngv, ‘Gaux aqv.’ “Mienh huotv ninh nyei die,

eng: that scorns an aged mother, will be pecked out by the ravens of the valley, will be eaten by the vultures. “There are three things that are too amazing for me,
imn: mangc piex, maiv muangx ninh nyei maa nyei waac, ziouc zuqc horngz nyei norqc aa nyorqv ninh nyei m'zing cuotv, yaac zuqc domh jaangv-ngongh nyanc ninh nyei sei. “Yie mbuoqc horngh jiex ndaangc nyei maaih buo nyungc,

eng: four that I do not understand: the way of an eagle in the sky,
imn: yie maiv bieqc hnyouv nyei maaih biei nyungc, se domh jaangv hnangv haaix nor ndaix wuov lungh,

eng: the way of a snake on a rock, the way of a ship on the high seas, and the way of a man with a young woman. “This is the way of an adulterous woman:
imn: naang hnangv haaix nor luih la'bieiv gu'nguaaic, nzangv hnangv haaix nor yangh domh koiv gu'nyuoz, dorn hnangv haaix nor caux sieqv jiu tong. “Hienx nqox nyei m'sieqv dorn hnangv naaiv nor.

eng: She eats and wipes her mouth and says, ‘I’ve done nothing wrong.’ “Under three things the earth trembles,
imn: Ninh nyanc liuz, sortv nzuih, ziouc gorngv, ‘Yie maiv zoux dorngc haaix nyungc.’ “Bun ndau dongz maaih buo nyungc,

eng: under four it cannot bear up: a servant who becomes king,
imn: ndau-beih ndaam maiv duqv nyei maaih biei nyungc. Maaih nouh gauv duqv zoux hungh,

eng: a godless fool who gets plenty to eat, a contemptible woman who gets married,
imn: maaih mienh hngongx mienh duqv nyanc beuv, maaih maiv maaih mienh hnamv nyei sieqv duqv nqox longc,

eng: and a servant who displaces her mistress. “Four things on earth are small,
imn: yaac maaih nouh beiz duqv zoux ziouv div ninh nyei biauv-ziouv maac. “Ndau-beih maaih biei nyungc ga'naaiv faix! nyei,

eng: yet they are extremely wise: Ants are creatures of little strength,
imn: mv baac za'gengh! cong-mengh haic. Nziouv qaqv faix,

eng: yet they store up their food in the summer; hyraxes are creatures of little power,
imn: mv baac nqaai gorng ninh mbuo liuc leiz nyanc hopv. Yiem la'bieiv nyei domh naauz yaac maiv henv,

eng: yet they make their home in the crags; locusts have no king,
imn: mv baac ninh mbuo yiem la'bieiv-qangx zoux zunh. Jopv-nyeic maiv maaih hungh,

eng: yet they advance together in ranks; a lizard can be caught with the hand,
imn: mv baac ninh mbuo haih mbaih jienv ziangh guanh nyei mingh. Naang-daqc dingx, mienh longc buoz zorqv duqv nyei,

eng: yet it is found in kings’ palaces. “There are three things that are stately in their stride,
imn: mv baac ninh haih yiem hungh dinc. “Laamx zaux nzueic maaih buo nyungc,

eng: four that move with stately bearing: a lion, mighty among beasts,
imn: yangh jauv zorqv laangc maaih biei nyungc. Maaih sienh, beiv zuangx hieh zoih ninh henv jiex,

eng: who retreats before nothing; a strutting rooster, a he-goat,
imn: maiv weic haaix nyungc huin nqaang nzuonx. Jai-gorngx yangh jauv zorqv laangc,

eng: and a king secure against revolt. “If you play the fool and exalt yourself,
imn: yaac maaih yungh gouv, aengx maaih hungh diex maaih ninh nyei baeng weih gormx. “Se gorngv meih zoux sic hngongx, ceng ganh zoux hlo,

eng: or if you plan evil, clap your hand over your mouth! For as churning cream produces butter,
imn: fai daav orqv nyei za'eix, oix zuqc longc buoz buang jienv nzuih. Weic zuqc mienh qouv ngongh nyorx ziouc duqv nyorx-hmei,

eng: and as twisting the nose produces blood, so stirring up anger produces strife.”
imn: mborqv mba'zorng ziouc cuotv nziaamv, cuoqv mienh qiex jiez ziouc maaih nzaeng jaax nyei sic.”

eng: The sayings of King Lemuel—an inspired utterance his mother taught him. Listen, my son! Listen, son of my womb!
imn: Naaiv se Le^mu^en Hungh douc nyei waac, se ninh nyei maa njaaux ninh wuov deix waac. “O yie nyei dorn aah! O yie yungz nyei dorn aah!

eng: Listen, my son, the answer to my prayers! Do not spend your strength on women,
imn: Yie laengz ngaengc waac duqv daaih nyei dorn aah! Yie oix zuqc njaaux haaix nyungc meih? Maiv dungx zorqv meih nyei qaqv bun nzengc m'sieqv dorn,

eng: your vigor on those who ruin kings. It is not for kings, Lemuel—
imn: maiv dungx bun naaiv nyungc jauv hoic hungh diex zuqc baaic. “O Le^mu^en aah! Hungh diex maiv horpc zuqc,

eng: it is not for kings to drink wine, not for rulers to crave beer, lest they drink and forget what has been decreed,
imn: hungh diex maiv horpc zuqc hopv a'ngunc diuv. Zoux hlo gunv wuov dauh maiv horpc zuqc maaih hnyouv oix hopv diuv-kuv. Nziex hopv liuz ziouc la'kuqv leiz-latc,

eng: and deprive all the oppressed of their rights. Let beer be for those who are perishing,
imn: yaac bun zuqc kouv nyei yietc zungv mienh horpc zuqc duqv nyei leiz pien mingh. Bun diuv-kuv aav lamh daic nyei mienh hopv,

eng: wine for those who are in anguish! Let them drink and forget their poverty
imn: bun a'ngunc diuv hnyouv nzauh kouv nyei mienh. Bun ninh mbuo hopv ziouc la'kuqv jomc nyei jauv,

eng: and remember their misery no more. Speak up for those who cannot speak for themselves,
imn: maiv aengx jangx ninh mbuo nyei kouv naanc. “Oix zuqc tengx hngongx, maiv haih gorngv waac nyei mienh gorngv,

eng: for the rights of all who are destitute. Speak up and judge fairly;
imn: yaac beu jienv yietc zungv doh naanc nyei mienh horpc zuqc duqv nyei leiz. Oix zuqc tengx gorngv yaac baengh fim nyei dunx sic.

eng: defend the rights of the poor and needy. A wife of noble character who can find?
imn: Oix zuqc beu jienv jomc nyei mienh caux dangx-donx mienh horpc zuqc duqv nyei leiz.” Haaix dauh haih lorz duqv ziangh horngh nyei auv?

eng: She is worth far more than rubies. Her husband has full confidence in her
imn: Ziangh horngh nyei auv gauh jaaix jiex la'bieiv-jaaix. Ninh nyei nqox kaux duqv zuqc ninh,

eng: and lacks nothing of value. She brings him good, not harm,
imn: yaac maiv ndortv buonv aqv. Ninh yietc seix zoux longx bun ninh nyei nqox,

eng: all the days of her life. She selects wool and flax
imn: yietc liuz maiv zoux haaix nyungc hoic. Ninh lorz ba'gi yungh biei caux la'maah,

eng: and works with eager hands. She is like the merchant ships,
imn: a'hneiv longc buoz zoux gong. Ninh hnangv zoux saeng-eix nyei nzangv nor,

eng: bringing her food from afar. She gets up while it is still night;
imn: wuonc ninh nyei nyanc hopv yiem go nyei daaih. Lungh maiv gaengh njang ninh jiez sin daaih

eng: she provides food for her family and portions for her female servants. She considers a field and buys it;
imn: mbenc nyanc hopv bun hmuangv doic, yaac paaiv gong bun ninh nyei m'sieqv bou. Ninh samx mangc lingh deic yaac maaiz daaih.

eng: out of her earnings she plants a vineyard. She sets about her work vigorously;
imn: Ninh longc ganh nyei buoz zornc daaih nyei nyaanh zoux huingx zuangx a'ngunc biouv. Ninh cuotv qaqv zoux gong jienh,

eng: her arms are strong for her tasks. She sees that her trading is profitable,
imn: yaac bun ninh nyei buoz henv. Ninh hiuv duqv ninh zoux daaih nyei huox maaih jaax-zinh,

eng: and her lamp does not go out at night. In her hand she holds the distaff
imn: ninh lungh muonz buov dang zoux gong lauh nyei. Ninh weic ganh baeng suix

eng: and grasps the spindle with her fingers. She opens her arms to the poor
imn: yaac weic ganh ndatv ndie. Ninh cuotv fim nzie mienh jomc mienh,

eng: and extends her hands to the needy. When it snows, she has no fear for her household;
imn: yaac sung buoz tengx dangx-donx mienh. Duih sorng nyei ziangh hoc ninh maiv nzauh ninh nyei biauv zong mienh,

eng: for all of them are clothed in scarlet. She makes coverings for her bed;
imn: weic zuqc ninh nyei ziangh biauv mienh maaih lui-siouv zuqv. Ninh zoux dimc coux nyei ndie,

eng: she is clothed in fine linen and purple. Her husband is respected at the city gate,
imn: yaac longc la'maah ndie-muonc caux setv luoqc nyei ndie zoux ganh nyei lui-houx. Ninh nyei nqox caux buonv-deic mienh gox zueiz zingh gaengh nyei ziangh hoc,

eng: where he takes his seat among the elders of the land. She makes linen garments and sells them,
imn: zuangx mienh hiuv duqv ninh nyei nqox. Ninh longc la'maah ndie-muonc zoux lui-houx maaic,

eng: and supplies the merchants with sashes. She is clothed with strength and dignity;
imn: yaac fungx la'sin maaic bun saeng-eix mienh. Ninh maaih qaqv yaac duqv mienh taaih,

eng: she can laugh at the days to come. She speaks with wisdom,
imn: ninh yaac jatv hoz nqaang nyei hnoi-nyieqc. Ninh nyei nzuih gorngv cong-mengh waac.

eng: and faithful instruction is on her tongue. She watches over the affairs of her household
imn: Ninh yaac longc suonc nyei hnyouv njaaux. Ninh zaangv longx ninh nyei jaa-dingh,

eng: and does not eat the bread of idleness. Her children arise and call her blessed;
imn: maiv qopv jienv buoz yiem. Ninh nyei fu'jueiv jiez daaih gorngv ninh maaih fuqv,

eng: her husband also, and he praises her: “Many women do noble things,
imn: ninh nyei nqox yaac ceng ninh gorngv, “Maaih ziangh horngh nyei m'sieqv dorn camv nyei,

eng: but you surpass them all.” Charm is deceptive, and beauty is fleeting;
imn: mv baac meih gauh ziangh horngh jiex.” Mienh maaih yuoqc fei mv baac haih jaav nyei.

eng: but a woman who fears the Lord is to be praised. Honor her for all that her hands have done,
imn: Nzueic nyei jauv yaac haih jiex. Mv baac taaih Ziouv nyei m'sieqv dorn puix duqv ceng. Tov bun ninh duqv zingh nyeic ziux ninh nyei buoz zoux nyei gong,

eng: and let her works bring her praise at the city gate.
imn: yaac bun ninh zoux nyei gong yiem wuov zingh gaengh duqv mienh ceng.

eng: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth. Now the earth was formless and empty, darkness was over the surface of the deep, and the Spirit of God was hovering over the waters.
imn: Dongh cor Tin-Hungh zeix lungh zeix ndau. Baamh gen maiv maaih haaix nyungc mou zeiv, yaac maiv maaih yietc nyungc ga'naaiv yiem. Wuom gu'nguaaic mapv hmuangx nyei. Tin-Hungh nyei Singx Lingh yiem wuom gu'nguaaic youh mingh youh daaih.

eng: So God created mankind in his own image,
imn: Tin-Hungh ziouc ziux ninh ganh nyei fangx zeiv zeix cuotv baamh mienh,

eng: God saw all that he had made, and it was very good. And there was evening, and there was morning—the sixth day.
imn: Tin-Hungh mangc ninh zeix daaih nyei nyungc-nyungc ga'naaiv, buatc za'gengh longx haic. Maaih lungh hmuangx yaac maaih lungh ndorm. Naaiv se da'luoqc hnoi.

eng: This is the account of the heavens and the earth when they were created, when the Lord God made the earth and the heavens.
imn: Zeix lungh zeix ndau nyei jauv-louc se hnangv naaiv.

eng: The Lord God said, “It is not good for the man to be alone. I will make a helper suitable for him.”
imn: Ziouv Tin-Hungh aengx gorngv, “Nduqc laanh mienh ganh yiem hnangv se maiv horpc. Yie aengx oix zeix dauh daaih puix ninh, caux ninh zoux doic tengx ninh.”

eng: “You will not certainly die,” the serpent said to the woman. “For God knows that when you eat from it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like God, knowing good and evil.”
imn: Naang gorngv mbuox m'sieqv dorn, “Meih mbuo maiv daic lorqc. Tin-Hungh hnangv naaiv gorngv weic zuqc ninh hiuv duqv meih mbuo nyanc liuz, meih mbuo haih hnamv duqv tong, meih mbuo ziouc hnangv Tin-Hungh nor, haih hiuv duqv longx hiuv duqv ciouv.”

eng: He answered, “I heard you in the garden, and I was afraid because I was naked; so I hid.”
imn: Wuov dauh m'jangc dorn dau, “Yie haiz meih yiem naaiv huingx. Yie gamh nziex haic weic zuqc yie laengh gaengv nyei yiem ziouc bingx meih.”

eng: And he said, “Who told you that you were naked? Have you eaten from the tree that I commanded you not to eat from?”
imn: Tin-Hungh naaic gaax, “Haaix dauh mbuox meih, meih nyei sin laengh gaengv nyei? Meih nyanc dongh yie hatc meih maiv dungx nyanc wuov zungh biouv mi'aqv saah?”

eng: The man said, “The woman you put here with me—she gave me some fruit from the tree, and I ate it.”
imn: Wuov dauh m'jangc dorn dau, “Dongh meih bun daaih caux yie yiem naaiv wuov dauh m'sieqv dorn, ninh bun naaiv zungh ndiangx nyei biouv yie, yie ziouc nyanc mi'aqv.”

eng: Then the Lord God said to the woman, “What is this you have done?”
imn: Ziouv Tin-Hungh naaic gaax wuov dauh m'sieqv dorn, “Meih zoux cuotv haaix nyungc sic mi'aqv?”

eng: The woman said, “The serpent deceived me, and I ate.” So the Lord God said to the serpent, “Because you have done this,
imn: Wuov dauh m'sieqv dorn dau, “Naang ganh nduov yie, yie ziouc nyanc.” Ziouv Tin-Hungh mbuox wuov diuh naang, “Laaix meih hnangv naaiv nor zoux,

eng: “Cursed are you above all livestock and all wild animals! You will crawl on your belly and you will eat dust all the days of your life. And I will put enmity
imn: “Meih ziouc zuqc houv gauh hniev yietc zungv saeng-kuv, gauh camv yietc zungv hieh zoih. Meih oix zuqc longc ga'sie luih jienv gan ndau mingh, yietc seix oix zuqc nyanc nie-mbung. Yie oix bun meih caux naaiv dauh m'sieqv dorn daav win,

eng: between you and the woman, and between your offspring and hers; he will crush your head, and you will strike his heel.” To the woman he said,
imn: yaac bun meih nyei zeiv-fun caux ninh nyei zeiv-fun zoux win-wangv. Ninh nyei zeiv-fun oix mborqv meih nyei m'nqorngv huv, meih yaac oix nyorqv ninh nyei zaux-nqo.” Tin-Hungh yaac gorngv mbuox wuov dauh m'sieqv dorn,

eng: “Cursed is the ground because of you; through painful toil you will eat food from it all the days of your life. It will produce thorns and thistles for you,
imn: Ndau ziouc laaix meih zoux nyei sic zuqc houv. Meih yietc seix oix zuqc bangc ndau zoux gong kouv cingx duqv nyanc. Ndau oix weic meih cuotv miev cuotv njimv.

eng: and you will eat the plants of the field. By the sweat of your brow
imn: Meih yaac oix zuqc nyanc yiem ndau cuotv nyei lai-miev. Meih oix zuqc zoux gong kouv cuotv ziex sin hanc

eng: Adam made love to his wife Eve, and she became pregnant and gave birth to Cain. She said, “With the help of the Lord I have brought forth a man.” Later she gave birth to his brother Abel.
imn: Aa^ndam caux ninh nyei auv, Haawaa, nitv liuz doic, ninh nyei auv ziouc maaih jienv sin aqv. Yungz duqv dauh dorn daaih. Ninh ziouc gorngv, “ Ziouv tengx yie duqv dauh dorn daaih aqv.” Ninh ziouc cuotv mbuox heuc Kaa^in. Nqa'haav ninh aengx yungz Kaa^in nyei youz, Aa^mbaen.

eng: Now Cain said to his brother Abel, “Let’s go out to the field.” While they were in the field, Cain attacked his brother Abel and killed him.
imn: Kaa^in gorngv mbuox ninh nyei youz, Aa^mbaen, “Mbuo mingh wuov jiex ndeic orqc?” Mingh taux ndeic, Kaa^in ziouc caa jienv ninh nyei youz daix guangc mi'aqv.

eng: Then the Lord said to Cain, “Where is your brother Abel?”
imn: Ziouv naaic gaax Kaa^in, “Meih nyei youz, Aa^mbaen, jiex haaix mi'aqv?”

eng: Lamech said to his wives,
imn: Laamekv mbuox ninh nyei auv,

eng: “Adah and Zillah, listen to me; wives of Lamech, hear my words. I have killed a man for wounding me, a young man for injuring me. If Cain is avenged seven times,
imn: “Aa^ndaa caux Sinx^laa muangx yie gorngv maah! Laamekv nyei m'sieqv dorn aah! Muangx longx yie nyei waac. Yie daix dauh mienh weic zuqc ninh hoic yie siang haic. Daix dauh mienh lunx mienh weic ninh mborqv yie mun. Se gorngv haaix dauh daix Kaa^in, oix zuqc daix siec dauh div nor.

eng: The Nephilim were on the earth in those days—and also afterward—when the sons of God went to the daughters of humans and had children by them. They were the heroes of old, men of renown.
imn: Wuov zanc maaih Nefin Mienh yiem lungh ndiev, nqa'haav corc maaih nyei. Se doz-mienh dorn longc baamh mienh sieqv bun njiec daaih nyei fun-faqv. Naaiv se loz-hnoi maaih banh zeic, maaih mengh dauh nyei mienh.

eng: This is the account of Noah and his family.
imn: Yiem naaiv mingh gorngv Noyaa nyei jauv-louc.

eng: Noah was a righteous man, blameless among the people of his time, and he walked faithfully with God. Noah had three sons: Shem, Ham and Japheth.
imn: Noyaa zoux kuv mienh. Wuov jaax lungh ndiev kungx ninh ganh maiv maaih dorngx daanh hnangv. Ninh haaix zanc yaac gan longx Tin-Hungh. Noyaa maaih buo dauh dorn mbuox heuc Sem, Haam caux Yaafetv.

eng: Noah did everything just as God commanded him.
imn: Tin-Hungh hnangv haaix nor paaiv Noyaa, Noyaa yaac ei jienv zoux nzengc.

eng: And Noah did all that the Lord commanded him.
imn: Noyaa ziouc ei jienv Ziouv paaiv nyei waac zoux ziangx nzengc.

eng: The waters flooded the earth for a hundred and fifty days.
imn: Wuom yiemx jienv lungh ndiev yietc baeqv hmz ziepc hnoi.

eng: “As long as the earth endures,
imn: “Da'faanh baamh gen corc yiem jienv nyei ziangh hoc,

eng: seedtime and harvest, cold and heat, summer and winter, day and night will never cease.”
imn: zuangx nyei cun-ciou caux siou nyei cun-ciou, juangv nyei ziangh hoc caux yuoqv nyei ziangh hoc, dong-gen caux cun-gen, lungh hnoi zanc caux lungh muonz zanc, yietc liuz maiv haih nzengc.”

eng: “Whoever sheds human blood,
imn: “Haaix dauh daix mienh, bun nziaamv liouc cuotv,

eng: by humans shall their blood be shed; for in the image of God has God made mankind. As for you, be fruitful and increase in number; multiply on the earth and increase upon it.”
imn: wuov dauh zungv zuqc mienh daix, bun nziaamv liouc cuotv, weic zuqc Tin-Hungh zeix mienh, se zeix daaih zuangv ninh ganh. Meih mbuo yaac oix zuqc maaih fu'jueiv camv, hiaangx daaih maaih fun maaih faqv camv jienv faaux buangv lungh ndiev.”

eng: So God said to Noah, “This is the sign of the covenant I have established between me and all life on the earth.”
imn: Tin-Hungh gorngv mbuox Noyaa, “Naaiv se yie caux lungh ndiev yietc zungv maaih maengc nyei ga'naaiv liepc ngaengc waac nyei jangx-hoc.”

eng: When Noah awoke from his wine and found out what his youngest son had done to him, he said,
imn: Nqa'haav Noyaa nzang daaih, hiuv duqv ninh nyei dorn-faix hnangv haaix nor zoux bun ninh. Ninh ziouc gorngv,

eng: “Cursed be Canaan! The lowest of slaves will he be to his brothers.” He also said,
imn: “Kaa^naa^an zuqc houv aqv! Ninh oix zuqc tengx ninh nyei i dauh gorx zoux gauh aiv jiex nyei nouh gauv.” Ninh yaac gorngv,

eng: “Praise be to the Lord , the God of Shem! May Canaan be the slave of Shem. May God extend Japheth’s territory;
imn: “Oix zuqc ceng Ziouv, Sem nyei Tin-Hungh. Tov bun Kaa^naa^an zoux Sem nyei nouh gauv. Tov Tin-Hungh bun Yaafetv hiaangx jiez daaih,

eng: may Japheth live in the tents of Shem, and may Canaan be the slave of Japheth.” After the flood Noah lived 350 years. Noah lived a total of 950 years, and then he died.
imn: bun ninh duqv yiem Sem nyei ndopv-liuh, yaac bun Kaa^naa^an zoux Yaafetv nyei nouh gauv.” Mbiungc-suiv-imx yiemx liuz lungh ndiev, Noyaa corc ziangh duqv faam-baeqv hmz ziepc hnyangx. Ninh nyei hnyangx-jeiv ziangh duqv juov baeqv hmz ziepc hnyangx ziouc guei seix mi'aqv.

eng: This is the account of Shem, Ham and Japheth, Noah’s sons, who themselves had sons after the flood. The sons of Japheth:
imn: Yiem naaiv mingh gorngv Noyaa nyei dorn, Sem, Haam caux Yaafetv nyei zeiv-fun. Se mbiungc-suiv-imx yiemx liuz lungh ndiev, nqa'haav ninh mbuo buo dauh duqv nyei dorn. Yaafetv nyei dorn se Go^me, Maagokc, Maa^ndai, Yaawaan, Tu^mban, Mesekv, caux Tilaatv.

eng: Gomer, Magog, Madai, Javan, Tubal, Meshek and Tiras. The sons of Gomer:
imn: Go^me nyei dorn se Atc^ke^natv, Lifaatv, caux To^gaan^maa.

eng: Ashkenaz, Riphath and Togarmah. The sons of Javan:
imn: Yaawaan nyei dorn se E^li^se^aa, Taansitv, Kitv^tim caux Lo^ndaa^nim. (Se naaiv deix mienh nyei hoz doic nzaanx mingh yiem koiv-hlen, gorqv-mienh ziux ninh mbuo nyei guoqv, ziux ninh mbuo nyei fingx, ziux ninh mbuo nyei bungh buonc, ziux ninh mbuo nyei waac.)

eng: The sons of Ham:
imn: Haam nyei dorn se Kutv, I^yipv, Putv caux Kaa^naa^an.

eng: Cush, Egypt, Put and Canaan. The sons of Cush:
imn: Kutv nyei dorn se Se^mbaa, Haa^wi^laa, Sapc^taa, Laa^aa^maa caux Sapc^te^kaa.

eng: Egypt was the father of
imn: I^yipv nyei dorn se Lu^ndim, Aa^naa^mim, Le^haa^mbim, Napv^tu^him, Batc^lu^simx, Katv^lu^him, (dongh zoux Fi^li^saa^die Mienh nyei ong-taaix-ngaeqv wuov dauh), caux Kapv^to^lim Mienh.

eng: Canaan was the father of
imn: Kaa^naa^an maaih i dauh dorn. Dorn-hlo heuc Sai^ndorn. Dorn-faix heuc Hetv. Kaa^naa^an yaac zoux camv-fingx mienh nyei ong-taaix-ngaeqv. Maaih Ye^mbutc Mienh, Aa^mo^lai Mienh, Ge^gaa^si Mienh, Hiwai Mienh, Aankaa Mienh, Sini Mienh, Aanwatv Mienh, Seme Mienh caux Haamatv Mienh.

eng: Later the Canaanite clans scattered and the borders of Canaan reached from Sidon toward Gerar as far as Gaza, and then toward Sodom, Gomorrah, Admah and Zeboyim, as far as Lasha.
imn: Nqa'haav Kaa^naa^an nyei camv-fingx mienh nzaanx mingh yiem. Ninh mbuo nyei gapv-jaaix yiem Sai^ndorn yangh naamh bung taux Ge^laa^aa, nitv Gaasaa wuov. Aengx mingh dong bung maengx taux So^ndom , Go^mo^laa, Atc^maa caux Se^mbo^yim taux Laasaa wuov. Naaiv deix yietc zungv se Haam nyei zeiv-fun, ziux ninh mbuo nyei bungh buonc, ziux ninh mbuo nyei waac, ziux ninh mbuo nyei deic caux ninh mbuo nyei guoqv.

eng: Sons were also born to Shem, whose older brother was Japheth; Shem was the ancestor of all the sons of Eber.
imn: Sem se Yaafetv nyei domh gorx, yaac maaih dorn-jueiv camv nyei. Ninh zoux E^mbe nyei zeiv-fun nyei ong-taaix-ngaeqv.

eng: The sons of Shem:
imn: Sem nyei dorn se Elaam, Atc^su, Aan^faa^satv, Lu^nde caux Aalam.

eng: Elam, Ashur, Arphaxad, Lud and Aram. The sons of Aram:
imn: Aalam nyei dorn se Utc, Hun, Ge^te caux Matv.

eng: Uz, Hul, Gether and Meshek. Arphaxad was the father of Shelah,
imn: Aan^faa^satv zoux Qelaa nyei die. Qelaa yaac zoux E^mbe nyei die.

eng: and Shelah the father of Eber. Two sons were born to Eber:
imn: E^mbe maaih i dauh dorn. Maaih dauh, mbuox heuc Belekv, weic zuqc yiem ninh nyei seix zeiv mienh bun nqoi ndau-beih yiem. Ninh nyei youz heuc Yokv^taan.

eng: One was named Peleg, because in his time the earth was divided; his brother was named Joktan. Joktan was the father of
imn: Yokv^taan nyei dorn se An^mo^ndatc, Qelepv, Haa^saan^maa^wetv, Yelaa, Haa^ndo^lam, Usaan, Ndikc^laa, O^mbaan, Aa^mbi^maa^en, Qe^mbaa, Ofi, Haa^wi^laa caux Yo^mbapc. Naaiv deix yietc zungv zoux Yokv^taan nyei dorn.

eng: The region where they lived stretched from Mesha toward Sephar, in the eastern hill country.
imn: Ninh mbuo yiem nyei deic-bung se yiem Mesaa mingh Sefaa wuov bung, dong bung maengx mbong-aiv dorngx.

eng: These are the sons of Shem by their clans and languages, in their territories and nations.
imn: Naaiv deix yietc zungv se Sem nyei zeiv-fun, ziux ninh mbuo nyei bungh buonc, ziux ninh mbuo nyei waac, ziux ninh mbuo nyei deic, caux ninh mbuo nyei guoqv.

eng: These are the clans of Noah’s sons, according to their lines of descent, within their nations. From these the nations spread out over the earth after the flood.
imn: Naaiv deix mienh yiem Noyaa bun njiec nyei zeiv-fun ziux ninh mbuo nyei douh zong, yiem ninh mbuo nyei guoqv. Yiem mbiungc-suiv-imx yiemx liuz lungh ndiev, nqa'haav naaiv deix mienh yietc zungv nzaanx mingh yiem gormx lungh ndiev.

eng: Now the whole world had one language and a common speech. As people moved eastward, they found a plain in Shinar and settled there.
imn: Zinh ndaangc lungh ndiev mienh yietc zungv gorngv nduqc nyungc waac hnangv. Waac-eix fih hnangv nzengc. Ninh mbuo suiv jienv mingh dong bung maengx, taux Sinaa Deic-Bung, maaih ndau-baengh nyei dorngx, ziouc dingh njiec yiem wuov aqv.

eng: This is the account of Shem’s family line.
imn: Yiem naaiv mingh gorngv Sem nyei zeiv-fun.

eng: Two years after the flood, when Shem was 100 years old, he became the father of Arphaxad. And after he became the father of Arphaxad, Shem lived 500 years and had other sons and daughters.
imn: Mbiungc-suiv-imx yiemx liuz lungh ndiev, nqa'haav i hnyangx, Sem duqv yietc baeqv hnyangx wuov hnyangx, ninh duqv dauh dorn, mbuox heuc Aan^faa^satv. Duqv liuz Aan^faa^satv, ninh ziangh jienv hmz baeqv hnyangx, corc maaih dorn maaih sieqv nyei.

eng: When Arphaxad had lived 35 years, he became the father of Shelah. And after he became the father of Shelah, Arphaxad lived 403 years and had other sons and daughters.
imn: Aan^faa^satv duqv faah ziepc hmz hnyangx, ninh duqv dauh dorn, mbuox heuc Qelaa. Duqv liuz Qelaa, Aan^faa^satv ziangh jienv feix baeqv lengh buo hnyangx, corc maaih dorn maaih sieqv nyei.

eng: When Shelah had lived 30 years, he became the father of Eber. And after he became the father of Eber, Shelah lived 403 years and had other sons and daughters.
imn: Qelaa duqv faah ziepc hnyangx, ninh duqv dauh dorn, mbuox heuc E^mbe. Duqv liuz E^mbe, Qelaa ziangh duqv feix baeqv lengh buo hnyangx, corc maaih dorn maaih sieqv nyei.

eng: When Eber had lived 34 years, he became the father of Peleg. And after he became the father of Peleg, Eber lived 430 years and had other sons and daughters.
imn: E^mbe duqv faah ziepc feix hnyangx, ninh duqv dauh dorn, mbuox heuc Belekv. Duqv liuz Belekv, ninh ziangh duqv feix baeqv faah ziepc hnyangx, corc maaih dorn maaih sieqv nyei.

eng: When Peleg had lived 30 years, he became the father of Reu. And after he became the father of Reu, Peleg lived 209 years and had other sons and daughters.
imn: Belekv duqv faah ziepc hnyangx, ninh duqv dauh dorn, mbuox heuc Le^u. Duqv liuz Le^u, ninh ziangh duqv nyic baeqv lengh nduoh hnyangx, corc maaih dorn maaih sieqv nyei.

eng: When Reu had lived 32 years, he became the father of Serug. And after he became the father of Serug, Reu lived 207 years and had other sons and daughters.
imn: Le^u duqv faah ziepc nyeic hnyangx, ninh duqv dauh dorn, mbuox heuc Selukv. Duqv liuz Selukv, ninh ziangh duqv nyic baeqv lengh siec hnyangx, corc maaih dorn maaih sieqv nyei.

eng: When Serug had lived 30 years, he became the father of Nahor. And after he became the father of Nahor, Serug lived 200 years and had other sons and daughters.
imn: Selukv duqv faah ziepc hnyangx, ninh duqv dauh dorn, mbuox heuc Naa^ho. Duqv liuz Naa^ho, ninh ziangh duqv nyic baeqv hnyangx, corc maaih dorn maaih sieqv nyei.

eng: When Nahor had lived 29 years, he became the father of Terah. And after he became the father of Terah, Nahor lived 119 years and had other sons and daughters.
imn: Naa^ho duqv nyic ziepc juov hnyangx, ninh duqv dauh dorn, mbuox heuc Telaa. Duqv liuz Telaa, ninh ziangh duqv yietc baeqv ziepc juov hnyangx, corc maaih dorn maaih sieqv nyei.

eng: After Terah had lived 70 years, he became the father of Abram, Nahor and Haran.
imn: Telaa duqv cietv ziepc hnyangx, ninh duqv buo dauh dorn, mbuox heuc Apc^laam, Naa^ho caux Haalaan.

eng: This is the account of Terah’s family line.
imn: Yiem naaiv mingh gorngv Telaa nyei zeiv-fun.

eng: Terah lived 205 years, and he died in Harran.
imn: Telaa ziangh duqv nyic baeqv lengh biaa hnyangx ziouc yiem Haalaan Zingh guei seix mi'aqv.

eng: The Lord had said to Abram, “Go from your country, your people and your father’s household to the land I will show you. “I will make you into a great nation,
imn: Ziouv gorngv mbuox Apc^laam, “Oix zuqc leih nqoi meih nyei buonv-deic, meih nyei cien-ceqv caux meih nyei die nyei biauv mingh taux yie oix bun meih hiuv nyei deic-bung. Yie oix ceix fuqv bun meih,

eng: and I will bless you; I will make your name great, and you will be a blessing. I will bless those who bless you,
imn: bun meih zoux yietc fingx hlo nyei mienh. Yie oix bun meih maaih mengh hoc. Meih yaac haih bun mienh duqv fuqv. Haaix dauh buang waac meih,

eng: From there he went on toward the hills east of Bethel and pitched his tent, with Bethel on the west and Ai on the east. There he built an altar to the Lord and called on the name of the Lord .
imn: Nqa'haav Apc^laam aengx suiv mingh taux Mbetc^en Zingh nyei dong bung maengx, mbong-aiv nyei dorngx, corng ndopv-liuh. Mbetc^en Zingh yiem ninh mbuo nyei fai bung, Ai Zingh yiem ninh mbuo nyei dong bung. Yiem wuov ninh aengx ceix norm ziec-dorngh daaih gorngv cuotv Ziouv nyei mbuox zaangc Ziouv. Apc^laam aengx cuotv jauv hungx jienv Negepc mingh.

eng: So Abram went up from Egypt to the Negev, with his wife and everything he had, and Lot went with him. Abram had become very wealthy in livestock and in silver and gold.
imn: Apc^laam dorh jienv ninh nyei auv caux ninh maaih nyei nyungc-nyungc ga'naaiv cuotv I^yipv Deic-Bung mingh taux Negepc . Lotv yaac caux jienv ninh mingh. Apc^laam butv zoih haic, maaih saeng-kuv, maaih jiem, maaih nyaanh camv haic.

eng: So Abram went to live near the great trees of Mamre at Hebron, where he pitched his tents. There he built an altar to the Lord .
imn: Apc^laam ziouc suiv ninh nyei ndopv-liuh mingh corng jienv yiem He^mbo^lon Horngc nitv Mamle nyei domh mba'ong zuei ndiangx-lomc. Yiem wuov ninh aengx ceix norm ziec-dorngh zaangc Ziouv.

eng: After Abram returned from defeating Kedorlaomer and the kings allied with him, the king of Sodom came out to meet him in the Valley of Shaveh (that is, the King’s Valley).
imn: Apc^laam mborqv hingh Ke^ndo^laa^o^me Hungh aengx caux ninh dongh hnyouv mborqv jaax wuov deix hungh, ninh ziouc nzuonx. Taux Saawe Horngc, (aengx maaih mbuox heuc “Hungh Horngc”), So^ndom Zingh nyei hungh cuotv daaih zipv.

eng: Then Melchizedek king of Salem brought out bread and wine. He was priest of God Most High, and he blessed Abram, saying,
imn: Aengx maaih Saalem Zingh nyei hungh, Men^ki^se^ndekc, dorh deix njuov caux a'ngunc diuv bun Apc^laam. Men^ki^se^ndekc zoux Yiem Hlang Jiex nyei Tin-Hungh nyei sai mienh. Ninh yaac buang waac bun Apc^laam,

eng: “Blessed be Abram by God Most High, Creator of heaven and earth. And praise be to God Most High,
imn: “Tov Yiem Hlang Jiex nyei Tin-Hungh, Zeix Lungh Zeix Ndau Wuov Dauh, ceix fuqv bun Apc^laam. Ceng Yiem Hlang Jiex nyei Tin-Hungh,

eng: who delivered your enemies into your hand.” Then Abram gave him a tenth of everything. The king of Sodom said to Abram, “Give me the people and keep the goods for yourself.”
imn: weic zuqc ninh bun meih nyei win-wangv zuqc yiem meih nyei buoz-ndiev.” Apc^laam ziouc zorqv ninh duqv nzuonx daaih nyei yietc zungv ga'naaiv, ziepc gouv nyei yietc gouv bun Men^ki^se^ndekc. So^ndom Zingh nyei hungh gorngv mbuox Apc^laam, “Tuix mienh nzuonx bun yie. Ga'naaiv meih ganh longc aqv.”

eng: Abram believed the Lord , and he credited it to him as righteousness.
imn: Apc^laam sienx Ziouv. Weic ninh sienx, Ziouv funx Apc^laa^ham caux ninh horpc hnyouv.

eng: He also said to him, “I am the Lord , who brought you out of Ur of the Chaldeans to give you this land to take possession of it.”
imn: Ziouv aengx gorngv mbuox ninh, “Yie se Ziouv, dongh dorh meih cuotv Ken^ndie Deic-Bung nyei U^aa Zingh weic zorqv naaiv norm deic-bung jiu bun meih nzipc.”

eng: But Abram said, “Sovereign Lord , how can I know that I will gain possession of it?”
imn: Apc^laam aengx naaic, “Ziouv Tin-Hungh aah! Yie hnangv haaix nor haih hiuv duqv dingc yie duqv nzipc naaiv norm deic-bung?”

eng: So the Lord said to him, “Bring me a heifer, a goat and a ram, each three years old, along with a dove and a young pigeon.”
imn: Ziouv dau, “Oix zuqc dorh dauh buo hnyangx nyei yangh ngongh nyeiz, caux dauh buo hnyangx nyei yungh nyeiz, caux dauh buo hnyangx nyei ba'gi yungh gouv, caux norm norqc nqo, caux norm baeqc gopv lunx nyei daaih bun yie.”

eng: “Your slave is in your hands,” Abram said. “Do with her whatever you think best.” Then Sarai mistreated Hagar; so she fled from her.
imn: Apc^laam dau, “Ninh m'daaih zoux meih nyei nouh beiz, yiem meih nyei buoz-ndiev. Meih oix hnangv haaix nor zoux bun ninh, sueih meih zoux aqv maah!” Saalaai zoux doqc Haagaa, Haagaa ziouc biaux mi'aqv.

eng: The angel of the Lord also said to her:
imn: Ziouv nyei fin-mienh aengx gorngv mbuox Haagaa,

eng: “You are now pregnant and you will give birth to a son. You shall name him Ishmael, for the Lord has heard of your misery. He will be a wild donkey of a man;
imn: “Meih ih zanc maaih jienv sin, oix yungz duqv dauh dorn. Meih oix zuqc tengx ninh cuotv mbuox heuc I^saa^maa^en, weic zuqc Ziouv haiz liuz meih nyei kouv naanc aqv. Meih nyei dorn oix hnangv dauh hieh lorh nor.

eng: So Hagar bore Abram a son, and Abram gave the name Ishmael to the son she had borne. Abram was eighty-six years old when Hagar bore him Ishmael.
imn: Nqa'haav Haagaa yungz duqv dauh dorn bun Apc^laam. Apc^laam ziouc tengx ninh nyei dorn cuotv mbuox heuc I^saa^maa^en. Haagaa yungz I^saa^maa^en bun Apc^laam wuov zanc Apc^laam nyei hnyangx-jeiv duqv betv ziepc luoqc hnyangx.

eng: “Very well,” they answered, “do as you say.” So Abraham hurried into the tent to Sarah. “Quick,” he said, “get three seahs of the finest flour and knead it and bake some bread.”
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Longx aqv. Gunv ziux meih nyei waac zoux.” Apc^laa^ham gaanv jienv bieqc ndopv-liuh mbuox Saalaa, “Gaanv siepv deix zorqv yietc tongv mbuonv-muonc caix daaih zoux njuov.”

eng: “Where is your wife Sarah?” they asked him.
imn: Wuov deix mienh naaic Apc^laa^ham, “Meih nyei auv, Saalaa, yiem haaix?”

eng: “There, in the tent,” he said. Then one of them said, “I will surely return to you about this time next year, and Sarah your wife will have a son.”
imn: Apc^laa^ham dau, “Yiem wuov, ndopv-liuh gu'nyuoz.” Wuov deix buo dauh mienh maaih dauh gorngv, “Da'nyeic hnyangx taux naaiv norm ziangh hoc yie zungv aengx nzuonx taux naaiv. Taux wuov zanc meih nyei auv, Saalaa, ziouc duqv dauh dorn daaih aqv.”

eng: Sarah was afraid, so she lied and said, “I did not laugh.”
imn: Saalaa gamh nziex nyei, maiv laengz ninh jatv. Ninh gorngv, “Yie maiv jatv niaa.”

eng: The Lord said, “If I find fifty righteous people in the city of Sodom, I will spare the whole place for their sake.”
imn: Ziouv gorngv, “Se gorngv yie buatc hmz ziepc dauh kuv mienh yie ziouc weic naaiv deix hmz ziepc dauh siex cuotv naaiv norm zingh.”

eng: “If I find forty-five there,” he said, “I will not destroy it.” Once again he spoke to him, “What if only forty are found there?”
imn: Ziouv gorngv, “Se gorngv yiem naaic buatc maaih feix ziepc hmz dauh kuv mienh, yie maiv mietc naaiv norm zingh aqv lorqc.” Apc^laa^ham aengx gorngv nzunc, “Se gorngv yiem wuov kungx maaih feix ziepc dauh ni? Hnangv haaix nor?”

eng: He said, “For the sake of forty, I will not do it.” Then he said, “May the Lord not be angry, but let me speak. What if only thirty can be found there?”
imn: Ziouv dau, “Weic naaiv deix feix ziepc dauh, yie yaac maiv mietc.” Apc^laa^ham gorngv, “Ziouv aah! Maiv dungx gaengh qiex jiez oc. Tov bun yie gorngv. Se gorngv yiem wuov kungx maaih faah ziepc dauh hnangv haaix nor?”

eng: He answered, “I will not do it if I find thirty there.” Abraham said, “Now that I have been so bold as to speak to the Lord, what if only twenty can be found there?”
imn: Ziouv aengx dau, “Se gorngv yiem wuov yie buatc maaih faah ziepc dauh yie yaac maiv mietc.” Apc^laa^ham gorngv, “Yie ndongc naaiv bungx laangc caux Ziouv gorngv. Se gorngv yiem naaic kungx maaih nyic ziepc dauh hnangv ni?”

eng: He said, “For the sake of twenty, I will not destroy it.” Then he said, “May the Lord not be angry, but let me speak just once more. What if only ten can be found there?”
imn: Ziouv gorngv, “Weic naaiv deix nyic ziepc dauh yie yaac maiv mietc wuov norm zingh aqv.” Apc^laa^ham gorngv, “Ziouv aah! Tov maiv dungx qiex jiez. Bun yie aengx gorngv nduqc nzunc hnangv. Se gorngv yiem wuov buatc maaih ziepc dauh hnangv ni?”

eng: He answered, “For the sake of ten, I will not destroy it.” When the Lord had finished speaking with Abraham, he left, and Abraham returned home.
imn: Ziouv dau, “Weic naaiv deix ziepc dauh yie yaac maiv mietc.” Ziouv caux Apc^laa^ham gorngv liuz waac, ziouc mingh mi'aqv. Apc^laa^ham yaac nzuonx mi'aqv.

eng: With the coming of dawn, the angels urged Lot, saying, “Hurry! Take your wife and your two daughters who are here, or you will be swept away when the city is punished.”
imn: Da'nyeic ndorm lungh njang, fin-mienh beqv Lotv. Ninh mbuo gorngv, “Gaanv siepv deix maah! Dorh jienv meih nyei auv aengx caux meih yiem naaiv nyei i dauh sieqv biaux nqoi. Maiv zei aeqv, naaiv norm zingh zuqc dingc zuiz zuqc mietc, meih mbuo yaac caux jienv zuqc daic aqv.”

eng: So when God destroyed the cities of the plain, he remembered Abraham, and he brought Lot out of the catastrophe that overthrew the cities where Lot had lived.
imn: Hnangv naaic, Tin-Hungh mietc wuov norm horngc zaangc nyei zingh, liemh Lotv yiem nyei zingh, wuov zanc, ninh jangx zuqc Apc^laa^ham cingx daaih dorh Lotv biaux cuotv, maiv zuqc daic.

eng: That night they got their father to drink wine, and the older daughter went in and slept with him. He was not aware of it when she lay down or when she got up.
imn: Wuov muonz ninh mbuo i muoz-sieqv bun ninh mbuo nyei die hopv a'ngunc diuv, sieqv-hlo ziouc bieqc mingh caux ninh nyei die bueix. Die zungv maiv hiuv duqv ninh nyei sieqv ziangh haaix bueix, ziangh haaix jiez sin mingh.

eng: But God came to Abimelek in a dream one night and said to him, “You are as good as dead because of the woman you have taken; she is a married woman.”
imn: Maaih muonz Aa^mbi^me^lekv Hungh mbeix buatc Tin-Hungh daaih mbuox ninh, “Meih za'gengh oix zuqc daic aqv, weic zuqc meih dorh daaih naaiv dauh m'sieqv dorn zungv maaih nqox nyei.”

eng: Then Abimelek brought sheep and cattle and male and female slaves and gave them to Abraham, and he returned Sarah his wife to him. And Abimelek said, “My land is before you; live wherever you like.”
imn: Aa^mbi^me^lekv ziouc fungx ngongh, ba'gi yungh, nouh gauv nouh beiz mingh bun Apc^laa^ham, yaac fungx Apc^laa^ham nyei auv, Saalaa, nzuonx bun ninh. Aa^mbi^me^lekv mbuox Apc^laa^ham, “Mangc gaax! Yie nyei deic-bung yiem meih nyei nza'hmien. Meih oix yiem haaix, gunv yiem aqv.”

eng: To Sarah he said, “I am giving your brother a thousand shekels of silver. This is to cover the offense against you before all who are with you; you are completely vindicated.”
imn: Ninh aengx gorngv mbuox Saalaa, “Yie zorqv siec joih nyaanh daaih bun meih nyei gorx zoux nzaaux hmien nyaanh, bun caux meih yiem nyei mienh hiuv duqv meih za'gengh maiv dorngc. Haaix dauh ziouc hiuv duqv meih maiv zoux dorngc haaix nyungc.”

eng: Then God opened her eyes and she saw a well of water. So she went and filled the skin with water and gave the boy a drink.
imn: Gorngv liuz, Tin-Hungh bun Haagaa buatc norm wuom-kuotv. Haagaa ziouc mingh zaangh wuom buangv ndopv-mbuoqc bun ninh nyei dorn hopv.

eng: God was with the boy as he grew up. He lived in the desert and became an archer. While he was living in the Desert of Paran, his mother got a wife for him from Egypt.
imn: Tin-Hungh caux wuov dauh dorn yiem. Ninh hlo daaih yiem deic-bung-huaang aengx benx dauh buonv orv henv nyei mienh. Ninh yiem Baalaan Deic-Bung-Huaang. Ninh nyei maa mingh gorngv dauh I^yipv sieqv bun ninh longc zoux auv.

eng: Abraham said, “I swear it.”
imn: Apc^laa^ham gorngv, “Yie ziangv jienv Tin-Hungh laengz aqv.”

eng: He replied, “Accept these seven lambs from my hand as a witness that I dug this well.”
imn: Apc^laa^ham dau, “Yie tov meih longc naaiv deix siec dauh ba'gi yungh zeic dorn weic zoux zorng-zengx bun cing se dongh yie wetv naaiv norm wuom-kuotv.”

eng: So that place was called Beersheba, because the two men swore an oath there.
imn: Wuov norm dorngx ninh mbuo ziouc cuotv mbuox heuc Mbe^e^qe^mbaa, weic zuqc ninh mbuo i dauh yiem wuov lomh nzoih liepc jiez ngaengc waac.

eng: “Here I am,” he replied. “Do not lay a hand on the boy,” he said. “Do not do anything to him. Now I know that you fear God, because you have not withheld from me your son, your only son.”
imn: Apc^laa^ham dau, “Oeix! Yie yiem naaiv oc.” Fin-mienh gorngv, “Maiv dungx hoic naaiv dauh dorn. Maiv dungx zoux haaix nyungc bun ninh. Ih zanc yie hiuv duqv meih gamh nziex yaac taaih Tin-Hungh weic zuqc meih maiv nzaeng meih nyei dorn, nyunc zorqv meih nyei ndoqc dorn fungx bun yie.” Apc^laa^ham cau hmien huin nzuonx mangc nqa'haav nqaang, buatc maaih dauh ba'gi yungh gouv, jorng kuangx jienv lomc-mbiorqc. Ninh ziouc mingh zorqv daaih zoux buov ziec nyei ga'naaiv div ninh nyei dorn. Wuov norm dorngx Apc^laa^ham tengx cuotv mbuox heuc “ Ziouv ganh oix liuc leiz ziangx.” Ih jaax hnoi mienh corc gorngv, “Yiem Ziouv nyei mbong, Ziouv ganh liuc leiz ziangx bun.”

eng: Sarah lived to be a hundred and twenty-seven years old. She died at Kiriath Arba (that is, Hebron) in the land of Canaan, and Abraham went to mourn for Sarah and to weep over her.
imn: Saalaa ziangh seix zaangc nyei hnyangx-jeiv duqv yietc baeqv nyic ziepc cietv hnyangx. Ninh guei seix wuov zanc yiem Kaa^naa^an Deic, Ki^li^yaatv Aan^mbaa, se He^mbo^lon Zingh wuov. Saalaa daic mingh, Apc^laa^ham nzauh haic, yiem wuov nyiemv.

eng: Then Abraham rose from beside his dead wife and spoke to the Hittites. He said, “I am a foreigner and stranger among you. Sell me some property for a burial site here so I can bury my dead.”
imn: Apc^laa^ham yiem geqc ninh nyei auv nyei ga'hlen mingh mbuox Hitv^tai Mienh, “Yie se ganh fingx mienh daaih caux meih mbuo yiem naaiv. Tov meih mbuo maaic deix yiem naaiv nyei ndau bun yie zoux zouv-gemh zangx yie nyei auv.”

eng: The Hittites replied to Abraham, “Sir, listen to us. You are a mighty prince among us. Bury your dead in the choicest of our tombs. None of us will refuse you his tomb for burying your dead.”
imn: Wuov deix Hitv^tai Mienh dau, “Domh Gorx aac, meih muangx yie mbuo gorngv oc. Meih yiem yie mbuo mbu'ndongx se yietc dauh zoux hlo nyei mienh. Meih mangc gaax, haaix norm dorngx gauh longx jiex, meih gunv dorh meih nyei auv mingh zangx wuov. Yiem yie mbuo naaiv maiv maaih haaix dauh nzaeng jienv ninh nyei zouv fai laanh jienv maiv bun zangx meih nyei auv.”

eng: Ephron answered Abraham, “Listen to me, my lord; the land is worth four hundred shekels of silver, but what is that between you and me? Bury your dead.”
imn: E^fo^lon dau Apc^laa^ham, “Domh Gorx aac, muangx yie oc. Naaiv nzong ndeic nyei jaax-zinh zic duqv i joih caux faah ziepc feix lungz nyaanh hnangv. Mv baac meih caux yie maiv zuqc funx haaix nyungc aqv. Gunv zangx meih nyei auv aqv.”

eng: Abraham agreed to Ephron’s terms and weighed out for him the price he had named in the hearing of the Hittites: four hundred shekels of silver, according to the weight current among the merchants.
imn: Apc^laa^ham ziouc dingc maaiz aqv. Ei E^fo^lon dorng jienv Hitv^tai Mienh gorngv nyei waac, ninh nziang i joih faah ziepc feix lungz nyaanh, ziux wuov norm deic-bung nyei saeng-eix mienh nziang nyei hniev-daauh, bun E^fo^lon.

eng: The servant asked him, “What if the woman is unwilling to come back with me to this land? Shall I then take your son back to the country you came from?”
imn: Bou naaic gaax, “Se gorngv sieqv maiv kangv gan yie daaih naaiv norm deic-bung ni? Yie oix zuqc fungx meih nyei dorn nzuonx meih cuotv daaih wuov norm deic-bung saah?”

eng: The servant hurried to meet her and said, “Please give me a little water from your jar.”
imn: Apc^laa^ham nyei bou ziouc tiux jienv mingh taux sieqv nyei ga'hlen gorngv, “Tov bun deix meih naaic nie-ha'louh nyei wuom yie hopv oc.”

eng: “Drink, my lord,” she said, and quickly lowered the jar to her hands and gave him a drink.
imn: Wuov dauh sieqv gorngv, “Domh Gorx aac, gunv hopv maah!” Sieqv ziouc bungx nie-ha'louh njiec mba'dauh, bouh jienv bun ninh hopv.

eng: She answered him, “I am the daughter of Bethuel, the son that Milkah bore to Nahor.” And she added, “We have plenty of straw and fodder, as well as room for you to spend the night.”
imn: Sieqv dau, “Yie Mbe^tu^en nyei sieqv. Yie nyei ong heuc Naa^ho. Gux heuc Minkaa.” Ninh aengx gorngv, “Yie mbuo nyei biauv maaih miev maaih mbiauh nqaauv camv nyei uix saeng-kuv. Yaac maaih dorngx bun meih bueix.”

eng: “Then I asked my master, ‘What if the woman will not come back with me?’
imn: “Yie aengx naaic gaax yie nyei ziouv, ‘Se gorngv wuov dauh sieqv maiv kangv gan yie daaih ni?’+”

eng: “Before I finished praying in my heart, Rebekah came out, with her jar on her shoulder. She went down to the spring and drew water, and I said to her, ‘Please give me a drink.’
imn: “Yie yiem hnyouv daux gaux zungv maiv gaengh setv, Le^mbe^kaa ziouc ndaam jienv nie-ha'louh cuotv daaih. Ninh njiec taux wuom-zingv zaangh wuom, yie ziouc mbuox ninh, ‘Tov deix wuom hopv oc.’

eng: “She quickly lowered her jar from her shoulder and said, ‘Drink, and I’ll water your camels too.’ So I drank, and she watered the camels also.
imn: “Ninh ziouc siepv-siepv nyei bungx nie-ha'louh njiec mba'dauh yaac gorngv, ‘Gunv hopv maah! Yie aengx zaangh wuom bun meih nyei lorh torh hopv.’ Yie ziouc hopv. Ninh aengx zaangh wuom bun lorh torh hopv.

eng: “I asked her, ‘Whose daughter are you?’
imn: “Yie aengx naaic ninh, ‘Meih haaix dauh nyei sieqv?’

eng: When they got up the next morning, he said, “Send me on my way to my master.” But her brother and her mother replied, “Let the young woman remain with us ten days or so; then you may go.”
