imn: Da'nyeic ndorm jiez sin daaih, wuov dauh bou gorngv, “Tov bun yie nzuonx yie nyei ziouv wuov oc.” Sieqv nyei gorx caux ninh nyei maa gorngv, “Tov bun sieqv caux yie mbuo yiem ziepc baav hnoi liuz, nqa'haav mingh duqv nyei.”

eng: But he said to them, “Do not detain me, now that the Lord has granted success to my journey. Send me on my way so I may go to my master.”
imn: Wuov dauh bou gorngv, “Tov meih mbuo maiv dungx zorv yie weic zuqc Ziouv tengx yie zoux sung yie nyei jauv mi'aqv. Tov bun yie nzuonx yie nyei ziouv wuov aqv.”

eng: Then they said, “Let’s call the young woman and ask her about it.” So they called Rebekah and asked her, “Will you go with this man?”
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Wuov nyungc, heuc sieqv daaih naaic gaax ninh hnangv haaix nor gorngv.” Ninh mbuo ziouc heuc Le^mbe^kaa daaih naaic, “Meih za'gengh oix gan naaiv dauh m'jangc dorn mingh nyei fai?”

eng: “I will go,” she said. So they sent their sister Rebekah on her way, along with her nurse and Abraham’s servant and his men. And they blessed Rebekah and said to her,
imn: Sieqv dau, “Mingh nyei.” Ninh mbuo ziouc bun Le^mbe^kaa caux dorh Le^mbe^kaa hlo daaih wuov dauh bou gan Apc^laa^ham nyei bou mbuo mingh. Ninh mbuo buang waac Le^mbe^kaa, gorngv mbuox ninh,

eng: Abraham left everything he owned to Isaac. But while he was still living, he gave gifts to the sons of his concubines and sent them away from his son Isaac to the land of the east.
imn: Apc^laa^ham jiu ninh nyei jaa-dingh yietc zungv ga'naaiv bun ninh nyei dorn, I^sakc, nzipc nzengc. Mv baac Apc^laa^ham maiv gaengh guei seix wuov zanc ninh bun zingh nyeic ninh nyei auv-dorn nyei dorn mbuo. Nqa'haav ziouc heuc ninh mbuo leih nqoi I^sakc mingh yiem dong bung maengx nyei deic-bung,

eng: This is the account of the family line of Abraham’s son Ishmael, whom Sarah’s slave, Hagar the Egyptian, bore to Abraham.
imn: Ih zanc gorngv Apc^laa^ham nyei dorn, I^saa^maa^en, nyei zeiv-fun, dongh Saalaa nyei nouh beiz, I^yipv Mienh, Haagaa, yungz bun Apc^laa^ham wuov dauh dorn nyei zeiv-fun.

eng: This is the account of the family line of Abraham’s son Isaac.
imn: Yiem naaiv mingh gorngv Apc^laa^ham nyei dorn, I^sakc, nyei jauv-louc.

eng: Abraham became the father of Isaac, and Isaac was forty years old when he married Rebekah daughter of Bethuel the Aramean from Paddan Aram and sister of Laban the Aramean.
imn: Apc^laa^ham zoux I^sakc nyei die. I^sakc longc Le^mbe^kaa zoux auv wuov zanc ninh duqv feix ziepc hnyangx. Le^mbe^kaa zoux Mbe^tu^en nyei sieqv. Mbe^tu^en se Aalam Mienh, yiem Batc^ndaan Aalam. Le^mbe^kaa yaac zoux Aalam Mienh, Laa^mbaan nyei muoc.

eng: The Lord said to her,
imn: Ziouv gorngv mbuox ninh,

eng: Once when Jacob was cooking some stew, Esau came in from the open country, famished. He said to Jacob, “Quick, let me have some of that red stew! I’m famished!” (That is why he was also called Edom. )
imn: Maaih hnoi nor, Yaakopv wuonh jienv dopc siqv, Esaau mingh gemh nzuonx daaih, hnyouv za'gengh! sie haic. Ninh gorngv mbuox Yaakopv, “Yie hnyouv sie, oix daic haic aqv. Bun deix wuov nyungc ga'naaiv-siqv yie naqv gaax.” (Weic naaiv mienh cuotv mbuox heuc ninh zoux E^ndom.)

eng: Jacob replied, “First sell me your birthright.”
imn: Yaakopv gorngv, “Maaic meih zoux dorn-hlo duqv sung-gouv nyei leiz bun yie ndaangc maah!”

eng: “Look, I am about to die,” Esau said. “What good is the birthright to me?”
imn: Esaau dau, “Mangc maah! Yie oix daic haic aqv. Zoux gorx duqv sung-gouv nyei leiz weic yie maaih haaix nyungc maaih lamh longc?”

eng: But Jacob said, “Swear to me first.” So he swore an oath to him, selling his birthright to Jacob.
imn: Yaakopv gorngv, “Mv baac meih oix zuqc houv maaic bun yie ndaangc.”

eng: Then Jacob gave Esau some bread and some lentil stew. He ate and drank, and then got up and left.
imn: Esaau houv liuz ziouc maaic ninh zoux dorn-hlo duqv sung-gouv nyei leiz bun Yaakopv mi'aqv. Yaakopv ziouc zorqv deix njuov caux dopc bun Esaau nyanc. Nyanc liuz hopv liuz, Esaau ziouc jiez sin mingh mi'aqv. Maiv korv-fiqv ninh zoux dorn-hlo duqv sung-gouv nyei leiz.

eng: So Abimelek gave orders to all the people: “Anyone who harms this man or his wife shall surely be put to death.”
imn: Aa^mbi^me^lekv ziouc hatc ninh nyei baeqc fingx, “Haaix dauh zoux haaix nyungc bun naaiv dauh mienh fai ninh nyei auv, wuov dauh oix zuqc daix daic.”

eng: Then Abimelek said to Isaac, “Move away from us; you have become too powerful for us.”
imn: Aa^mbi^me^lekv gorngv mbuox I^sakc, “Meih oix zuqc suiv nqoi yie mbuo aqv, weic zuqc meih ih zanc gauh henv yie mbuo.”

eng: Isaac built an altar there and called on the name of the Lord . There he pitched his tent, and there his servants dug a well.
imn: I^sakc ziouc yiem wuov ceix norm ziec-dorngh yaac gorngv cuotv Ziouv nyei mbuox daaih zaangc. Ninh yaac corng ndopv-liuh yiem wuov norm dorngx. Ninh nyei bou aengx wetv norm wuom-kuotv.

eng: Isaac then made a feast for them, and they ate and drank. Early the next morning the men swore an oath to each other. Then Isaac sent them on their way, and they went away peacefully.
imn: I^sakc weic ninh mbuo mbenc donx hnaangx longx nyei. Ninh mbuo ziouc yiem wuov nyanc, yiem wuov hopv. Da'nyeic ndorm ninh mbuo jiez sin nziouv nyei, laanh caux laanh liepc jiez ngaengc waac. Liepc liuz, I^sakc fungx ninh mbuo cuotv jauv, baengh orn nyei leih nqoi doic.

eng: That day Isaac’s servants came and told him about the well they had dug. They said, “We’ve found water!” He called it Shibah, and to this day the name of the town has been Beersheba.
imn: Dongh wuov hnoi I^sakc nyei bou daaih gorngv taux ninh mbuo wetv wuom-kuotv nyei jauv. Ninh mbuo gorngv, “Yie mbuo wetv taux wuom mi'aqv.” I^sakc ziouc cuotv wuov norm wuom-kuotv nyei mbuox heuc Qi^mbaa. Weic naaiv, wuov norm dorngx nyei zingh nyei mbuox heuc Mbe^e^qe^mbaa taux ih jaax hnoi.

eng: When Esau was forty years old, he married Judith daughter of Beeri the Hittite, and also Basemath daughter of Elon the Hittite. They were a source of grief to Isaac and Rebekah.
imn: Esaau duqv feix ziepc hnyangx wuov zanc ninh dorng jaa. Ninh longc Hitv^tai Mienh, Mbe^e^li, nyei sieqv, Yu^nditc. Aengx longc Hitv^tai Mienh, Elon, nyei sieqv, Mbaa^se^matv. Weic naaiv deix i dauh m'sieqv dorn, I^sakc caux Le^mbe^kaa zuqc nzauh camv.

eng: His mother said to him, “My son, let the curse fall on me. Just do what I say; go and get them for me.”
imn: Ninh nyei maa gorngv, “Yie nyei dorn aac, se gorngv zuqc houv nor, se ndortv zuqc maa ganh. Kungx oix zuqc muangx maa nyei waac hnangv. Mingh zorqv yungh daaih bun maa oc.”

eng: He went to his father and said, “My father.”
imn: Yaakopv mingh taux ninh nyei die wuov gorngv, “Aa die aac.”

eng: “Yes, my son,” he answered. “Who is it?” Jacob said to his father, “I am Esau your firstborn. I have done as you told me. Please sit up and eat some of my game, so that you may give me your blessing.”
imn: Ninh nyei die dau, “Dorn aac, meih dongh haaix dauh dorn?” Yaakopv dau, “Yie meih nyei dorn-hlo, Esaau. Yie ei die nyei waac mbenc ziangx. Die jiez sin zueiz jienv nyanc yie buonv daaih nyei orv aqv. Nyanc liuz, die duqv buang waac bun yie.”

eng: Isaac asked his son, “How did you find it so quickly, my son?”
imn: I^sakc naaic ninh nyei dorn, “Dorn aah! Meih fungc buonv duqv orv ndongc naaic siepv?”

eng: “The Lord your God gave me success,” he replied. Then Isaac said to Jacob, “Come near so I can touch you, my son, to know whether you really are my son Esau or not.”
imn: Yaakopv dau, “ Ziouv, meih nyei Tin-Hungh, bun yie haih buangh orv siepv nyei.” I^sakc aengx gorngv mbuox Yaakopv, “Dorn aac, daaih fatv deix bun yie hluo gaax, meih za'gengh yie nyei dorn, Esaau, zeiz fai maiv zeiz.”

eng: “I am,” he replied. Then he said, “My son, bring me some of your game to eat, so that I may give you my blessing.”
imn: Yaakopv dau, “Zeiz nyei.” I^sakc gorngv, “Dorn aac, dorh meih buonv daaih nyei orv bun die nyanc. Nyanc liuz ziouc buang waac bun meih.”

eng: Jacob brought it to him and he ate; and he brought some wine and he drank. Then his father Isaac said to him, “Come here, my son, and kiss me.”
imn: Yaakopv ziouc dorh orv daaih bun ninh nyanc, aengx dorh a'ngunc diuv daaih bun ninh hopv. Nyanc liuz, hopv liuz I^sakc gorngv, “Dorn aac, daaih fatv deix zom dangh die nyei hmien.”

eng: So he went to him and kissed him. When Isaac caught the smell of his clothes, he blessed him and said,
imn: Yaakopv mingh zom ninh nyei die wuov zanc, ninh nyei die hnomv zuqc Yaakopv nyei lui nyei nziaau ziouc buang waac ninh nyei dorn aqv. Ninh gorngv,

eng: “Ah, the smell of my son is like the smell of a field that the Lord has blessed. May God give you heaven’s dew
imn: “Yie nyei dorn ndaang nyei, hnangv Ziouv ceix fuqv nyei liangx-ndeic ndaang nor. Tov Tin-Hungh bun mbiungc-suiv yiem wuov lungh njiec,

eng: and earth’s richness— an abundance of grain and new wine. May nations serve you
imn: yaac bun meih nyei ndau longx. Tov ninh yaac bun meih duqv mbiauh hmeiv caux siang-a'ngunc diuv camv. Tov bun camv-fingx mienh fu-sux meih,

eng: His father Isaac asked him, “Who are you?”
imn: Ninh nyei die, I^sakc, naaic gaax, “Meih haaix dauh?”

eng: But he said, “Your brother came deceitfully and took your blessing.”
imn: I^sakc dau, “Dongh yie oix buang meih nyei waac meih nyei youz daaih nduov yie buang bun ninh nzengc mi'aqv.”

eng: Isaac answered Esau, “I have made him lord over you and have made all his relatives his servants, and I have sustained him with grain and new wine. So what can I possibly do for you, my son?”
imn: I^sakc dau, “Die bun ninh zoux meih nyei ziouv yaac bun yietc zungv muoz-doic cien-ceqv zoux ninh nyei bou. Liemh mbiauh hmeiv caux siang-a'ngunc diuv yie bun weic yungz ninh nyei maengc. Dorn aah! Yie maaih haaix nyungc haih tengx meih?”

eng: Esau said to his father, “Do you have only one blessing, my father? Bless me too, my father!” Then Esau wept aloud.
imn: Esaau qaqv tov ninh nyei die, “Aa die aah! Meih kungx maaih nduqc nyungc buang nyei waac hnangv? Tov meih yaac buang deix bun yie maah!” Ninh aengx nyiemv nzunc aqv.

eng: His father Isaac answered him,
imn: I^sakc ziouc gorngv mbuox ninh,

eng: “Your dwelling will be away from the earth’s richness, away from the dew of heaven above. You will live by the sword
imn: “Meih zuqc yiem leih ndau-touv longx nyei dorngx go. Lungh nyei mbiungc-suiv meih yaac maiv duqv. Meih oix zuqc kaux nzuqc ndaauv yungz maengc,

eng: Early the next morning Jacob took the stone he had placed under his head and set it up as a pillar and poured oil on top of it. He called that place Bethel, though the city used to be called Luz.
imn: Da'nyeic ndorm Yaakopv jiez sin daaih nziouv nyei, zorqv ninh nzomz wuov norm la'bieiv liepc jiez daaih don jienv zoux jangx-hoc. Aengx zorqv youh dox jienv la'bieiv fungx bun Tin-Hungh. Wuov norm dorngx ninh cuotv mbuox heuc Mbetc^en. (Zinh ndaangc wuov norm zingh nyei mbuox heuc Lutv.)

eng: Jacob asked the shepherds, “My brothers, where are you from?”
imn: Yaakopv naaic gaax goux yungh nyei mienh, “Gorx-youz aac, meih mbuo yiem haaix daaih?”

eng: “We’re from Harran,” they replied. He said to them, “Do you know Laban, Nahor’s grandson?”
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Yie mbuo yiem Haalaan Zingh daaih.” Ninh aengx naaic gaax, “Meih mbuo hiuv duqv Naa^ho nyei fun, Laa^mbaan, nyei fai?”

eng: “Yes, we know him,” they answered. Then Jacob asked them, “Is he well?”
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Hiuv nyei oc.” Yaakopv aengx naaic gaax, “Ninh longx nyei saah?”

eng: “We can’t,” they replied, “until all the flocks are gathered and the stone has been rolled away from the mouth of the well. Then we will water the sheep.”
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Yie mbuo maiv haih hnangv naaic zoux. Yie mbuo oix zuqc zuov guanh guanh ba'gi yungh daaih nzoih cingx nzien nqoi la'bieiv zaangh wuom bun yungh hopv.”

eng: After Jacob had stayed with him for a whole month, Laban said to him, “Just because you are a relative of mine, should you work for me for nothing? Tell me what your wages should be.”
imn: Laa^mbaan gorngv mbuox Yaakopv, “Meih maiv dungx hnamv laaix zoux cien-ceqv muoz-doic oix zuqc baeqc baeqc tengx yie zoux gong. Meih oix longc gong-zinh mbu'ziex gunv mbuox yie.”

eng: Then Jacob said to Laban, “Give me my wife. My time is completed, and I want to make love to her.”
imn: Yaakopv gorngv mbuox Laa^mbaan, “Ziangh hoc buangv aqv. Tov bun yie nyei auv yie, weic bun yie duqv bieqc mingh caux ninh bueix.”

eng: When morning came, there was Leah! So Jacob said to Laban, “What is this you have done to me? I served you for Rachel, didn’t I? Why have you deceived me?”
imn: Taux lungh njang, Yaakopv zieqv duqv se Le^aa, ziouc mingh doix Laa^mbaan gorngv, “Meih weic haaix diuc hnangv naaiv nor zoux bun yie? Yie tengx meih zoux gong bietv Laasen maiv zeiz? Meih weic haaix diuc pienx yie?”

eng: She conceived again, and when she gave birth to a son she said, “Because the Lord heard that I am not loved, he gave me this one too.” So she named him Simeon.
imn: Le^aa aengx maaih jienv sin, yungz duqv dauh dorn. Ninh gorngv, “ Ziouv bun yie aengx duqv naaiv dauh dorn weic zuqc ninh muangx haiz yie nyei nqox maiv hnamv yie.” Le^aa ziouc cuotv naaiv dauh dorn nyei mbuox heuc Si^me^on.

eng: Again she conceived, and when she gave birth to a son she said, “Now at last my husband will become attached to me, because I have borne him three sons.” So he was named Levi.
imn: Le^aa aengx maaih nzunc sin, aengx yungz duqv dauh dorn. Ninh gorngv, “Yie yungz duqv buo dauh dorn. Yie nyei nqox za'gengh! oix hnamv yie.” Ninh aengx tengx wuov dauh dorn cuotv mbuox heuc Lewi.

eng: She conceived again, and when she gave birth to a son she said, “This time I will praise the Lord .” So she named him Judah. Then she stopped having children.
imn: Ninh aengx maaih jienv sin, aengx yungz duqv dauh dorn. Ninh gorngv, “Naaiv nzunc yie oix ceng Ziouv. ” Ninh cuotv mbuox heuc Yu^ndaa. Ninh duqv liuz naaiv dauh dorn ziouc maiv yungz aqv.

eng: Then she said, “Here is Bilhah, my servant. Sleep with her so that she can bear children for me and I too can build a family through her.”
imn: Laasen gorngv, “Naaiv yie nyei nouh beiz, Mbin^haa. Meih gunv caux ninh bueix maah! Ninh haih yungz gu'nguaaz bun yie, yie ziouc laaix ninh duqv dauh gu'nguaaz funx yie nyei buonc.”

eng: Rachel’s servant Bilhah conceived again and bore Jacob a second son. Then Rachel said, “I have had a great struggle with my sister, and I have won.” So she named him Naphtali.
imn: Laasen nyei nouh beiz, Mbin^haa, aengx maaih jienv sin, yungz duqv da'nyeic dauh dorn bun Yaakopv. Laasen gorngv, “Yie caux yie nyei dorc nzaeng domh jaax yaac nzaeng duqv hingh.” Ninh tengx wuov dauh dorn cuotv mbuox heuc Napv^taa^li.

eng: Leah’s servant Zilpah bore Jacob a second son. Then Leah said, “How happy I am! The women will call me happy.” So she named him Asher.
imn: Le^aa nyei nouh beiz, Sinx^baa, aengx yungz duqv da'nyeic dauh dorn bun Yaakopv. Le^aa gorngv, “Yie njien-youh haic aqv. Zuangx m'sieqv dorn yaac oix gorngv yie njien-youh haic.” Ninh ziouc tengx wuov dauh dorn cuotv mbuox heuc Aa^se.

eng: During wheat harvest, Reuben went out into the fields and found some mandrake plants, which he brought to his mother Leah. Rachel said to Leah, “Please give me some of your son’s mandrakes.”
imn: Gaatv mbiauh witv nyei ziangh hoc, Lu^mben mingh saau wuov ndeic, buatc zungh ndu^ndaa^im jieh. Ninh ziouc dorh daaih bun ninh nyei maa Le^aa. Laasen gorngv mbuox ninh nyei dorc, “Tov bun deix dorc nyei dorn nyei ndu^ndaa^im jieh yie maah!”

eng: But she said to her, “Wasn’t it enough that you took away my husband? Will you take my son’s mandrakes too?”
imn: Le^aa dau, “Yie nyei nqox meih zungv nzaeng mingh aqv. Corc maiv gaengh gaux? Meih aengx oix nzaeng yie nyei dorn nyei ndu^ndaa^im jieh?”

eng: God listened to Leah, and she became pregnant and bore Jacob a fifth son. Then Leah said, “God has rewarded me for giving my servant to my husband.” So she named him Issachar.
imn: Tin-Hungh dau Le^aa jouh ninh nyei waac. Le^aa aengx maaih jienv sin, yungz duqv da'hmz dauh dorn bun Yaakopv. Le^aa gorngv, “Weic zuqc yie bun yie nyei nouh beiz yie nyei nqox longc, Tin-Hungh bun zingh nyeic yie.” Ninh cuotv wuov dauh dorn nyei mbuox heuc Itc^saax^kaa.

eng: Some time later she gave birth to a daughter and named her Dinah.
imn: Nqa'haav Le^aa aengx yungz duqv dauh sieqv yaac cuotv mbuox heuc Ndinaa.

eng: But Laban said to him, “If I have found favor in your eyes, please stay. I have learned by divination that the Lord has blessed me because of you.” He added, “Name your wages, and I will pay them.”
imn: Mv baac Laa^mbaan gorngv, “Se gorngv meih buangv hnyouv, tov aengx caux yie yiem. Yie samx duqv cuotv, hiuv duqv se weic meih, Ziouv cingx ceix fuqv bun yie. Meih nyei gong-zinh oix longc mbu'ziex gunv gorngv mbuox yie maah! Yie ziouc bun.”

eng: “What shall I give you?” he asked.
imn: Laa^mbaan naaic gaax ninh, “Wuov nyungc, yie oix zuqc bun haaix nyungc meih?”

eng: Then the Lord said to Jacob, “Go back to the land of your fathers and to your relatives, and I will be with you.”
imn: Ziouv gorngv mbuox Yaakopv, “Nzuonx meih nyei cien-ceqv nyei deic-bung wuov oc. Yie oix caux meih yiem.”

eng: Jacob also went on his way, and the angels of God met him. When Jacob saw them, he said, “This is the camp of God!” So he named that place Mahanaim.
imn: Yaakopv gan jienv jauv mingh wuov zanc, maaih deix Tin-Hungh nyei fin-mienh daaih buangh ninh. Yaakopv buatc fin-mienh ninh gorngv, “Naaiv se Tin-Hungh nyei baeng.” Ninh ziouc cuotv wuov norm dorngx nyei mbuox heuc Maa^haa^naa^im.

eng: When the messengers returned to Jacob, they said, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is coming to meet you, and four hundred men are with him.”
imn: Douc fienx mienh nzuonx taux Yaakopv wuov gorngv, “Yie mbuo taux meih nyei gorx Esaau wuov mi'aqv. Ih zanc ninh daaih buangh meih, aengx maaih feix baeqv dauh mienh caux jienv ninh daaih.”

eng: But Jacob replied, “I will not let you go unless you bless me.” The man asked him, “What is your name?”
imn: Yaakopv dau, “Meih maiv buang waac yie nor, yie maiv bungx meih.” Wuov dauh mienh naaic gaax, “Meih nyei mbuox heuc haaix nyungc?”

eng: “Jacob,” he answered. Then the man said, “Your name will no longer be Jacob, but Israel, because you have struggled with God and with humans and have overcome.”
imn: Ninh dau, “Yie heuc Yaakopv.” Wuov dauh mienh gorngv, “Yiem naaiv mingh meih nyei mbuox maiv heuc Yaakopv aqv. Meih nyei mbuox heuc I^saa^laa^en, weic zuqc meih caux Tin-Hungh borngz jaax yaac borngz duqv hingh. Caux mienh borngz jaax yaac borngz duqv hingh.”

eng: Jacob said, “Please tell me your name.”
imn: Yaakopv gorngv, “Tov mbuox yie, meih nyei mbuox heuc haaix nyungc?”

eng: But he replied, “Why do you ask my name?” Then he blessed him there. So Jacob called the place Peniel, saying, “It is because I saw God face to face, and yet my life was spared.”
imn: Mv baac ninh dau, “Meih weic haaix diuc naaic yie nyei mbuox?” Ninh ziouc yiem wuov buang waac Yaakopv. Yaakopv gorngv, “Yie hmien doix hmien buatc liuz Tin-Hungh, yie corc maaih maengc ziangh nyei.” Ninh ziouc cuotv wuov norm dorngx nyei mbuox heuc Be^ni^en. Yaakopv jiex Be^ni^en wuov zanc, mba'hnoi cuotv daaih aqv. Ninh ziouc la'mbai-la'mbai nyei mingh aqv, laaix ninh nyei camh zuih mbungv piatv mingh. Laaix Yaakopv zuqc wuov dauh mienh muoqv ninh nyei camh zuih mbungv piatv mingh, yiem naaic daaih taux ih zanc, I^saa^laa^en Mienh maiv nyanc camh zuih mbungv nyei jaan.

eng: But Esau ran to meet Jacob and embraced him; he threw his arms around his neck and kissed him. And they wept. Then Esau looked up and saw the women and children. “Who are these with you?” he asked.
imn: Mv baac Esaau tiux mingh zipv, qam jienv zom. I muoz zungv nyiemv. Esaau cau hmien buatc wuov deix m'sieqv dorn caux fu'jueiv, ninh naaic gaax, “Gan meih daaih naaiv deix mienh se haaix dauh?”

eng: Esau asked, “What’s the meaning of all these flocks and herds I met?”
imn: Esaau naaic gaax, “Yie buangh wuov deix saeng-kuv guanh maaih haaix nyungc eix leiz?”

eng: “To find favor in your eyes, my lord,” he said. But Esau said, “I already have plenty, my brother. Keep what you have for yourself.”
imn: Yaakopv dau, “Ov, naaiv se weic oix bun gorx nqoi fim nqoi eix hnamv yie.” Mv baac Esaau dau, “Youz aac, yie mv baac maaih gaux longc nyei. Meih nyei, liouh meih ganh longc aqv.”

eng: Then Esau said, “Let us be on our way; I’ll accompany you.”
imn: Esaau gorngv, “Mbuo lomh nzoih mingh aqv. Yie mingh ndaangc dorh meih.”

eng: Esau said, “Then let me leave some of my men with you.”
imn: Esaau gorngv, “Wuov nyungc, yie bun i ziex dauh yie ganh nyei mienh caux meih yiem naaiv.”

eng: When Jacob heard that his daughter Dinah had been defiled, his sons were in the fields with his livestock; so he did nothing about it until they came home.
imn: Yaakopv hiuv duqv Sekem zoux bun ninh nyei sieqv nyei mengh dauh waaic mv baac Yaakopv maiv gaengh gorngv haaix nyungc weic zuqc ninh nyei dorn mbuo yiem wuov naah bungx saeng-kuv. Ninh zuov jienv ninh nyei dorn mbuo nzuonx.

eng: All the men who went out of the city gate agreed with Hamor and his son Shechem, and every male in the city was circumcised.
imn: Wuov norm zingh nyei yietc zuangx gorx-youz buangv hnyouv Haamo caux Sekem nyei waac. Zingh gu'nyuoz nyei yietc zungv m'jangc dorn ziouc jiex gaatv nyei leiz nzengc.

eng: But they replied, “Should he have treated our sister like a prostitute?”
imn: Ninh nyei dorn mbuo dau, “Yie mbuo maiv bun ninh zoux bun yie mbuo nyei muoc dorngx maaic buonv-sin nyei m'sieqv dorn.”

eng: Now Deborah, Rebekah’s nurse, died and was buried under the oak outside Bethel. So it was named Allon Bakuth.
imn: Dongh zinh ndaangc dorh Le^mbe^kaa hlo daaih wuov dauh bou, Nde^mbo^laa, guei seix mi'aqv. Ninh mbuo ziouc zangx jienv yiem Mbetc^en nyei naamh bung, yietc diuh domh mba'ong zuei ndiangx-gorn hlen. Wuov diuh ndiangx ninh mbuo cuotv norm mbuox heuc Anlon Mbaakutv.

eng: Jacob set up a stone pillar at the place where God had talked with him, and he poured out a drink offering on it; he also poured oil on it. Jacob called the place where God had talked with him Bethel.
imn: Yaakopv zorqv norm la'bieiv don jienv Tin-Hungh caux ninh gorngv waac nyei dorngx, yaac zorqv a'ngunc diuv ciev dongc gu'nguaaic, yaac zorqv ga'lanv youh dox jienv. Ninh aengx cuotv wuov norm dorngx nyei mbuox heuc Mbetc^en.

eng: So Rachel died and was buried on the way to Ephrath (that is, Bethlehem). Over her tomb Jacob set up a pillar, and to this day that pillar marks Rachel’s tomb.
imn: Laasen daic mi'aqv, zangx jienv mingh E^faa^laa^taa nyei jauv-hlen. (E^faa^laa^taa aengx maaih norm mbuox heuc Mbetc^le^hem.) Yaakopv zorqv norm la'bieiv don jienv Laasen nyei zouv zoux jangx-hoc. Wuov norm la'bieiv yiem taux ih jaax hnoi.

eng: Israel moved on again and pitched his tent beyond Migdal Eder. While Israel was living in that region, Reuben went in and slept with his father’s concubine Bilhah, and Israel heard of it.
imn: I^saa^laa^en mbuo aengx suiv mingh corng ndopv-liuh yiem E^nde Fanh Taapv nyei wuov ndaangc maengx.

eng: Jacob had twelve sons: The sons of Leah:
imn: Yaakopv maaih ziepc nyeic dauh dorn. Le^aa nyei dorn maaih Lu^mben. Lu^mben se Yaakopv nyei dorn-hlo. Aengx maaih Si^me^on, Lewi, Yu^ndaa, Itc^saax^kaa, caux Se^mbu^lun.

eng: Reuben the firstborn of Jacob, Simeon, Levi, Judah, Issachar and Zebulun. The sons of Rachel:
imn: Laasen nyei dorn maaih Yosepv caux Mben^yaa^min.

eng: Joseph and Benjamin. The sons of Rachel’s servant Bilhah:
imn: Laasen nyei nouh beiz, Mbin^haa, nyei dorn maaih Ndaan caux Napv^taa^li.

eng: Dan and Naphtali. The sons of Leah’s servant Zilpah:
imn: Le^aa nyei nouh beiz, Sinx^baa, nyei dorn maaih Gaatc caux Aa^se.

eng: Adah bore Eliphaz to Esau, Basemath bore Reuel, and Oholibamah bore Jeush, Jalam and Korah. These were the sons of Esau, who were born to him in Canaan.
imn: Aa^ndaa yungz duqv E^li^fatv bun Esaau. Mbaa^se^matv yungz duqv Le^u^en. O^ho^li^mbaa^maa yungz duqv Ye^utc, Yaalaam caux Kolaa. Naaiv deix se Esaau nyei dorn, yiem Kaa^naa^an Deic-Bung yungz daaih.

eng: This is the account of the family line of Esau the father of the Edomites in the hill country of Seir.
imn: Yiem naaiv mingh gorngv yiem Se^i Deic nyei mbong-aiv dorngx, E^ndom Mienh nyei ong-taaix, Esaau, nyei zeiv-fun.

eng: These are the names of Esau’s sons:
imn: Naaiv se Esaau nyei dorn nyei mbuox. Maaih Esaau nyei auv, Aa^ndaa, nyei dorn, E^li^fatv, caux Esaau nyei auv, Mbaa^se^matv, nyei dorn, Le^u^en.

eng: Eliphaz, the son of Esau’s wife Adah, and Reuel, the son of Esau’s wife Basemath. The sons of Eliphaz:
imn: E^li^fatv nyei dorn maaih Temaan, Omaa, Sefo, Gaataam caux Kenatv. Esaau nyei dorn, E^li^fatv, aengx maaih dauh auv-dorn, mbuox heuc Timnaa. Timnaa yungz duqv dauh dorn, mbuox heuc Aa^maa^lekv, bun E^li^fatv. Naaiv deix dorn yietc zungv zoux Esaau nyei auv, Aa^ndaa, nyei fun.

eng: The sons of Reuel:
imn: Le^u^en nyei dorn maaih Naa^hatc, Zelaa, Sammaa caux Mitv^saa. Naaiv se Esaau nyei auv, Mbaa^se^matv, nyei fun.

eng: Nahath, Zerah, Shammah and Mizzah. These were grandsons of Esau’s wife Basemath. The sons of Esau’s wife Oholibamah daughter of Anah and granddaughter of Zibeon, whom she bore to Esau:
imn: Esaau nyei auv, Si^mbe^on nyei dorn, Aanaa, nyei sieqv, O^ho^li^mbaa^maa, yungz duqv buo dauh dorn bun Esaau. Maaih Ye^utc, Yaalaam caux Kolaa.

eng: Jeush, Jalam and Korah. These were the chiefs among Esau’s descendants:
imn: Naaiv se Esaau nyei zeiv-fun zoux bieiv zeiv nyei mienh.

eng: The sons of Eliphaz the firstborn of Esau: Chiefs Teman, Omar, Zepho, Kenaz, Korah, Gatam and Amalek. These were the chiefs descended from Eliphaz in Edom; they were grandsons of Adah.
imn: Maaih Esaau nyei dorn-hlo, E^li^fatv nyei dorn, Temaan, Omaa, Sefo, Kenatv, Kolaa, Gaataam, caux Aa^maa^lekv. Naaiv se yiem E^ndom Deic-Bung gan E^li^fatv cuotv nyei bieiv zeiv, se Esaau nyei auv, Aa^ndaa, nyei fun.

eng: The sons of Esau’s son Reuel:
imn: Maaih Esaau nyei dorn, Le^u^en, nyei dorn Naa^hatc, Zelaa, Sammaa caux Mitv^saa. Naaiv se yiem E^ndom Deic-Bung gan Le^u^en cuotv nyei bieiv zeiv, se Esaau nyei auv, Mbaa^se^matv, nyei fun.

eng: Chiefs Nahath, Zerah, Shammah and Mizzah. These were the chiefs descended from Reuel in Edom; they were grandsons of Esau’s wife Basemath. The sons of Esau’s wife Oholibamah:
imn: Maaih Esaau nyei auv, O^ho^li^mbaa^maa, nyei dorn, Ye^utc, Yaalaam caux Kolaa. Naaiv se yiem Esaau nyei auv, Aanaa nyei sieqv, O^ho^li^mbaa^maa, cuotv nyei bieiv zeiv.

eng: Chiefs Jeush, Jalam and Korah. These were the chiefs descended from Esau’s wife Oholibamah daughter of Anah. These were the sons of Esau (that is, Edom), and these were their chiefs.
imn: Naaiv deix yietc zungv se Esaau (dongh E^ndom) nyei dorn caux yiem Esaau cuotv daaih nyei bieiv zeiv.

eng: These were the sons of Seir the Horite, who were living in the region:
imn: Naaiv se yiem wuov norm deic-bung nyei Holi Mienh, Se^i nyei dorn. Maaih Lotaan, So^mbaan, Si^mbe^on, Aanaa, Ndison, E^se caux Ndisaan. Naaiv deix mienh se yiem E^ndom Deic, Se^i nyei zeiv-fun, Holi Mienh nyei bieiv zeiv.

eng: The sons of Lotan:
imn: Lotaan nyei dorn maaih Holi caux Homam. Lotaan nyei muoc heuc Timnaa.

eng: Hori and Homam. Timna was Lotan’s sister. The sons of Shobal:
imn: So^mbaan nyei dorn maaih Anwaan, Maa^naa^haatv, E^mbaan, Qefo caux Onam.

eng: Alvan, Manahath, Ebal, Shepho and Onam. The sons of Zibeon:
imn: Si^mbe^on nyei dorn maaih Aiyaa caux Aanaa. Dongh naaiv dauh Aanaa yiem deic-bung-huaang tengx ninh nyei die, Si^mbe^on, bungx lorh wuov zanc buatc zingv wuom-jorm.

eng: Aiah and Anah. This is the Anah who discovered the hot springs in the desert while he was grazing the donkeys of his father Zibeon. The children of Anah:
imn: Aanaa nyei dorn heuc Ndison. Aanaa yaac maaih dauh sieqv heuc O^ho^li^mbaa^maa.

eng: Dishon and Oholibamah daughter of Anah. The sons of Dishon :
imn: Ndison nyei dorn maaih Hem^ndaan, Etc^mbaan, Itc^laan caux Kelaan.

eng: Hemdan, Eshban, Ithran and Keran. The sons of Ezer:
imn: E^se nyei dorn maaih Mbin^haan, Saax^waan caux Aakaanx.

eng: Bilhan, Zaavan and Akan. The sons of Dishan:
imn: Ndisaan nyei dorn maaih Utc caux Aalan.

eng: Uz and Aran. These were the Horite chiefs:
imn: Naaiv se Holi Mienh nyei bieiv zeiv. Maaih Lotaan, So^mbaan, Si^mbe^on, Aanaa, Ndison, E^se caux Ndisaan. Naaiv deix yietc zungv se yiem Se^i Deic-Bung, Holi Mienh, ziux ninh mbuo nyei bungh buonc bieiv zeiv.

eng: These were the kings who reigned in Edom before any Israelite king reigned:
imn: Zinh ndaangc I^saa^laa^en Mienh maiv gaengh maaih hungh diex gunv, E^ndom Deic-Bung maaih hungh diex gunv nyei.

eng: Bela son of Beor became king of Edom. His city was named Dinhabah.
imn: Mbe^o nyei dorn, Mbelaa, duqv zoux E^ndom nyei hungh. Ninh nyei domh zingh heuc Ndin^haa^mbaa.

eng: When Bela died, Jobab son of Zerah from Bozrah succeeded him as king.
imn: Mbelaa guei seix, aengx maaih yiem Mbo^saa^laa Zingh daaih wuov dauh Zelaa nyei dorn, Yo^mbapc, duqv nzipc zoux hungh.

eng: When Jobab died, Husham from the land of the Temanites succeeded him as king.
imn: Yo^mbapc guei seix, aengx maaih yiem Temaan Deic-Bung daaih wuov dauh Husaam duqv nzipc zoux hungh.

eng: When Husham died, Hadad son of Bedad, who defeated Midian in the country of Moab, succeeded him as king. His city was named Avith.
imn: Husaam guei seix, aengx maaih Mbe^ndatc nyei dorn, Haa^ndatc, duqv nzipc zoux hungh. Haa^ndatc dongh yiem Mo^apc Deic mborqv hingh Mi^ndien Mienh wuov dauh. Ninh nyei domh zingh heuc Aawitv.

eng: When Hadad died, Samlah from Masrekah succeeded him as king.
imn: Haa^ndatc guei seix, aengx maaih yiem Matv^le^kaa Zingh daaih wuov dauh Samx^laa duqv nzipc zoux hungh.

eng: When Samlah died, Shaul from Rehoboth on the river succeeded him as king.
imn: Samx^laa guei seix, aengx maaih yiem Yu^fe^ditc Ndaaih hlen nyei Le^hox^mbotc Zingh daaih wuov dauh Saa^un duqv nzipc zoux hungh.

eng: When Shaul died, Baal-Hanan son of Akbor succeeded him as king.
imn: Saa^un guei seix, Akc^mbo nyei dorn, Mbaa^an Haanan, duqv nzipc zoux hungh.

eng: When Baal-Hanan son of Akbor died, Hadad succeeded him as king. His city was named Pau, and his wife’s name was Mehetabel daughter of Matred, the daughter of Me-Zahab.
imn: Akc^mbo nyei dorn, Mbaa^an Haanan, guei seix, Haa^ndaa duqv nzipc zoux hungh. Ninh nyei domh zingh heuc Baa^u. Ninh nyei auv heuc Me^he^taa^mben. Me^he^taa^mben zoux Matv^letv nyei sieqv, Me^saa^hapc nyei fun.

eng: These were the chiefs descended from Esau, by name, according to their clans and regions:
imn: Yiem naaiv mingh se yiem Esaau bun njiec nyei bieiv zeiv ziux ninh mbuo nyei mbuox, ninh mbuo nyei bungh buonc caux ninh mbuo yiem nyei dorngx. Maaih

eng: Timna, Alvah, Jetheth, Oholibamah, Elah, Pinon, Kenaz, Teman, Mibzar, Magdiel and Iram. These were the chiefs of Edom, according to their settlements in the land they occupied.
imn: Timnaa, Anwaa, Yetetv, O^ho^li^mbaa^maa, Elaa. Binon, Kenatv, Temaan, Mipv^saa, Makv^ndi^en, I^laam. Naaiv se E^ndom nyei bieiv zeiv ziux ninh mbuo yiem nyei deic liepc ninh mbuo nyei nguaaz-gorn nyei dorngx.

eng: This is the family line of Esau, the father of the Edomites.
imn: Naaiv se E^ndom Mienh nyei ong-taaix, Esaau, nyei zeiv-fun.

eng: Jacob lived in the land where his father had stayed, the land of Canaan. This is the account of Jacob’s family line.
imn: Yaakopv corc yiem jienv zinh ndaangc ninh nyei die yiem wuov norm deic-bung, se Kaa^naa^an Deic-Bung. Yiem naaiv mingh gorngv Yaakopv nyei dorn-jueiv nyei jauv-louc.

eng: His brothers said to him, “Do you intend to reign over us? Will you actually rule us?” And they hated him all the more because of his dream and what he had said.
imn: Ninh nyei gorx mbuo naaic gaax ninh, “Meih gengh oix zoux yie mbuo nyei hungh? Oix gunv yie mbuo?” Laaix Yosepv mbeix nyei mbeix caux ninh gorngv nyei waac, ninh nyei gorx mbuo gauh fatv nzorng ninh aqv.

eng: Then he had another dream, and he told it to his brothers. “Listen,” he said, “I had another dream, and this time the sun and moon and eleven stars were bowing down to me.”
imn: Nqa'haav Yosepv aengx mbeix buatc nzunc yaac gorngv bun ninh nyei gorx mbuo muangx. Ninh gorngv, “Yie aengx mbeix buatc. Naaiv nzunc yie buatc mba'hnoi caux hlaax caux ziepc yietv norm hleix yietc zungv baaix yie.”

eng: “Very well,” he replied. So he said to him, “Go and see if all is well with your brothers and with the flocks, and bring word back to me.” Then he sent him off from the Valley of Hebron.
imn: Yosepv dau, “Mingh nyei.” Ninh nyei die mbuox ninh, “Yie oix meih mingh mangc gaax meih nyei gorx mbuo longx nyei fai? Yungh longx nyei fai? Mangc liuz nzuonx daaih mbuox die oc.” Ninh nyei die ziouc mbuox ninh mingh, ninh ziouc yiem He^mbo^lon Horngc cuotv jauv mingh Sekem.

eng: When Joseph arrived at Shechem, a man found him wandering around in the fields and asked him, “What are you looking for?”
imn: Yosepv mingh taux Sekem, Maaih dauh mienh buangh zuqc ninh yiem lomc-huaang jiex naaiv jiex wuov nyei, ziouc naaic gaax ninh, “Meih lorz haaix nyungc?”

eng: He replied, “I’m looking for my brothers. Can you tell me where they are grazing their flocks?”
imn: Yosepv dau, “Yie lorz yie nyei gorx mbuo. Meih hiuv duqv ninh mbuo yiem haaix bungx yungh, tov mbuox yie oc.”

eng: “They have moved on from here,” the man answered. “I heard them say, ‘Let’s go to Dothan.’ ”
imn: Wuov dauh mienh gorngv, “Ninh mbuo suiv dorngx mi'aqv. Yie haiz ninh mbuo gorngv oix suiv mingh Ndotaan.” Yosepv ziouc mingh lorz ninh nyei gorx mbuo, lorz buatc yiem Ndotaan.

eng: So Joseph went after his brothers and found them near Dothan. But they saw him in the distance, and before he reached them, they plotted to kill him.
imn: Ninh nyei gorx mbuo buatc Yosepv corc yiem go nyei. Daaih maiv gaengh taux, ninh mbuo ziouc lomh nzoih caangh laangh daav za'eix daix. Laanh mbuox laanh, “Wuov dauh ga'naaiv-mbeix mbeix daaih aqv loh! Mbuo lomh nzoih mingh zorqv ninh daix guangc aqv. Daix dapv jienv wuom-kuotv-nqaai. Mbuo ziouc gorngv ga'naaiv ngaatc mi'aqv. Mbuo mangc gaax ninh nyei mbeix ndongc haaix lingh?”

eng: So when Joseph came to his brothers, they stripped him of his robe—the ornate robe he was wearing— and they took him and threw him into the cistern. The cistern was empty; there was no water in it.
imn: Yosepv daaih taux, ninh nyei gorx mbuo ziouc caa jienv zorqv ninh nyei lui ndaauv, dongh maaih lui-mueiz-ndaauv wuov deix lui, ciev guangc mingh, aengx zorqv ninh fongv ndortv wuom-kuotv. Wuov norm kuotv maiv maaih wuom.

eng: So when the Midianite merchants came by, his brothers pulled Joseph up out of the cistern and sold him for twenty shekels of silver to the Ishmaelites, who took him to Egypt.
imn: Wuov deix zoux saeng-eix nyei Mi^ndien Mienh jiex jauv taux wuov. Yosepv nyei gorx mbuo ziouc cie Yosepv cuotv kuotv daaih maaic bun naaiv deix I^saa^maa^en Mienh, duqv luoqc lungz nyaanh. Wuov deix saeng-eix mienh ziouc dorh Yosepv mingh I^yipv Deic-Bung aqv.

eng: When Reuben returned to the cistern and saw that Joseph was not there, he tore his clothes. He went back to his brothers and said, “The boy isn’t there! Where can I turn now?”
imn: Lu^mben daaih taux wuov norm kuotv, yaac maiv buatc Yosepv mi'aqv. Ninh nzauh haic ziouc betv ganh nyei lui. Ninh nzuonx ninh nyei youz mbuo wuov gorngv, “Wuov dauh dorn maiv yiem naaiv aqv loh! Yie oix zuqc hnangv haaix nor zoux?”

eng: He recognized it and said, “It is my son’s robe! Some ferocious animal has devoured him. Joseph has surely been torn to pieces.”
imn: Yaakopv zieqv duqv nyei. Ninh gorngv, “Naaiv za'gengh! yie nyei dorn nyei lui. Za'gengh! ga'naaiv ngaatc, ga'naaiv betv mi'aqv lorqc.”

eng: Meanwhile, the Midianites sold Joseph in Egypt to Potiphar, one of Pharaoh’s officials, the captain of the guard.
imn: Dongh wuov zanc yiem I^yipv Deic-Bung wuov deix zoux saeng-eix nyei Mi^ndien Mienh aengx maaic Yosepv bun hungh diex nyei yietc dauh jien, se goux hungh dinc nyei domh baeng-bieiv, Bo^ti^faa, mi'aqv.

eng: Judah got a wife for Er, his firstborn, and her name was Tamar. But Er, Judah’s firstborn, was wicked in the Lord ’s sight; so the Lord put him to death.
imn: Yu^ndaa tengx ninh nyei dorn-hlo, E^aa, lorz duqv dauh auv, mbuox heuc Taamaa. Mv baac ziux Ziouv mangc, E^aa waaic haic. Ziouv ziouc bun ninh daic mi'aqv.

eng: “And what will you give me to sleep with you?” she asked. “I’ll send you a young goat from my flock,” he said.
imn: Taamaa naaic, “Yie caux meih bueix, meih bun haaix nyungc yie?” Yu^ndaa dau, “Yie oix zorqv dauh yie nyei yungh guanh nyei yungh dorn bun meih.”

eng: “Will you give me something as a pledge until you send it?” she asked. He said, “What pledge should I give you?”
imn: Taamaa dau, “Meih oix longc haaix nyungc dingc jienv ndaangc taux meih fungx yungh dorn daaih?” Yu^ndaa naaic gaax, “Meih oix yie dingc haaix nyungc ndaangc gauh longx?”

eng: Then Judah said, “Let her keep what she has, or we will become a laughingstock. After all, I did send her this young goat, but you didn’t find her.”
imn: Yu^ndaa gorngv, “Wuov deix ga'naaiv cinh ninh longc aqv. Nziex mienh oix jatv mbuo mv bei. Dorh yungh dorn mingh oix bun yaac lorz maiv buatc ninh bun.”

eng: About three months later Judah was told, “Your daughter-in-law Tamar is guilty of prostitution, and as a result she is now pregnant.”
imn: Nqa'haav aav lamh deix buo hlaax nyieqc nyei dorngx, maaih mienh mbuox Yu^ndaa, “Meih nyei mbuangz, Taamaa, hienx nqox. Ih zanc toi gu'nguaaz aqv.”

eng: Judah recognized them and said, “She is more righteous than I, since I wouldn’t give her to my son Shelah.” And he did not sleep with her again.
imn: Yu^ndaa zieqv duqv naaiv deix ga'naaiv se ninh nyei. Ninh gorngv, “Naaiv dauh m'sieqv dorn zoux nyei se gauh horpc yie zoux nyei, weic zuqc yie maiv bun ninh longc yie nyei dorn, Qelaa.” Nqa'haav Yu^ndaa yietc liuz maiv caux ninh bueix.

eng: Now Joseph was well-built and handsome, and after a while his master’s wife took notice of Joseph and said, “Come to bed with me!”
imn: Yosepv nyei sin-pei ziangh duqv longx, hmien-mueic yaac nzueic. Yiem gau, ninh nyei ziouv nyei auv mangc Yosepv haiz hanc haic, ziouc yuoqc ninh gorngv, “Meih daaih caux yie bueix oc.”

eng: When Joseph came to them the next morning, he saw that they were dejected. So he asked Pharaoh’s officials who were in custody with him in his master’s house, “Why do you look so sad today?”
imn: Da'nyeic ndorm Yosepv bieqc mingh mangc, buatc ninh mbuo i dauh zungv nzauh nzauh wuov. Ninh naaic gaax Faalo nyei domh jien, dongh caux ninh zuqc wuonx jienv ninh nyei ziouv nyei biauv nyei loh wuov deix i dauh, “Meih mbuo ih hnoi zoux haaix nyungc? Buatc nzauh nzauh wuov!”

eng: “We both had dreams,” they answered, “but there is no one to interpret them.”
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “A'hmuangx yie mbuo i dauh zungv mbeix mbeix. Yiem naaiv yaac maiv maaih haaix dauh haih tengx yie mbuo porv mbeix.”

eng: “This is what it means,” Joseph said. “The three baskets are three days. Within three days Pharaoh will lift off your head and impale your body on a pole. And the birds will eat away your flesh.”
imn: Yosepv gorngv, “Meih nyei mbeix nyei eix leiz se hnangv naaiv. Wuov deix buo norm jei beiv buo hnoi. Aengx buo hnoi Faalo Hungh oix bun mienh hngaqv ndutv meih nyei m'nqorngv aengx zorqv meih nyei sei ndiux jienv ndiangx. Norqc yaac oix daaih nyanc meih nyei orv.”

eng: The chief cupbearer, however, did not remember Joseph; he forgot him.
imn: Mv baac bouh a'ngunc diuv wuov dauh jien la'kuqv Yosepv mi'aqv. Maiv jangx zuqc ninh aqv.

eng: In the morning his mind was troubled, so he sent for all the magicians and wise men of Egypt. Pharaoh told them his dreams, but no one could interpret them for him.
imn: Da'nyeic ndorm ninh haiz hnyouv maiv henh ziouc heuc I^yipv Deic-Bung nyei mangc sou porv mbeix nyei fin-saeng caux guai-gorqv fin-saeng yietc zungv daaih. Faalo ziouc gorngv ninh nyei mbeix bun ninh mbuo muangx. Mv baac maiv maaih haaix dauh haih porv duqv mbeix cuotv.

eng: So Pharaoh sent for Joseph, and he was quickly brought from the dungeon. When he had shaved and changed his clothes, he came before Pharaoh.
imn: Faalo Hungh ziouc paaiv mienh mingh heuc Yosepv daaih. Ninh mbuo ziouc siepv-siepv nyei mingh dorh ninh cuotv. Yosepv teix siaam, japv mba'bieiv, yienc nqoi lui-houx, mingh taux Faalo Hungh nyei nza'hmien.

eng: Pharaoh said to Joseph, “I had a dream, and no one can interpret it. But I have heard it said of you that when you hear a dream you can interpret it.”
imn: Hungh diex gorngv mbuox ninh, “Yie mbeix norm mbeix yaac maiv maaih haaix dauh haih porv duqv cuotv. Yie haiz mienh gorngv meih haiz liuz mbeix ziouc haih porv mbeix nyei.”

eng: “I cannot do it,” Joseph replied to Pharaoh, “but God will give Pharaoh the answer he desires.”
imn: Yosepv dau Faalo Hungh, “Se maiv zeiz yie haih porv mbeix. Tin-Hungh ganh oix porv duqv horpc mbuox Faalo.”

eng: The plan seemed good to Pharaoh and to all his officials. So Pharaoh asked them, “Can we find anyone like this man, one in whom is the spirit of God ?”
imn: Faalo Hungh caux ninh nyei yietc zungv jien haiz Yosepv nyei za'eix longx nyei. Hungh diex gorngv mbuox ninh nyei zuangx jien, “Mbuo haih lorz duqv ganh dauh hnangv naaiv dauh maaih Tin-Hungh nyei Singx Lingh fai?”

eng: “No, my lord,” they answered. “Your servants have come to buy food. We are all the sons of one man. Your servants are honest men, not spies.”
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Ziouv aah! Gengh maiv zeiz! Yie mbuo, meih nyei bou, daaih maaiz mbiauh hnangv. Yie mbuo yietc zungv benx yietc dauh die nyei dorn. Yie mbuo yietc zungv, meih nyei bou, hnyouv zingx nyei mienh. Yie mbuo maiv zeiz daaih zaah deic-bung.”

eng: “No!” he said to them. “You have come to see where our land is unprotected.”
imn: Yosepv aengx gorngv, “Maiv zeiz aqv. Meih mbuo daaih samx mangc yie mbuo nyei deic-bung haaix norm dorngx piex hnangv.”

eng: But they replied, “Your servants were twelve brothers, the sons of one man, who lives in the land of Canaan. The youngest is now with our father, and one is no more.”
imn: Ninh mbuo gorngv, “Yie mbuo, meih nyei bou, yiem Kaa^naa^an Deic-Bung. Yietc zungv maaih ziepc nyeic muoz-dorn juangc dauh die. Youz-laai caux yie mbuo nyei die yiem biauv. Aengx maaih dauh youz daic mi'aqv.”

eng: He turned away from them and began to weep, but then came back and spoke to them again. He had Simeon taken from them and bound before their eyes.
imn: Yosepv haiz naaiv deix waac, ninh ziouc cuotv mingh nyiemv. Nyiemv liuz, ninh aengx bieqc mingh caux ninh mbuo gorngv waac yaac zorqv Si^me^on cuotv daaih dorng jienv ninh mbuo ndoh jienv aqv.

eng: Then Reuben said to his father, “You may put both of my sons to death if I do not bring him back to you. Entrust him to my care, and I will bring him back.”
imn: Lu^mben gorngv mbuox ninh nyei die, “Se gorngv yie maiv duqv dorh Mben^yaa^min nzuonx daaih bun meih, meih gunv zorqv yie nyei i dauh dorn daix guangc duqv nyei. Gunv bun yie goux Mben^yaa^min. Yie aengx dorh ninh nzuonx daaih bun meih.”

eng: Now the famine was still severe in the land. So when they had eaten all the grain they had brought from Egypt, their father said to them, “Go back and buy us a little more food.”
imn: Yiem Kaa^naa^an Deic-Bung maaih domh huaang domh ngorc kouv haic. Yaakopv nyei hmuangv doic nyanc nzengc mingh I^yipv Deic-Bung maaiz daaih nyei mbiauh mi'aqv. Yaakopv ziouc gorngv mbuox ninh nyei dorn, “Meih mbuo oix zuqc aengx mingh maaiz deix mbiauh maah!”

eng: Israel asked, “Why did you bring this trouble on me by telling the man you had another brother?”
imn: I^saa^laa^en gorngv, “Meih mbuo zoux haaix nyungc hoic yie ndongc naaiv kouv? Weic haaix diuc mbuox wuov laanh mienh meih mbuo aengx maaih dauh youz?”

eng: “It’s all right,” he said. “Don’t be afraid. Your God, the God of your father, has given you treasure in your sacks; I received your silver.” Then he brought Simeon out to them.
imn: Wuov dauh bou dau, “Maiv dungx nzauh, maiv dungx gamh nziex. Daaix meih mbuo nyei Tin-Hungh, meih mbuo nyei die nyei Tin-Hungh, zorqv naaiv deix nyaanh dapv mbuoqc bun meih mbuo. Meih mbuo maaiz daauh nzunc mbiauh nyei nyaanh yie duqv siou mi'aqv.” Gorngv liuz, ninh aengx dorh Si^me^on daaih.

eng: They replied, “Your servant our father is still alive and well.” And they bowed down, prostrating themselves before him.
imn: Ninh mbuo dau, “Meih nyei bou, yie mbuo nyei die, yiem longx nyei.” Ninh mbuo aengx ndoqv njiec baaix.

eng: After he had washed his face, he came out and, controlling himself, said, “Serve the food.”
imn: Nzaaux liuz hmien, ninh cuotv daaih nyienz jienv wuom-mueic, aengx heuc ninh nyei bou mbenc hnaangx.

eng: “Very well, then,” he said, “let it be as you say. Whoever is found to have it will become my slave; the rest of you will be free from blame.”
imn: Bou gorngv, “Horpc nyei. Ei jienv meih mbuo nyei waac zoux aqv. Mv baac lorz buatc yiem haaix dauh nyei mbuoqc, wuov dauh ganh oix zuqc zoux yie nyei nouh. Zengc njiec wuov deix maiv dorngc haaix nyungc.”

eng: But Joseph said, “Far be it from me to do such a thing! Only the man who was found to have the cup will become my slave. The rest of you, go back to your father in peace.”
imn: Yosepv gorngv, “Maiv zeiz hnangv naaic lorqc. Kungx zaanv yiem mbuoqc wuov dauh oix zuqc zoux yie nyei nouh hnangv. Zengc wuov deix gunv baengh orn nyei nzuonx meih mbuo nyei die wuov aqv.”

eng: Joseph said to his brothers, “I am Joseph! Is my father still living?” But his brothers were not able to answer him, because they were terrified at his presence.
imn: Yosepv mbuox ninh nyei muoz-doic, “Yie se Yosepv. Yie nyei die corc yiem nyei fai?” Ninh nyei muoz-doic haiz ninh hnangv naaiv nor gorngv, ninh mbuo hnyouv waaic nzengc, zungv maiv haih dau.

eng: Then he threw his arms around his brother Benjamin and wept, and Benjamin embraced him, weeping. And he kissed all his brothers and wept over them. Afterward his brothers talked with him.
imn: Yosepv qam jienv ninh nyei youz, Mben^yaa^min, nyiemv. Mben^yaa^min yaac qam jienv ninh nyei gorx nyiemv. Yosepv aengx zom ninh nyei dauh dauh gorx aengx nyiemv. Nqa'haav ninh nyei muoz-doic caux ninh gorngv waac.

eng: So Israel set out with all that was his, and when he reached Beersheba, he offered sacrifices to the God of his father Isaac. And God spoke to Israel in a vision at night and said, “Jacob! Jacob!”
imn: I^saa^laa^en dorh jienv ninh maaih nyei yietc zungv ga'naaiv cuotv jauv mingh taux Mbe^e^qe^mbaa Deic yiem wuov ziec ninh nyei die, I^sakc, nyei Tin-Hungh. Lungh muonz zanc Tin-Hungh hinc cuotv daaih heuc ninh, “Yaakopv aac, Yaakopv!”

eng: These are the names of the sons of Israel (Jacob and his descendants) who went to Egypt:
imn: Yiem naaiv mingh gorngv mingh yiem I^yipv Deic-Bung nyei I^saa^laa^en Mienh nyei mbuox. Se Yaakopv caux ninh nyei fun-faqv.

eng: Reuben the firstborn of Jacob. The sons of Reuben:
imn: Maaih Yaakopv nyei dorn-hlo, Lu^mben. Lu^mben nyei dorn se Haanokv, Banlu, Hetv^lon, caux Kaanmi.

eng: Hanok, Pallu, Hezron and Karmi. The sons of Simeon:
imn: Si^me^on nyei dorn se Ye^mu^en, Yaamin, O^haatc, Yaakin, So^haax caux Saa^un. Saa^un zoux Kaa^naa^an sieqv nyei dorn.

eng: Jemuel, Jamin, Ohad, Jakin, Zohar and Shaul the son of a Canaanite woman. The sons of Levi:
imn: Lewi nyei dorn se Geson, Ko^haatv caux Me^laa^li.

eng: Gershon, Kohath and Merari. The sons of Judah:
imn: Yu^ndaa nyei dorn se E^aa, Onan, Qelaa, Beletv caux Zelaa. (Mv baac E^aa caux Onan yiem Kaa^naa^an Deic-Bung wuov zanc guei seix mi'aqv.) Beletv nyei dorn se Hetv^lon caux Haamun.

eng: Er, Onan, Shelah, Perez and Zerah (but Er and Onan had died in the land of Canaan). The sons of Perez: Hezron and Hamul. The sons of Issachar:
imn: Itc^saax^kaa nyei dorn se Tolaa, Buwaa, I^opc caux Simlon.

eng: Tola, Puah, Jashub and Shimron. The sons of Zebulun:
imn: Se^mbu^lun nyei dorn se Seletv, Elon caux Yaa^le^en.

eng: Sered, Elon and Jahleel. These were the sons Leah bore to Jacob in Paddan Aram, besides his daughter Dinah. These sons and daughters of his were thirty-three in all.
imn: Naaiv deix se Le^aa yiem Batc^ndaan Aalam yungz bun Yaakopv nyei dorn-jueiv fun-faqv. Aengx maaih dauh sieqv, mbuox heuc Ndinaa. Le^aa nyei buonc dorn-jueiv fun-faqv yietc zungv maaih faah ziepc faam dauh.

eng: The sons of Gad:
imn: Gaatc nyei dorn se Si^fi^yon, Hakv^gi, Suni, Etc^mbon, E^le^i, Aa^lo^ndi caux Aa^le^li.

eng: Zephon, Haggi, Shuni, Ezbon, Eri, Arodi and Areli. The sons of Asher:
imn: Aa^se nyei dorn se Imnaa, Itc^waa, Itc^wi caux Mbe^li^yaa. Naaiv deix dorn nyei muoc heuc Se^laa^aa.

eng: The sons of Jacob’s wife Rachel:
imn: Yaakopv nyei auv, Laasen, nyei dorn se Yosepv caux Mben^yaa^min. Yosepv yiem I^yipv Deic-Bung duqv Maa^natv^se caux E^faa^im, se On Zingh nyei sai mienh, Bo^ti^fe^laa, nyei sieqv, Aa^se^natv, yungz bun ninh.

eng: The sons of Benjamin:
imn: Mben^yaa^min nyei dorn se Mbelaa, Mbeke, Atc^mben, Gelaa, Naa^aa^maan, E^hai, Lo^atc, Mapv^bim, Hupc^bim caux Aatc.

eng: Bela, Beker, Ashbel, Gera, Naaman, Ehi, Rosh, Muppim, Huppim and Ard. These were the sons of Rachel who were born to Jacob—fourteen in all.
imn: Naaiv deix se Laasen yungz bun Yaakopv nyei dorn-jueiv fun-faqv. Yietc zungv maaih ziepc feix dauh.

eng: The son of Dan:
imn: Ndaan nyei dorn se Husim.

eng: Hushim. The sons of Naphtali:
imn: Napv^taa^li nyei dorn se Yaa^si^en, Guni, Yese caux Sinlem.

eng: Jahziel, Guni, Jezer and Shillem. These were the sons born to Jacob by Bilhah, whom Laban had given to his daughter Rachel—seven in all.
imn: Naaiv deix se Laa^mbaan bun ninh nyei sieqv, Laasen, wuov dauh nouh beiz, Mbin^haa, yungz bun Yaakopv nyei dorn-jueiv fun-faqv. Yietc zungv maaih siec dauh.

eng: Israel said to Joseph, “Now I am ready to die, since I have seen for myself that you are still alive.”
imn: I^saa^laa^en gorngv mbuox Yosepv, “Ih zanc yie duqv buatc liuz meih, hiuv duqv meih corc ziangh jienv nyei, yie daic yaac hnyouv nqaai aqv.”

eng: Pharaoh asked the brothers, “What is your occupation?”
imn: Faalo Hungh naaic Yosepv nyei gorx mbuo, “Meih mbuo zoux haaix nyungc gong nyanc nyei mienh?”

eng: Then Joseph brought his father Jacob in and presented him before Pharaoh. After Jacob blessed Pharaoh, Pharaoh asked him, “How old are you?”
imn: Yosepv aengx dorh ninh nyei die, Yaakopv, mingh wuic buangh Faalo Hungh. Yaakopv yaac buang waac Faalo. Hungh diex naaic Yaakopv, “Meih nyei hnyangx-jeiv duqv mbu'ziex hnyangx aqv?”

eng: “You have saved our lives,” they said. “May we find favor in the eyes of our lord; we will be in bondage to Pharaoh.”
imn: Baeqc fingx gorngv, “Meih njoux cuotv yie mbuo nyei maengc. Tov bun yie mbuo yiem meih nyei nza'hmien duqv longx, yie mbuo ziouc laengz zoux Faalo Hungh nyei nouh.”

eng: So Joseph established it as a law concerning land in Egypt—still in force today—that a fifth of the produce belongs to Pharaoh. It was only the land of the priests that did not become Pharaoh’s.
imn: Yosepv liepc diuh leiz daaih, corc longc jienv taux ih jaax hnoi, se I^yipv Deic-Bung nyei gaeng-zuangx ga'naaiv biaa gouv oix zuqc bun gouv hungh diex. Kungx maaih sai mienh nyei ndau hnangv maiv benx hungh diex nyei buonc.

eng: Now the Israelites settled in Egypt in the region of Goshen. They acquired property there and were fruitful and increased greatly in number.
imn: I^saa^laa^en Mienh yiem I^yipv Deic-Bung, Gosen Deic, duqv ninh mbuo ganh nyei ndau yaac hiaangx daaih maaih fu'jueiv camv.

eng: “I will do as you say,” he said. “Swear to me,” he said. Then Joseph swore to him, and Israel worshiped as he leaned on the top of his staff.
imn: Yosepv dau, “Yie zungv oix ei jienv die nyei waac zoux.” Yaakopv gorngv, “Yie oix meih laengz ngaengc waac meih za'gengh zoux.” Yosepv ziouc laengz jiez ngaengc waac. Laengz liuz, Yaakopv yiem wuov coux-daauh ndoqv njiec baaix Tin-Hungh.

eng: When Israel saw the sons of Joseph, he asked, “Who are these?”
imn: I^saa^laa^en buatc Yosepv nyei dorn mbuo, ninh naaic gaax, “Naaiv haaix dauh?”

eng: “They are the sons God has given me here,” Joseph said to his father.
imn: Yosepv mbuox ninh nyei die, “Naaiv se Tin-Hungh bun yie yiem naaiv duqv daaih nyei dorn.”

eng: Israel said to Joseph, “I never expected to see your face again, and now God has allowed me to see your children too.”
imn: I^saa^laa^en gorngv mbuox Yosepv, “Yie hnamv daaih yietc liuz maiv duqv buatc meih, mv baac Tin-Hungh bun yie liemh meih nyei dorn duqv buatc.”

eng: Then he blessed Joseph and said,
imn: Ninh ziouc buang waac Yosepv aqv. Ninh gorngv,

eng: “May the God before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac walked faithfully, the God who has been my shepherd all my life to this day, the Angel who has delivered me from all harm
imn: “Tov yie nyei ong, Apc^laa^ham, caux yie nyei die, I^sakc, buoqc zaangc nyei Tin-Hungh ceix fuqv naaiv deix i dauh dorn. Tov yietc seix dorh yie taux ih hnoi nyei Tin-Hungh ceix fuqv bun ninh mbuo. Tov njoux yie leih ndutv yietc zungv haih hoic yie nyei zeqc naanc wuov dauh fin-mienh

eng: Then Jacob called for his sons and said: “Gather around so I can tell you what will happen to you in days to come. “Assemble and listen, sons of Jacob;
imn: Yaakopv heuc ninh nyei dorn mbuo daaih nzoih. Ninh gorngv, “Meih mbuo daaih gapv zunv yie naaiv. Yie oix gorngv nqa'haav hingv meih mbuo oix buangh nyei sic. “Yaakopv nyei dorn aac, meih mbuo daaih zunv muangx.

eng: listen to your father Israel. “Reuben, you are my firstborn,
imn: Daaih muangx meih nyei die, I^saa^laa^en, gorngv. “Lu^mben aac, meih zoux yie nyei dorn-hlo.

eng: my might, the first sign of my strength, excelling in honor, excelling in power. Turbulent as the waters, you will no longer excel,
imn: Meih se yie nyei qaqv. Yie benx domh mienh duqv daaih nyei daauh dauh dorn. Meih zoux domh gorx, yietc buoqv dorn meih gauh maaih mengh jiex, meih yaac gauh henv. Meih beiv hnangv wuom-seix nor, ziouc maiv duqv zoux hlo,

eng: for you went up onto your father’s bed, onto my couch and defiled it. “Simeon and Levi are brothers—
imn: weic zuqc meih bueix meih nyei die nyei auv-dorn, bun die nyei coux maaih uix. “Si^me^on caux Lewi zoux i muoz.

eng: their swords are weapons of violence. Let me not enter their council,
imn: Ninh mbuo nyei nzuqc ndaauv se zoux doqc nyei wuoqc ginc. Ninh mbuo daav za'eix, yie maiv caux ninh mbuo caangh laangh.

eng: let me not join their assembly, for they have killed men in their anger and hamstrung oxen as they pleased. Cursed be their anger, so fierce,
imn: Ninh mbuo gapv zunv nyei ziangh hoc, yie maiv caux ninh mbuo zoux doic, weic zuqc ninh mbuo qiex jiez daix mienh. Ninh mbuo sueih eix hngaqv ngongh gouv nyei domh luangc jaan dangx. Laaix ninh mbuo qiex jiez, ciouv, ninh mbuo zuqc houv.

eng: and their fury, so cruel! I will scatter them in Jacob and disperse them in Israel. “Judah, your brothers will praise you;
imn: Laaix qiex beqv, hnyouv doqc, ninh mbuo zuqc zioux. Yie oix bun ninh mbuo nzaanx mingh gormx I^saa^laa^en Deic-Bung, yaac oix bun ninh mbuo zorpc jienv I^saa^laa^en Mienh yiem. “Yu^ndaa aac, meih nyei muoz-doic oix ceng meih.

eng: your hand will be on the neck of your enemies; your father’s sons will bow down to you. You are a lion’s cub, Judah;
imn: Meih longc meih nyei buoz nanv jienv meih nyei win-wangv nyei jaang. Meih nyei muoz-doic oix gueic njiec ndau baaix meih. Yu^ndaa aac, meih se hnangv dauh sienh dorn nor.

eng: you return from the prey, my son. Like a lion he crouches and lies down, like a lioness—who dares to rouse him? The scepter will not depart from Judah,
imn: Yie nyei dorn aac, meih cuotv mingh ngaatc orv aengx nzuonx meih nyei dorngx. Meih mbaapv njiec aengx bueix njiec hnangv sienh gouv. Hnangv sienh nyeiz nor, haaix dauh haih gaamv haeqv ninh nyie? Yu^ndaa oix yietc liuz nanv jienv hungh diex nyei biaav.

eng: nor the ruler’s staff from between his feet, until he to whom it belongs shall come and the obedience of the nations shall be his. He will tether his donkey to a vine,
imn: Ninh gunv mienh nyei biaav-mbiaac zungv maiv leih ninh nyei i jieqv zaux mbu'ndongx, zuov taux wuov diuh biaav-mbiaac nyei ziouv daaih taux. Maanc fingx mienh yaac muangx ninh nyei waac. Ninh nyei lorh gouv ninh ndoh jienv a'ngunc hmei,

eng: his colt to the choicest branch; he will wash his garments in wine, his robes in the blood of grapes. His eyes will be darker than wine,
imn: lorh dorn ndoh jienv gauh longx jiex nyei a'ngunc hmei nquaah. Ninh oix longc a'ngunc diuv nzox ninh nyei lui-houx, longc a'ngunc wuom siqv nyei hnangv nziaamv nor, nzox ninh nyei lui-ndaauv. Weic hopv a'ngunc diuv, ninh nyei m'zing siqv,

eng: his teeth whiter than milk. “Zebulun will live by the seashore
imn: weic ninh hopv ngongh nyorx, ninh nyei nyaah yaac baeqc. “Se^mbu^lun duqv yiem wuov koiv-hlen,

eng: and become a haven for ships; his border will extend toward Sidon. “Issachar is a rawboned donkey
imn: benx norm nzangv-bouc bun nzangv dingh. Ninh nyei gapv-jaaix mingh daaux jienv Sai^ndorn Zingh. “Itc^saax^kaa hnangv lorh nor henv,

eng: lying down among the sheep pens. When he sees how good is his resting place
imn: mbaapv njiec ninh nyei ba'gi yungh laanh mbu'ndongx. Ninh buatc ninh hitv nyei dorngx longx,

eng: and how pleasant is his land, he will bend his shoulder to the burden and submit to forced labor. “Dan will provide justice for his people
imn: wuov norm deic-bung kuh yiem, ninh ziouc nyiex jienv ga'naaiv jutv-jutv nyei mingh, laengz zoux nouh bun mienh aapv zoux gong kouv. “Ndaan oix baengh fim nyei siemv ninh nyei baeqc fingx.

eng: as one of the tribes of Israel. Dan will be a snake by the roadside,
imn: Ninh nyei fingx se yietc fingx I^saa^laa^en Mienh. Ndaan oix benx diuh naang yiem wuov jauv,

eng: a viper along the path, that bites the horse’s heels so that its rider tumbles backward. “I look for your deliverance, Lord .
imn: benx diuh naang-doqc yiem wuov jauv-hlen, nyorqv maaz nyei zaux, hoic geh maaz nyei mienh da'nziaaux faan, ndortv ga'ndiev ndau mingh. “ Ziouv aac, yie zuov jienv meih njoux yie.

eng: “Gad will be attacked by a band of raiders,
imn: “Gaatc oix zuqc guanh janx-zaqc caangv,

eng: but he will attack them at their heels. “Asher’s food will be rich;
imn: mv baac ninh daaux sin zunc jienv nitv jienv zaux-nqo mingh. “Aa^se nyei deic-bung bungx-zuoqc, ga'naaiv-zuangx longx,

eng: he will provide delicacies fit for a king. “Naphtali is a doe set free
imn: haih zoux duqv puix hungh diex nyanc. “Napv^taa^li benx dauh njaih nyeiz bungx wuov lomc,

eng: that bears beautiful fawns. “Joseph is a fruitful vine,
imn: ninh njiec duqv dorn nzueic nyei. “Yosepv se ziangh biouv camv nyei ndiangx-nquaah,

eng: a fruitful vine near a spring, whose branches climb over a wall. With bitterness archers attacked him;
imn: se yiem wuom-zingv hlen ziangh biouv gitv nyei nquaah. Ninh nyei nquaah yaac yormc cuotv ga'nyiec ziqc laatc. Buonv juang-zinx nyei mienh daaih caux ninh mborqv jaax zoux doqc,

eng: they shot at him with hostility. But his bow remained steady,
imn: buonv ninh, zoux hoic ninh. Mv baac ninh zaangv dingc ninh nyei juang-zinx.

eng: his strong arms stayed limber, because of the hand of the Mighty One of Jacob, because of the Shepherd, the Rock of Israel, because of your father’s God, who helps you,
imn: Ninh nyei buoz yaac maaih qaqv henv weic Yaakopv nyei Nernh Jiex nyei Tin-Hungh, weic Goux Ba'gi Yungh nyei Mienh, se I^saa^laa^en nyei La'bieiv . Weic meih nyei die nyei Tin-Hungh, dongh tengx meih wuov dauh,

eng: because of the Almighty, who blesses you with blessings of the skies above, blessings of the deep springs below, blessings of the breast and womb. Your father’s blessings are greater
imn: yaac weic Nernh Jiex nyei Tin-Hungh ceix fuqv bun meih. Ninh bun gu'nguaaic nyei fuqv, ga'ndiev ndau nyei fuqv, caux maaih saeng-kuv caux duqv fu'jueiv nyei fuqv. Meih nyei die bun meih duqv nyei fuqv

eng: than the blessings of the ancient mountains, than the bounty of the age-old hills. Let all these rest on the head of Joseph, on the brow of the prince among his brothers. “Benjamin is a ravenous wolf;
imn: se gauh hlo jiex yietc liuz yiem nyei mbong duqv daaih nyei fuqv. Se gauh hlo jiex yie nyei ong-taaix bun daaih nyei fuqv. Tov naaiv deix fuqv yiem Yosepv nyei m'nqorngv, yaac yiem zinh ndaangc leih ninh nyei muoz-doic wuov dauh nyei biorngh. “Mben^yaa^min hnangv dauh hieh juv cei orv,

eng: When Jacob had finished giving instructions to his sons, he drew his feet up into the bed, breathed his last and was gathered to his people.
imn: Yaakopv paaiv waac mbuox liuz ninh nyei dorn mbuo, ninh bueix njiec coux ziouc dangx qiex, guei seix mi'aqv.

eng: Pharaoh said, “Go up and bury your father, as he made you swear to do.”
imn: Faalo Hungh dau, “Gunv dorh meih nyei die mingh zangx ziux meih caux ninh laengz jiez nyei waac.”

eng: His brothers then came and threw themselves down before him. “We are your slaves,” they said.
imn: Ninh nyei gorx mbuo ziouc mingh taux Yosepv nyei nza'hmien gueic njiec ndau baaix aengx gorngv, “Yie mbuo yiem meih nyei nza'hmien tov zoux meih nyei nouh.”

eng: So Joseph died at the age of a hundred and ten. And after they embalmed him, he was placed in a coffin in Egypt.
imn: Yosepv ziangh yietc baeqv ziepc hnyangx yiem wuov I^yipv Deic-Bung guei seix mi'aqv. Ninh mbuo zorqv sei ziemx jienv ndie-wuom bun maiv haih huv, maiv haih zueix, yiem wuov I^yipv Deic-Bung dapv jienv benv aqv.

eng: This is the genealogy of Jesus the Messiah the son of David, the son of Abraham: Abraham was the father of Isaac,
imn: Yesu Giduc zoux Ndaawitv Hungh nyei zeiv-fun. Ndaawitv Hungh yaac zoux Apc^laa^ham nyei zeiv-fun. Ih zanc fiev naaiv se Yesu Giduc doic jiex doic nyei douh zong. Apc^laa^ham zoux I^sakc nyei die.

eng: Isaac the father of Jacob, Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers, Judah the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar,
imn: I^sakc zoux Yaakopv nyei die. Yaakopv zoux Yu^ndaa mbuo muoz-dorn doic nyei die. Yu^ndaa zoux Beletv caux Zelaa nyei die. Ninh mbuo nyei maa heuc Taamaa.

eng: Perez the father of Hezron, Hezron the father of Ram, Ram the father of Amminadab,
imn: Beletv zoux Hetv^lon nyei die. Hetv^lon zoux Laam nyei die. Laam zoux Am^mi^naa^ndapc nyei die.

eng: Amminadab the father of Nahshon, Nahshon the father of Salmon, Salmon the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab,
imn: Am^mi^naa^ndapc zoux Naason nyei die. Naason zoux Sanx^mon nyei die. Sanx^mon zoux Mbo^aatc nyei die. Mbo^aatc nyei maa heuc Laa^hapc.

eng: Boaz the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth, Obed the father of Jesse, and Jesse the father of King David.
imn: Mbo^aatc zoux O^mbetc nyei die. O^mbetc nyei maa heuc Lu^te. O^mbetc zoux Jetc^si nyei die. Jetc^si zoux Ndaawitv Hungh nyei die.

eng: David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been Uriah’s wife, Solomon the father of Rehoboam,
imn: Ndaawitv Hungh zoux Saa^lo^morn nyei die. Saa^lo^morn nyei maa zinh ndaangc zoux U^li^yaa nyei auv. Saa^lo^morn zoux Le^hox^mbo^am nyei die.

eng: Rehoboam the father of Abijah, Abijah the father of Asa, Asa the father of Jehoshaphat,
imn: Le^hox^mbo^am zoux Aa^mbi^yaa nyei die. Aa^mbi^yaa zoux Aasaax nyei die. Aasaax zoux Ye^ho^saa^fatv nyei die.

eng: Jehoshaphat the father of Jehoram, Jehoram the father of Uzziah, Uzziah the father of Jotham,
imn: Ye^ho^saa^fatv zoux Yolam nyei die. Yolam zoux Utc^si^yaa nyei die. Utc^si^yaa zoux Yotaam nyei die.

eng: Jotham the father of Ahaz, Ahaz the father of Hezekiah, Hezekiah the father of Manasseh,
imn: Yotaam zoux Aa^hatc nyei die. Aa^hatc zoux He^se^ki^yaa nyei die. He^se^ki^yaa zoux Maa^natv^se nyei die.

eng: Manasseh the father of Amon, Amon the father of Josiah, and Josiah the father of Jeconiah and his brothers at the time of the exile to Babylon.
imn: Maa^natv^se zoux Aamon nyei die. Aamon zoux Yo^si^yaa nyei die. Yo^si^yaa zoux Ye^ko^ni^yaa mbuo muoz-doic nyei die.

eng: After the exile to Babylon:
imn: Wuov zanc doix zuqc I^saa^laa^en Mienh zuqc caangv mingh Mbaa^mbi^lon Deic-Bung. Zuqc caangv mingh liuz, nqa'haav Ye^ko^ni^yaa zoux Se^an^ti^en nyei die.

eng: Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel, Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel, Zerubbabel the father of Abihud,
imn: Se^an^ti^en zoux Se^lupv^mbaa^mben nyei die. Se^lupv^mbaa^mben zoux Aa^mbi^yutv nyei die.

eng: Abihud the father of Eliakim, Eliakim the father of Azor, Azor the father of Zadok,
imn: Aa^mbi^yutv zoux E^li^yaa^kim nyei die. E^li^yaa^kim zoux Aasor nyei die. Aasor zoux Saax^ndokc nyei die.

eng: Zadok the father of Akim, Akim the father of Elihud, Elihud the father of Eleazar,
imn: Saax^ndokc zoux Aakim nyei die. Aakim zoux E^li^utc nyei die. E^li^utc zoux E^le^aa^saa nyei die.

eng: Eleazar the father of Matthan, Matthan the father of Jacob, and Jacob the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, and Mary was the mother of Jesus who is called the Messiah.
imn: E^le^aa^saa zoux Matv^taan nyei die. Matv^taan zoux Yaakopv nyei die. Yaakopv zoux Yosepv nyei die. Yosepv se Maa^li^yaa nyei nqox. Maa^li^yaa zoux Yesu nyei maa. Yesu dongh mienh heuc Giduc wuov dauh.

eng: Thus there were fourteen generations in all from Abraham to David, fourteen from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen from the exile to the Messiah.
imn: Hnangv naaic, yiem Apc^laa^ham mingh taux Ndaawitv Hungh maaih ziepc feix doic mienh. Aengx yiem Ndaawitv Hungh mingh taux I^saa^laa^en Mienh zuqc guaatv mingh Mbaa^mbi^lon Deic-Bung maaih ziepc feix doic. Aengx yiem zuqc guaatv mingh nyei ziangh hoc taux Giduc cuotv seix wuov zanc, maaih ziepc feix doic.

eng: All this took place to fulfill what the Lord had said through the prophet: “The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and they will call him Immanuel” (which means “God with us”).
imn: Naaiv deix sic cuotv daaih weic bun doix nzengc zinh ndaangc Ziouv longc wuov dauh douc waac mienh gorngv nyei waac. Ninh gorngv,

eng: “ ‘But you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah,
imn: ‘Yu^ndaa Deic-Bung, Mbetc^le^hem Mungv aah!

eng: “A voice is heard in Ramah,
imn: “Haiz mienh yiem wuov Laamaa Zingh

eng: In those days John the Baptist came, preaching in the wilderness of Judea and saying, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.” This is he who was spoken of through the prophet Isaiah:
imn: Ziangh hoc taux, tengx mienh jiex wuom nyei leiz wuov dauh Yo^han mingh wuov Yu^ndie Deic-Bung-Huaang zunh doz. Ninh gorngv, “Oix zuqc goiv hnyouv guangc meih mbuo nyei zuiz, weic zuqc Tin-Hungh daaih zoux Ziouv gunv mienh nyei ziangh hoc taux fatv aqv.” Douc waac mienh, I^saa^yaa, gorngv nyei, se dongh naaiv dauh Yo^han aqv. I^saa^yaa gorngv,

eng: Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to be baptized by John. But John tried to deter him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and do you come to me?”
imn: Wuov zanc Yesu yiem Gaa^li^li mingh Jor^ndaen Ndaaih, oix Yo^han tengx ninh jiex wuom nyei leiz. Mv baac Yo^han oix Yesu goiv za'eix, ninh ziouc gorngv, “Horpc zuqc meih tengx yie jiex leiz, mv baac meih daaih lorz yie?”

eng: Jesus replied, “Let it be so now; it is proper for us to do this to fulfill all righteousness.” Then John consented.
imn: Yesu dau Yo^han, “Meih gunv tengx yie aqv. Mbuo hnangv naaiv zoux, se zoux duqv horpc nzengc ei Tin-Hungh oix mbuo zoux nyei.” Yo^han ziouc tengx aqv.

eng: Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’ ”
imn: Yesu dau, “Ging-Sou fiev jienv gorngv,

eng: Then the devil took him to the holy city and had him stand on the highest point of the temple. “If you are the Son of God,” he said, “throw yourself down. For it is written:
imn: ‘Mienh maiv zeiz kungx kaux nyanc hopv yungz maengc hnangv, mv baac oix zuqc kaux Tin-Hungh gorngv nyei joux-joux waac.’+” Mienv nyei hungh ziouc dorh Yesu mingh cing-nzengc nyei zingh, Ye^lu^saa^lem, bun ninh yiem zaangc Tin-Hungh nyei biauv hlang jiex nyei dorngx, gorngv mbuox ninh, “Meih za'gengh Tin-Hungh nyei Dorn nor, biu njiec gaax. Weic zuqc Ging-Sou fiev jienv gorngv,

eng: Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendor. “All this I will give you,” he said, “if you will bow down and worship me.”
imn: ‘Maiv dungx seix Ziouv, meih nyei Tin-Hungh.’+” Mienv nyei hungh aengx dorh Yesu faaux norm hlang haic nyei mbong, bun ninh mangc lungh ndiev maanc guoqv yaac mangc maanc guoqv nyei njang-laangc. Mienv nyei hungh gorngv, “Se gorngv meih gueic njiec baaix yie, yie ziouc zorqv naaiv deix yietc zungv bun meih.”

eng: Jesus said to him, “Away from me, Satan! For it is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.’ ”
imn: Yesu ziouc dau, “Saadaan aah! Biaux nqoi maah! Ging-Sou fiev jienv gorngv,

eng: Then the devil left him, and angels came and attended him.
imn: ‘Oix zuqc baaix Ziouv, meih nyei Tin-Hungh. Kungx oix zuqc fu-sux ninh nduqc dauh hnangv.’+” Mienv nyei hungh ziang naaic leih nqoi Yesu mi'aqv. Fin-mienh ziouc daaih liuc leiz Yesu.

eng: the Way of the Sea, beyond the Jordan, Galilee of the Gentiles— the people living in darkness
imn: “Se^mbu^lun caux Napv^taa^li Deic, yiem wuov ngaanc ziqc Jor^ndaen Ndaaih, dongh mingh koiv wuov diuh jauv, se Janx yiem nyei deic-bung, Gaa^li^li. Yiem hmuangx gu'nyuoz nyei baeqc fingx,

eng: have seen a great light; on those living in the land of the shadow of death a light has dawned.” From that time on Jesus began to preach, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.”
imn: zungv duqv buatc domh njang. Yiem zuqc daic nyei dongh linh gu'nyuoz wuov deix, maaih njang cuotv daaih ziux jienv ninh mbuo.” Yangh naaiv mingh Yesu jiez gorn zunh doz. Ninh gorngv, “Oix zuqc goiv hnyouv guangc zuiz. Tin-Hungh daaih zoux Ziouv gunv mienh nyei hnoi taux fatv aqv.”

eng: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on a mountainside and sat down. His disciples came to him, and he began to teach them.
imn: Yesu buatc mienh ndongc naaic camv, ninh ziouc faaux norm mbong mingh zueiz jienv. Ninh nyei sai-gorx mbuo yaac daaih yiem zunv. Yesu ziouc jiez gorn njaaux ninh mbuo. Ninh gorngv,

eng: He said: “Blessed are the poor in spirit,
imn: “Ganh hiuv duqv ganh qiemx zuqc Tin-Hungh wuov deix, maaih njien-youh aqv,

eng: for theirs is the kingdom of heaven. Blessed are those who mourn,
imn: weic zuqc maaih Tin-Hungh zoux Ziouv gunv ninh mbuo. Maaih you-nzauh wuov deix, maaih njien-youh aqv,

eng: for they will be comforted. Blessed are the meek,
imn: weic zuqc Tin-Hungh oix tengx jaiv you-nzauh. Hnyouv suonc wuov deix, maaih njien-youh aqv,

eng: for they will inherit the earth. Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness,
imn: weic zuqc Tin-Hungh laengz bun nyei, ninh mbuo oix duqv zipv nzengc. Ngorc oix zoux horpc Tin-Hungh nyei hnyouv wuov deix, maaih njien-youh aqv,

eng: for they will be filled. Blessed are the merciful,
imn: weic zuqc Tin-Hungh oix bun ninh mbuo duqv buangv eix. Korv-lienh mienh wuov deix, maaih njien-youh aqv,

eng: for they will be shown mercy. Blessed are the pure in heart,
imn: weic zuqc Tin-Hungh oix korv-lienh ninh mbuo. Maaih cing-nzengc nyei hnyouv wuov deix, maaih njien-youh aqv,

eng: for they will see God. Blessed are the peacemakers,
imn: weic zuqc ninh mbuo oix duqv buatc Tin-Hungh. Kuinx i bung mienh horpc hnyouv daaih maaih baengh orn wuov deix, maaih njien-youh aqv,

eng: for they will be called children of God. Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness,
imn: weic zuqc Tin-Hungh oix nyiemc ninh mbuo zoux ninh nyei naamh nyouz. Weic zoux ei Tin-Hungh nyei eix zuqc mienh zoux doqc wuov deix, maaih njien-youh aqv,

eng: “You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt loses its saltiness, how can it be made salty again? It is no longer good for anything, except to be thrown out and trampled underfoot.
imn: “Meih mbuo se hnangv nzauv weic yiem baamh mienh mbu'ndongx. Mv baac se gorngv nzauv nyei mueix tuix zaamv mingh, hnangv haaix nor noic duqv aengx nzaaih daaih? Naaiv deix nzauv maiv lamh longc aqv. Oix zuqc dox guangc bun mienh caaiv hnangv.

eng: “This, then, is how you should pray:
imn: “Meih mbuo horpc zuqc hnangv naaiv nor daux gaux.

eng: “ ‘Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name, your kingdom come,
imn: ‘Yie mbuo nyei tin-dorngh Diex aah! Tov bun mienh taaih meih nyei cing-nzengc mbuox. Tov bun meih zoux Ziouv gunv mienh nyei hnoi taux.

eng: your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven. Give us today our daily bread.
imn: Tov bun yiem naaiv baamh gen nyungc-nyungc ei meih nyei eix cuotv daaih, hnangv yiem tin-dorngh nyungc-nyungc ei meih nyei eix cuotv daaih nor. Yie mbuo hnoi-hnoi qiemx zuqc nyei nyanc hopv, tov bun gaux ih hnoi nyanc.

eng: And forgive us our debts,
imn: Tov guangc yie mbuo zoux nyei zuiz,

eng: as we also have forgiven our debtors. And lead us not into temptation,
imn: hnangv yie mbuo guangc zoux dorngc yie mbuo wuov deix mienh nyei zuiz nor. Maiv dungx bun yie mbuo buangh seix hnyouv nyei sic,

eng: “No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.
imn: “Maiv maaih haaix dauh haih zoux duqv i dauh ziouv nyei bou. Hnangv naaic, ninh oix nzorng naaiv dauh, oix hnamv wuov dauh. Maiv zei aeqv, ninh oix taaih naaiv dauh, oix mangc piex wuov dauh. Meih mbuo maiv haih dongh dangh zoux Tin-Hungh nyei bou yaac zoux Nyaanh nyei bou.

eng: “Do not judge, or you too will be judged. For in the same way you judge others, you will be judged, and with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.
imn: “Maiv dungx dingc mienh nyei zuiz, Tin-Hungh ziouc maiv dingc meih mbuo nyei zuiz, weic zuqc meih mbuo hnangv haaix nor dingc mienh nyei zuiz, Tin-Hungh yaac hnangv wuov nor dingc meih mbuo nyei zuiz aqv. Meih mbuo longc haaix nyungc hlaau bun mienh, Tin-Hungh yaac longc wuov nyungc hlaau bun meih mbuo.

eng: “Do not give dogs what is sacred; do not throw your pearls to pigs. If you do, they may trample them under their feet, and turn and tear you to pieces.
imn: “Maiv dungx zorqv cing-nzengc nyei ga'naaiv bun juv. Gamh nziex juv haih daaux sin daaih ngaatc meih mbuo. Yaac maiv dungx zorqv jaaix nyei la'bieiv-zou zoi bun dungz. Dungz kungx oix caaiv waaic hnangv.

eng: When Jesus had finished saying these things, the crowds were amazed at his teaching, because he taught as one who had authority, and not as their teachers of the law.
imn: Yesu gorngv liuz naaiv deix waac, zuangx mienh mbuoqc horngh haic ninh njaaux nyei waac, weic zuqc ninh njaaux maiv hnangv ninh mbuo nyei njaaux leiz fin-saeng. Ninh nyei waac maaih hatc maaz haic.

eng: When Jesus came down from the mountainside, large crowds followed him. A man with leprosy came and knelt before him and said, “Lord, if you are willing, you can make me clean.”
imn: Yesu yiem mbong njiec daaih wuov zanc, maaih mienh camv! nyei gan jienv ninh mingh. Maaih laanh mienh butv gomh huv baengc daaih gueic njiec Yesu nyei nza'hmien. Ninh gorngv, “Domh Gorx aac, se gorngv meih oix nor, meih haih zorc longx yie nyei baengc.”

eng: When Jesus had entered Capernaum, a centurion came to him, asking for help. “Lord,” he said, “my servant lies at home paralyzed, suffering terribly.”
imn: Yesu bieqc Kaa^be^naa^um Mungv wuov zanc, maaih dauh Lomaa baeng-bieiv daaih lorz ninh, tov ninh tengx. Baeng-bieiv gorngv, “Domh Gorx aac, yie nyei bou butv baengc-mbai, bueix wuov biauv, diev duqv kouv haic.”

eng: Jesus said to him, “Shall I come and heal him?”
imn: Yesu gorngv, “Yie oix mingh zorc ninh longx.”

eng: When Jesus came into Peter’s house, he saw Peter’s mother-in-law lying in bed with a fever. He touched her hand and the fever left her, and she got up and began to wait on him.
imn: Yesu bieqc Bide nyei biauv, buatc Bide nyei maa-diev butv baengc, sin jorm nyei, bueix jienv wuov coux. Yesu nanv ninh nyei buoz, sin ziouc laangh mi'aqv. Ninh ziouc jiez sin daaih fu-sux Yesu.

eng: When evening came, many who were demon-possessed were brought to him, and he drove out the spirits with a word and healed all the sick. This was to fulfill what was spoken through the prophet Isaiah:
imn: Lungh maanz-hmuangx, maaih mienh dorh zuqc mienv muoqv nyei mienh camv nyei daaih lorz Yesu. Yesu gorngv nduqc joux waac hnangv ziouc zunc mienv cuotv nzengc mi'aqv. Dauh dauh nyei baengc yaac zorc longx nzengc. Ninh hnangv naaiv zoux weic bun ninh zoux nyei sic cuotv daaih doix douc waac mienh, I^saa^yaa, gorngv nyei waac. I^saa^yaa gorngv,

eng: Jesus replied, “Foxes have dens and birds have nests, but the Son of Man has no place to lay his head.”
imn: Yesu gorngv mbuox ninh, “Hieh juv maaih mbaengx-kuotv yiem, norqc yaac maaih lauz, mv baac yie, Baamh Mienh nyei Dorn, maiv maaih dorngx bueix hitv kuonx.”

eng: Another disciple said to him, “Lord, first let me go and bury my father.”
imn: Aengx maaih dauh sai-gorx mbuox Yesu, “Domh Gorx aac, yie tov nzuonx mingh biopv yie nyei die ndaangc.”

eng: But Jesus told him, “Follow me, and let the dead bury their own dead.”
imn: Yesu dau, “Sueih janx-daic ganh biopv ninh mbuo ganh nyei janx-daic aqv. Gan jienv yie daaih maah!”

eng: He replied, “You of little faith, why are you so afraid?” Then he got up and rebuked the winds and the waves, and it was completely calm.
imn: Yesu dau, “Meih mbuo weic haaix diuc ndongc naaic gamh nziex? Meih mbuo sienx duqv hei haic weqv!” Yesu ziouc jiez sin daaih hatc nziaaux caux wuom-laangc. Domh nziaaux ziouc dingh mi'aqv. Wuom-laangc yaac haapv. Haaix nyungc yaac sekv nzengc mi'aqv.

eng: The men were amazed and asked, “What kind of man is this? Even the winds and the waves obey him!”
imn: Sai-gorx mbuo mbuoqc horngh haic yaac gorngv, “Naaiv haaix nyungc mienh orqv? Liemh nziaaux caux wuom-laangc yaac muangx ninh nyei waac.”

eng: Some distance from them a large herd of pigs was feeding. The demons begged Jesus, “If you drive us out, send us into the herd of pigs.”
imn: Maaih yietc domh guanh dungz leih wuov go deix nyei lorz nyanc. Naaiv deix mienv ziouc tov Yesu, “Meih zunc yie mbuo cuotv nor, bun yie mbuo mingh bieqc wuov guanh dungz maah!”

eng: At this, some of the teachers of the law said to themselves, “This fellow is blaspheming!”
imn: Maaih deix njaaux leiz nyei fin-saeng haiz Yesu hnangv naaic nor gorngv, ninh mbuo yiem hnyouv hnamv, “Aav, naaiv laanh mienh ki Tin-Hungh.”

eng: As Jesus went on from there, he saw a man named Matthew sitting at the tax collector’s booth. “Follow me,” he told him, and Matthew got up and followed him.
imn: Yesu cuotv wuov norm dorngx mi'aqv. Gan jienv jauv mingh gau, buatc dauh siou nzou-zinh nyei mienh, mbuox heuc Matv^taai, zueiz jienv wuov siou nzou zaamc. Yesu gorngv mbuox ninh, “Daaih gan yie oc.” Matv^taai ziouc jiez sin gan jienv Yesu mingh aqv.

eng: Then John’s disciples came and asked him, “How is it that we and the Pharisees fast often, but your disciples do not fast?”
imn: ‘Yie oix mienh maaih hnyouv korv-lienh mienh. Maiv zeiz oix mienh dorh saeng-kuv daaih ziec yie.’ Yie maiv zeiz daaih weic heuc kuv mienh. Yie daaih heuc mengh dauh waaic nyei mienh.” Wuov zanc tengx mienh jiex wuom nyei leiz wuov dauh Yo^han nyei sai-gorx daaih naaic Yesu, “Yie mbuo caux Faa^li^si Mienh zei maiv nyanc haaix nyungc. Meih nyei sai-gorx mbuo weic haaix diuc maiv zei?”

eng: Jesus answered, “How can the guests of the bridegroom mourn while he is with them? The time will come when the bridegroom will be taken from them; then they will fast.
imn: Yesu dau, “Siang-laangh corc yiem jienv cing-jaa-yinh nyei ziangh hoc, kaeqv mienh haih nzauh nyei fai? Zungv maiv haih. Mv baac hnoi-nyieqc oix taux, siang-laangh zuqc dorh mingh mi'aqv. Taux wuov zanc kaeqv mienh cingx daaih zei maiv nyanc haaix nyungc.

eng: Just then a woman who had been subject to bleeding for twelve years came up behind him and touched the edge of his cloak. She said to herself, “If I only touch his cloak, I will be healed.”
