eng: his greatness no one can fathom. One generation commends your works to another;
imn: Ninh gengh! hlo haic, zungv hnamv maiv zaaic aqv. Naaiv doic oix ceng meih zoux nyei sic bun wuov doic muangx,

eng: they tell of your mighty acts. They speak of the glorious splendor of your majesty—
imn: yaac oix zunh yaangh meih nyei domh qaqv zoux nyei sic. Yie oix fei-fangv meih domh njang-laangc nyei haangh daauh

eng: and I will meditate on your wonderful works. They tell of the power of your awesome works—
imn: caux zoux mbuoqc horngh nyei jauv. Mienh oix zunh gorngv Ziouv zoux mbuoqc horngh, gamh nziex nyei sic,

eng: and I will proclaim your great deeds. They celebrate your abundant goodness
imn: yaac zunh yaangh Ziouv za'gengh hlo haic. Ninh mbuo oix zunh gorngv meih longx haic nyei jauv,

eng: and joyfully sing of your righteousness. The Lord is gracious and compassionate,
imn: yaac oix cuotv qiex baaux nzung gorngv meih se baengh fim nyei. Ziouv ceix en yaac korv-lienh mienh.

eng: slow to anger and rich in love. The Lord is good to all;
imn: Ninh maiv qiex jiez siepv yaac maaih hnamv mienh nyei hnyouv buangv nzengc. Ziouv zoux longx bun maanc mienh.

eng: he has compassion on all he has made. All your works praise you, Lord ;
imn: Ninh yaac korv-lienh ninh zeix daaih nyei yietc zungv. O Ziouv aac, meih zeix daaih nyei maanc muotc zungv oix dor-ziec meih.

eng: your faithful people extol you. They tell of the glory of your kingdom
imn: Meih nyei yietc zungv baeqc fingx yaac oix ceng meih. Ninh mbuo oix zunh gorngv meih nyei guoqv nyei njang-laangc,

eng: and speak of your might, so that all people may know of your mighty acts
imn: yaac gorngv taux meih nyei domh qaqv, weic bun zuangx mienh hiuv duqv meih zoux henv haic nyei sic,

eng: and the glorious splendor of your kingdom. Your kingdom is an everlasting kingdom,
imn: caux meih nyei guoqv nyei domh njang-laangc. Meih nyei guoqv se yietc liuz nyei guoqv.

eng: and your dominion endures through all generations. The Lord is trustworthy in all he promises and faithful in all he does. The Lord upholds all who fall
imn: Meih yietc liuz duqv gunv taux maanc gouv maanc doic. Ziouv ziepc zuoqv ei jienv ninh yietc zungv laengz nyei waac zoux. Yiem ninh zoux nyei yietc zungv sic ninh korv-lienh. Ziouv oix baav jienv yietc zungv zuqc ndorpc nyei mienh.

eng: and lifts up all who are bowed down. The eyes of all look to you,
imn: Zuqc kouv nyei mienh ninh yaac liepc jiez daaih. Da'faanh maaih maengc nyei ga'naaiv mangc jienv meih,

eng: and you give them their food at the proper time. You open your hand
imn: ziux horpc zuqc bun nyei ziangh hoc meih yaac bun nyanc hopv ninh mbuo. Meih zoux hnyouv jangv

eng: and satisfy the desires of every living thing. The Lord is righteous in all his ways
imn: bun nyungc-nyungc maaih maengc nyei ga'naaiv duqv nyanc beuv. Ziouv yiem ninh zoux nyei diuc-diuc jauv baengh fim nyei.

eng: and faithful in all he does. The Lord is near to all who call on him,
imn: Yiem ninh zoux nyei yietc zungv sic ninh yaac korv-lienh. Ziouv yiem nitv fatv dauh dauh heuc jienv jouh ninh nyei mienh,

eng: to all who call on him in truth. He fulfills the desires of those who fear him;
imn: dongh yietc zungv longc hnyouv zingx nyei heuc wuov deix. Ninh oix ei jienv taaih ninh wuov deix nyei hnyouv oix nyei zoux ziangx.

eng: he hears their cry and saves them. The Lord watches over all who love him,
imn: Ninh yaac muangx haiz ninh mbuo heuc jienv tov ziouc njoux ninh mbuo. Ziouv beu jienv yietc zungv hnamv ninh nyei mienh,

eng: but all the wicked he will destroy. My mouth will speak in praise of the Lord .
imn: mv baac da'faanh orqv mienh ninh oix mietc nzengc. Yie nyei nzuih oix gorngv cuotv ceng Ziouv nyei waac.

eng: Let every creature praise his holy name for ever and ever.
imn: Zuangx mienh oix zuqc lomh nzoih yietc liuz ceng ninh nyei cing-nzengc mengh taux yietc liuz.

eng: Praise the Lord . Praise the Lord , my soul. I will praise the Lord all my life;
imn: Oix zuqc ceng Ziouv. Yie nyei hnyouv aac, oix zuqc ceng Ziouv. Da'faanh yie ziangh jienv nyei hnoi-nyieqc yie oix ceng Ziouv.

eng: I will sing praise to my God as long as I live. Do not put your trust in princes,
imn: Yie yietc seix oix baaux nzung ceng yie nyei Tin-Hungh. Meih mbuo maiv dungx kaux zoux hlo nyei mienh,

eng: in human beings, who cannot save. When their spirit departs, they return to the ground;
imn: maiv dungx kaux baamh mienh, weic zuqc ninh mbuo maiv haih tengx maiv duqv. Ninh yietv dangx qiex ziouc nzuonx benx nie.

eng: on that very day their plans come to nothing. Blessed are those whose help is the God of Jacob,
imn: Dongh wuov hnoi ninh daav nyei za'eix zuqc mietc nzengc mi'aqv. Maaih Yaakopv nyei Tin-Hungh tengx wuov dauh maaih orn-lorqc,

eng: whose hope is in the Lord their God. He is the Maker of heaven and earth,
imn: se kaux Ziouv, ninh nyei Tin-Hungh, maaih lamh hnamv dorngx, dongh zeix lungh zeix ndau zeix koiv,

eng: the sea, and everything in them— he remains faithful forever. He upholds the cause of the oppressed
imn: caux yiem naaiv deix gu'nyuoz nyei maanc muotc, ninh yietc liuz ziepc zuoqv ei jienv ninh nyei waac zoux. Ninh baengh fim nyei siemv tengx zuqc zeix nyei mienh

eng: and gives food to the hungry. The Lord sets prisoners free, the Lord gives sight to the blind,
imn: yaac ceix nyanc hopv bun hnyouv sie nyei mienh nyanc. Ziouv bun zuqc wuonx nyei mienh duqv bungx nqoi. Ziouv bun m'zing maengh nyei mienh haih mangc duqv buatc.

eng: the Lord lifts up those who are bowed down, the Lord loves the righteous. The Lord watches over the foreigner
imn: Zuqc kouv nyei mienh, Ziouv yaac liepc jiez. Ziouv hnamv kuv mienh. Ziouv goux jienv yiem deic-bung nyei ganh fingx mienh,

eng: and sustains the fatherless and the widow, but he frustrates the ways of the wicked. The Lord reigns forever,
imn: ninh tengx guh hanh fu'jueiv caux auv-guaav, mv baac ninh bun orqv mienh nyei za'eix hoic ninh mbuo zuqc baaic. Ziouv yietc liuz zoux hungh.

eng: your God, O Zion, for all generations. Praise the Lord .
imn: O Si^on aah! Meih nyei Tin-Hungh duqv gunv taux maanc gouv maanc doic. Oix zuqc ceng Ziouv.

eng: Praise the Lord . How good it is to sing praises to our God, how pleasant and fitting to praise him! The Lord builds up Jerusalem;
imn: Oix zuqc ceng Ziouv. Baaux nzung ceng yie mbuo nyei Tin-Hungh se za'gengh longx haic. Ceng ninh se za'gengh horpc yaac puix haic. Ziouv ceix Ye^lu^saa^lem jiez daaih.

eng: he gathers the exiles of Israel. He heals the brokenhearted
imn: Ninh siou zunv zuqc guaatv mingh nzaanx nzengc nyei I^saa^laa^en Mienh. Zuqc hnyouv mun kouv nyei mienh ninh zorc longx

eng: and binds up their wounds. He determines the number of the stars
imn: yaac beu jienv ninh mbuo zuqc mun nyei dorngx. Ninh dingc ziangx hleix nyei soux mouc,

eng: and calls them each by name. Great is our Lord and mighty in power;
imn: tengx norm-norm hleix cuotv mbuox. Yie mbuo nyei Ziouv za'gengh hlo haic, maaih domh qaqv.

eng: his understanding has no limit. The Lord sustains the humble
imn: Ninh nyei cong-mengh jiex ndaangc haih ndorqc duqv taux. Ziouv bouh zuqc zeix nyei mienh faaux,

eng: but casts the wicked to the ground. Sing to the Lord with grateful praise;
imn: zorqv orqv mienh zoi njiec ndau. Oix zuqc dor-ziec, baaux nzung ceng Ziouv,

eng: make music to our God on the harp. He covers the sky with clouds;
imn: longc daanh baah patv nzung bun yie mbuo nyei Tin-Hungh. Ninh longc mbuonx om jienv lungh,

eng: he supplies the earth with rain and makes grass grow on the hills. He provides food for the cattle
imn: ninh liuc leiz mbiungc duih njiec ndau, bun miev cuotv yiem mbong gu'nguaaic. Ninh ceix nyanc hopv bun saeng-kuv,

eng: and for the young ravens when they call. His pleasure is not in the strength of the horse,
imn: caux heuc jienv nyei norqc aa dorn. Ninh a'hneiv nyei se maiv zeiz maaz nyei qaqv,

eng: nor his delight in the legs of the warrior; the Lord delights in those who fear him,
imn: yaac maiv zeiz a'hneiv baamh mienh nyei zaux. Ziouv a'hneiv nyei se taaih ninh nyei mienh,

eng: who put their hope in his unfailing love. Extol the Lord , Jerusalem;
imn: dongh kaux ninh ziepc zuoqv hnamv mienh nyei hnyouv wuov deix. O Ye^lu^saa^lem aah! Oix zuqc ceng Ziouv.

eng: praise your God, Zion. He strengthens the bars of your gates
imn: O Si^on aah! Oix zuqc ceng meih nyei Tin-Hungh. Ninh bun meih nyei zingh laatc nyei gaengh sorn wuonv,

eng: and blesses your people within you. He grants peace to your borders
imn: ceix fuqv bun yiem meih gu'nyuoz nyei naamh nyouz. Ninh bun meih nyei gapv-jaaix gu'nyuoz taaix-baengh,

eng: and satisfies you with the finest of wheat. He sends his command to the earth;
imn: bun gauh longx jiex nyei mbiauh meih mbuo duqv nyanc beuv. Ninh bun ninh nyei lingc cuotv mingh gormx lungh ndiev,

eng: his word runs swiftly. He spreads the snow like wool
imn: ninh nyei waac mingh siepv nyei. Ninh bun sorng hnangv ba'gi yungh biei,

eng: and scatters the frost like ashes. He hurls down his hail like pebbles.
imn: bun mbiungc-suiv gitv daaih nzaanx mingh hnangv saaiv nor. Ninh bun duih borqc njiec hnangv la'bieiv-muonc nor.

eng: Who can withstand his icy blast? He sends his word and melts them;
imn: Ninh bun nziaaux-namx buonc daaih, haaix dauh haih diev duqv! Ninh yietv gorngv cuotv waac, naaiv deix yuqc nzengc.

eng: he stirs up his breezes, and the waters flow. He has revealed his word to Jacob,
imn: Ninh bun ninh nyei nziaaux buonc daaih, wuom ziouc liouc. Ninh zorqv ninh nyei waac bun Yaakopv,

eng: his laws and decrees to Israel. He has done this for no other nation;
imn: ninh nyei leiz-nyeic caux leiz jiu bun I^saa^laa^en. Ninh maiv hnangv naaiv zoux weic ganh fingx mienh,

eng: they do not know his laws. Praise the Lord .
imn: ninh mbuo maiv hiuv duqv ninh nyei leiz. Oix zuqc ceng Ziouv.

eng: Praise the Lord . Praise the Lord from the heavens; praise him in the heights above. Praise him, all his angels;
imn: Oix zuqc ceng Ziouv. Oix zuqc yiem gu'nguaaic lungh ceng Ziouv, yiem hlang nyei dorngx ceng ninh. Ninh nyei zuangx fin-mienh oix zuqc ceng ninh.

eng: praise him, all his heavenly hosts. Praise him, sun and moon;
imn: Ninh nyei zuangx fin-mienh baeng oix zuqc ceng ninh. Mba'hnoi aah! Hlaax aah! Oix zuqc ceng ninh.

eng: praise him, all you shining stars. Praise him, you highest heavens
imn: Ziux njang nyei yietc zungv hleix aah! Oix zuqc ceng ninh. Hlang jiex nyei lungh caux

eng: and you waters above the skies. Let them praise the name of the Lord ,
imn: yiem lungh gu'nguaaic nyei wuom aah! Oix zuqc ceng ninh. Tov bun naaiv deix yietc zungv ceng Ziouv nyei mengh,

eng: for at his command they were created, and he established them for ever and ever—
imn: weic zuqc ninh yietv hatc ziouc cuotv daaih aqv. Ninh liepc naaiv deix daaih yietc liuz taux yietc liuz.

eng: he issued a decree that will never pass away. Praise the Lord from the earth,
imn: Ninh njiec nyei lingc yietc liuz maiv haih jiex. Oix zuqc yiem ndau-beih ceng Ziouv.

eng: you great sea creatures and all ocean depths, lightning and hail, snow and clouds,
imn: Koiv nyei domh mbiauz caux yiem koiv ndo nyei dorngx yietc zungv oix zuqc ceng. Douz caux borqc, sorng caux mbuonx,

eng: stormy winds that do his bidding, you mountains and all hills,
imn: caux ei jienv ninh nyei lingc nyei domh nziaaux, domh mbong fiuv-mbong yietc zungv,

eng: fruit trees and all cedars, wild animals and all cattle,
imn: ziangh biouv nyei ndiangx caux yietc zungv si^ndaa zongh ndiangx, hieh zoih caux yietc zungv saeng-kuv,

eng: small creatures and flying birds, kings of the earth and all nations,
imn: nyorng ndau nyei ga'naaiv caux haih ndaix nyei norqc oix zuqc ceng ninh. Lungh ndiev nyei hungh diex caux maanc fingx mienh,

eng: you princes and all rulers on earth, young men and women,
imn: zoux jien nyei caux yietc zungv zoux hlo nyei mienh, houh saeng caux sieqv,

eng: old men and children. Let them praise the name of the Lord ,
imn: mienh gox mienh caux fu'jueiv oix zuqc ceng ninh. Tov bun naaiv deix yietc zungv ceng Ziouv nyei mengh,

eng: for his name alone is exalted; his splendor is above the earth and the heavens. And he has raised up for his people a horn,
imn: weic zuqc kungx maaih ninh nyei mbuox ganh hlo jiex hnangv. Ninh nyei njang-laangc yiem lungh caux ndau gu'nguaaic. Ninh bun ninh nyei baeqc fingx henv

eng: the praise of all his faithful servants, of Israel, the people close to his heart. Praise the Lord .
imn: weic bun ninh nyei cing-nzengc baeqc fingx ceng ninh, se dongh nitv ninh fatv nyei baeqc fingx, I^saa^laa^en Mienh. Oix zuqc ceng Ziouv.

eng: Praise the Lord . Sing to the Lord a new song, his praise in the assembly of his faithful people. Let Israel rejoice in their Maker;
imn: Oix zuqc ceng Ziouv. Oix zuqc baaux diuh siang-nzung bun Ziouv. Oix zuqc yiem ninh ziepc zuoqv nyei baeqc fingx gapv zunv nyei dorngx ceng ninh. Oix zuqc bun I^saa^laa^en Mienh weic Zeix Ninh Mbuo Daaih Wuov Dauh a'hneiv.

eng: let the people of Zion be glad in their King. Let them praise his name with dancing
imn: Oix zuqc bun Si^on nyei baeqc fingx weic ninh mbuo nyei Hungh Diex orn-lorqc. Oix zuqc bun ninh mbuo cangx heix ceng ninh nyei mengh,

eng: and make music to him with timbrel and harp. For the Lord takes delight in his people;
imn: mborqv nzoz patv daanh baah ceng ninh. Weic zuqc Ziouv a'hneiv ninh nyei baeqc fingx,

eng: he crowns the humble with victory. Let his faithful people rejoice in this honor
imn: ninh bun nyiemc ganh zoux faix nyei mienh duqv hingh jiex. Tov bun gan longx ninh wuov deix weic ninh mbuo duqv hingh jiex nyei njang-laangc njien-youh.

eng: and sing for joy on their beds. May the praise of God be in their mouths
imn: Tov bun ninh mbuo yiem ninh mbuo nyei coux njien-youh nyei baaux nzung. Tov bun ninh mbuo baaux nzung mbui nyei ceng Tin-Hungh,

eng: and a double-edged sword in their hands, to inflict vengeance on the nations
imn: yaac bun ninh mbuo nyei buoz nanv jienv i maengx laic nyei nzuqc ndaauv, weic jaauv win bun maanc guoqv mienh,

eng: and punishment on the peoples, to bind their kings with fetters,
imn: siemv dingc maanc fingx nyei zuiz, weic zorqv ninh mbuo nyei hungh ndoh jienv,

eng: their nobles with shackles of iron, to carry out the sentence written against them—
imn: yaac longc hlieqv-limc ndoh ninh mbuo zoux hlo nyei mienh, weic zoux ziangx ziux fiev jienv oix hnangv haaix nor dingc ninh mbuo nyei zuiz.

eng: this is the glory of all his faithful people. Praise the Lord .
imn: Naaiv se Tin-Hungh nyei yietc zungv baeqc fingx duqv nyei njang-laangc. Oix zuqc ceng Ziouv.

eng: Praise the Lord . Praise God in his sanctuary; praise him in his mighty heavens. Praise him for his acts of power;
imn: Oix zuqc ceng Ziouv. Oix zuqc yiem ninh nyei singx dinc ceng Tin-Hungh, oix zuqc yiem ninh nernh haic nyei lungh ceng ninh. Ceng ninh weic ninh zoux domh qaqv nyei sic.

eng: praise him for his surpassing greatness. Praise him with the sounding of the trumpet,
imn: Ceng ninh weic zuqc ninh za'gengh! hlo haic. Longc nzatc ceng ninh,

eng: praise him with the harp and lyre, praise him with timbrel and dancing,
imn: longc domh daanh baah, fiuv-daanh baah ceng ninh. Mborqv nzoz, cangx heix ceng ninh,

eng: praise him with the strings and pipe, praise him with the clash of cymbals,
imn: patv daanh baah, biomv hlauv-ndongh nux ceng ninh. Nqaapv caauh zeiv ceng ninh,

eng: praise him with resounding cymbals. Let everything that has breath praise the Lord .
imn: longc caauh zeiv nqaapv qom-qaau nyei mbui ceng ninh. Yietc zungv haih tauv qiex nyei ga'naaiv oix zuqc ceng Ziouv.

eng: Praise the Lord .
imn: Oix zuqc ceng Ziouv.

eng: New Brochure Designed to Help Us Make Return Visits and Start Bible Studies
imn: Siang-Sou Zoux Daaih Longc Tengx Mbuo Daaux Nqaang Caux Mienh Gorngv Waac Caux Jiez Gorn Njaaux Mienh Ging-Sou

eng: What new brochure released at the district convention is designed to help us make return visits and start Bible studies?
imn: Maaih haaix buonv siang-sou zoux cuotv daaih bun mbuo longc tengx mbuo daaux nqaang caux mienh gorngv waac caux jiez gorn njaaux mienh Ging-Sou?

eng: Why was the Good News brochure produced?
imn: Weic haaix diuc Kuv Fienx wuov buonv sou se zoux cuotv daaih?

eng: Brothers around the world have been asking for something simple that would attract people to the truth and lead into the Bible Teach book, our primary tool for Bible studies.
imn: Gormx naaiv lungh ndiev, gorx-yiouz dorc muoc tov nyungc hec nyei daaih longc tengx mienh hiuv duqv zien leiz yaac haih longc doqc bieqc Bible Teach wuov buonv sou, naaic se da'yietv nyungc jaa-sic mbuo longc caux mienh hoqc Ging-Sou.

eng: People who are intimidated by a book are often more willing to study the Bible using a brochure.
imn: Mienh dongh buatc sou hoz haic wuov deix maiv oix doqc sou, mv baac ninh mbuo maiv nqemh longc naaiv buonv sou hoqc Ging-Sou.

eng: In addition, a brochure is more easily translated into a large number of languages.
imn: Maiv daan mbuoqc naaic, naaiv buonv sou se gauh hec duqv faan cuotv benx camv-nyungc waac.

eng: How is this brochure different from other study publications?
imn: Naaiv buonv sou caux dieh deix hoqc nyei sou se hnangv haaix nor maiv fih hnangv?

eng: Many of our study publications are written so that a person can read it and understand the truth, even without help. This publication is different. It is written as a guide for Bible study with an instructor.
imn: Mbuo longc hoqc nyei sou se maaih camv-buonv nyei fiev daaih bun mienh ganh doqc liuz yaac haih bieqc hnyiouv zien leiz, maiv zuqc longc mienh tengx. Naaiv buonv sou se maiv zeiz hnangv naaic. Naaiv se fiev daaih dorh mienh hoqc Ging-Sou yaac aa zuqc caux dauh njaaux nyei mienh hoqc.

eng: Like other publications for conducting studies, this brochure can be offered at any time, regardless of what is being featured for the month.
imn: M'daaih hnangv dieh deix hoqc Ging-Sou nyei sou nor, haaix zanc bun naaiv buonv sou mienh yaac duqv nyei, maiv gunv yietc hlaax ganh maaih nyungc sou bun mienh doqc.

eng: Many will enjoy using it with the direct approach to start doorstep studies.
imn: Maaih camv-dauh a'hneiv nyei oix dorng jienv mienh jiez gorn longc naaiv buonv sou njaaux Ging-Sou.

eng: In addition, as mentioned at the district convention, using it when calling back on those who showed interest can really make the return visit work exciting!
imn: Maiv daan mbuoqc naaic, haaix zanc longc naaiv buonv sou daaux nqaang caux oix muangx wuov deix mienh gorngv waac nor, se gengh haiz muonh haic!

eng: How might you conduct a Bible study using the brochure?
imn: Meih hnangv haaix nor haih longc naaiv buonv sou njaaux mienh Ging-Sou?

eng: Use tactful questions to help the householder understand what the scriptures mean.
imn: Longc dorh leiz nyei waac-naaic tengx biauv-ziouv bieqc hnyiouv Ging-Sou nyei waac.

eng: A word of caution: Do not talk too much.
imn: Faix fim oc. Maiv dungx gorngv waac camv.

eng: When introducing a scripture, avoid saying, "The apostle Paul wrote" or, "Notice what Jeremiah foretold." The householder might think that we are reading the words of mere humans.
imn: Haaix zanc doqc Ging-Sou nor, maiv dungx gorngv, "Baulo gong-zoh fiev daaih", fai gorngv, "Mangc gaax Yeˆleˆmi jaa-ndaangc gorngv haaix nyungc." Naaiv se haih zoux bun biauv-ziouv laaic duqv mbuo doqc baamh mienh nyei waac.

eng: It would be better to say, "The Word of God says" or, "Notice what the Bible foretold."
imn: Hnangv naaiv nor gorngv se gauh longx, "Tin-Hungh nyei Waac gorngv", fai gorngv, "Mangc gaax Ging-Sou jaa-ndaangc gorngv haaix nyungc."

eng: In a world where good news is scarce, we have the grand privilege of declaring the best news possible.
imn: Yiem maiv nangc maaih kuv fienx nyei lungh ndiev, mbuo duqv kuv gong zoux, duqv zunh longx jiex nyei kuv fienx.

eng: May we use this new brochure to bring good news from God to thirsting ones in our territory!
imn: Tov mbuo longc naaiv buonv siang-sou dorh Tin-Hungh nyei kuv fienx mingh bun jaang nqaatv nyei mienh!

eng: I'm calling to show you how easy it can be to find out what God has in mind for mankind's future.
imn: Yie daaih meih naaiv weic oix bun meih mangc ndongc haaix nyei hec mbuo haih hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh maaih haaix nyungc za'eix bun baamh mienh maaih hoz nqaang.

eng: Have you ever wondered whether God will relieve us of suffering?
imn: Meih duqv hnamv taux Tin-Hungh oix zorqv guangc nzengc mbuo nyei kouv naanc nyei fai?

eng: This brochure shows where in the Bible you can find the answer to that question.
imn: Naaiv buonv sou mbuox jienv meih mingh doqc haaix norm dorngx yiem Ging-Sou, tengx meih lorz waac dau naaic jioux waac-naaic.

eng: Many people like to pray, especially when they have problems. Do you pray sometimes?
imn: Maaih mienh camv nyei oix daux gaux. Meih nzunc baav daux gaux nyei fai?

eng: Do you think that God listens to all prayers, or could it be that some prayers do not please him?
imn: Meih hnamv daaih Tin-Hungh muangx yietc zungv daux gaux nyei waac nyei fai, aqv fai maaih deix daux gaux nyei waac se maiv horpc ninh nyei hnyiouv?

eng: It's nice to see you again. I brought you this brochure that gives the Bible's answer to many interesting questions.
imn: Yie longx hnyiouv aengx duqv buangh meih. Yie dorh naaiv buonv sou daaih bun meih. Naaiv maaih Ging-Sou nyei waac dau taux camv-jioux longx haic nyei waac-naaic.

eng: Which of these topics interests you the most?
imn: Meih gauh oix hiuv jiex haaix nyungc?

eng: You have just started a Bible study!
imn: Yaauc dingc aqv! Meih jiez gorn njaaux mienh Ging-Sou!

eng: How to Benefit From This Brochure:
imn: Hnangv Haaix Nor Longc Naaiv Buonv Sou Tengx Meih:

eng: As you review the Bible texts, consider how they answer these questions. Any one of Jehovah’s Witnesses will be pleased to help you to reason on the meaning of the Scriptures.—Read Luke 24:32, 45.
imn: Doqc jienv naaic deix Ging-Sou waac wuov zanc, hnamv mangc gaax naaic deix waac se hnangv haaix nor dau wuov waac-naaic. Dauh dauh Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx yaac a’hneiv nyei oix tengx meih bieqc hnyiouv naaic deix Ging-Sou waac.—Doqc Lugaa 24:32, 45.

eng: What Is the Good News?
imn: Kuv Fienx Se Gorngv Taux Haaix Nyungc?

eng: 1. What is the news from God?
imn: 1. Tin-Hungh nyei fienx se gorngv taux haaix nyungc?

eng: God wants people to enjoy life on earth. He created the earth and everything on it because he loves mankind. Soon he will act to provide a better future for people in every land. He will relieve mankind of the causes of suffering.—Read Jeremiah 29:11.
imn: Tin-Hungh oix mienh maanh duqv njien-yiouh kuv-yiem nyei maengc yiem baamh gen. Ninh zeix cuotv baamh gen caux nyungc-nyungc yiem baamh gen nyei ga’naaiv weic zuqc ninh hnamv baamh mienh. Maiv lauh ninh oix zoux bun baamh mienh yiem norm-norm dorngx duqv gauh longx ih zanc, liuz maiv bun maaih haaix nyungc daaih zoux bun ninh mbuo zuqc kouv.—Doqc Yeˆleˆmi 29:11.

eng: No government has ever succeeded in eliminating violence, disease, or death. But there is good news. Shortly, God will replace all human governments with his own government. Its subjects will enjoy peace and good health.—Read Isaiah 25:8; 33:24; Daniel 2:44.
imn: Maiv maaih jiex haaix norm hungh jaa haih zorqv guangc nzengc mienh zoux ciouv zoux waaic nyei jauv, baengc nyei jauv, fai daic nyei jauv. Mv baac se maaih kuv fienx bun mbuo nyei. Maiv lauh Tin-Hungh maiv bun maaih baamh mienh nyei hungh jaa aqv, liuz bun maaih ninh ganh nyei hungh jaa hnangv. Ninh nyei baeqc fingx ziouc njien-yiouh baengh orn, wangc siangx nyei yiem.—Doqc Iˆsaaˆyaa 25:8; 33:24; Ndaaˆniˆen 2:44.

eng: 2. Why is the good news urgent?
imn: 2. Weic haaix diuc naaiv kuv fienx se jienv haic?

eng: Suffering will end only when God clears the earth of bad people. (Zephaniah 2:3) When will that happen? God’s Word foretold the conditions that now threaten mankind. Current events indicate that God’s time to act is close.—Read 2 Timothy 3:1-5.
imn: Zuov taux Tin-Hungh fioux liuz baamh gen, zorqv guangc zoux waaic sic nyei mienh mingh, baamh gen cingx daaih maiv maaih kouv naanc nyei jauv. (Seˆfanˆyaa 2:3) Haaix zanc cingx daaih benx hnangv naaic? Tin-Hungh nyei Waac jaa-ndaangc gorngv ziangx maaih sic hnangv ih zanc mbuo buatc nor, cuotv daaih hoic mienh maanh. Ih zanc cuotv daaih nyei camv-nyungc waaic sic se bun cing Tin-Hungh nyei ziangh hoc dongh ninh oix fioux baamh gen wuov se taux fatv aqv.—Doqc 2 Tiˆmoˆtai 3:1-5.

eng: 3. What should we do?
imn: 3. Mbuo oix zuqc zoux haaix nyungc?

eng: We should learn about God from his Word, the Bible. It is like a letter to us from a loving father. It tells us how to enjoy a better way of life now and how to enjoy everlasting life on earth in the future. True, some may not like it that you are receiving help to understand the Bible. But the opportunity of a better future is too good to miss.—Read Proverbs 29:25; Revelation 14:6, 7.
imn: Mbuo oix zuqc hoqc Ging-Sou, hoqc taux Tin-Hungh nyei jauv. Ging-Sou se hnangv zeiv fienx yiem dauh hnamv haic dorn-jueiv nyei die bun taux mbuo. Ging-Sou mbuox mbuo hnangv haaix nor haih duqv njien-yiouh nyei yiem ih zanc caux hnangv haaix nor haih yietc liuz njien-yiouh nyei yiem yiem naaiv baamh gen nqa’haav hingv. Zien nyei, maaih deix mienh maiv oix meih duqv mienh tengx meih bieqc hnyiouv Ging-Sou. Mv baac maiv dungx piatv dongh meih haih duqv longx haic nyei maengc yiem nqa’haav hingv wuov.—Doqc Cong-Mengh Waac 29:25; Laauc Yaangh 14:6, 7.

eng: Who Is God?
imn: Tin-Hungh Se Haaix Dauh?

eng: 1. Why should we worship God?
imn: 1. Weic haaix diuc mbuo oix zuqc zangc Tin-Hungh?

eng: The true God is the Creator of all things. He had no beginning and will never have an end. (Psalm 90:2) He is the Source of the good news found in the Bible. (1 Timothy 1:11) Since God gave us life, we should worship only him.—Read Revelation 4:11.
imn: Zien Tin-Hungh se dongh Zeix cuotv maanc muotc Wuov Dauh. Yiem loz-hnoi taux nqa’haav hingv ninh m’daaih yietc liuz yiem. (Singx Nzung 90:2) Kuv fienx yiem Ging-Sou se Tin-Hungh bun daaih. (1 Tiˆmoˆtai 1:11) Weic Tin-Hungh bun maengc mbuo, mbuo horpc zuqc kungx zangc ninh ganh nduqc dauh hnangv.—Doqc Laauc Yaangh 4:11.

eng: 2. What is God like?
imn: 2. Tin-Hungh se hnangv haaix nor?

eng: No human has ever seen God because he is a Spirit, which means that he is a higher form of life than the physical creatures who live on earth. (John 1:18; 4:24) Nevertheless, we can discern God’s personality from the things he has made. For instance, the variety of fruits and flowers tells us of his love and wisdom. The size of the universe tells us of his power.—Read Romans 1:20.
imn: Maiv maaih haaix dauh baamh mienh duqv buatc jiex Tin-Hungh, weic zuqc ninh se mangc maiv buatc nyei ga’naaiv. Ninh nyei sin se maiv zeiz hnangv baamh mienh nyei sin nor maaih orv maaih nziaamv. Naaiv se gorngv ninh se zoux gauh hlo ninh zeix daaih yiem baamh gen nyei ga’naaiv. (Yoˆhan 1:18) Maiv gunv mbuo mangc maiv buatc ninh, mbuo haih hiuv duqv ninh nyei fiem-fingx yiem mbuo samx mangc ninh zeix daaih nyei ga’naaiv. Gorngv nyungc zeiv se hnangv naaiv, ziex nyungc biouv caux biangh se bun mbuo hiuv ninh maaih cong-mengh yaac hnamv mbuo. Mangc lungh caux ndau nyei ga’naaiv ndongc haaix hlo ndongc haaix jangv se bun mbuo hiuv duqv ninh nyei domh qaqv.—Doqc Lomaa 1:20.

eng: We can learn even more about God’s personality by reading the Bible. For example, it tells us what God likes and what he does not like, how he treats people, and how he reacts in different situations.—Read Psalm 103:7-10.
imn: Mbuo doqc Ging-Sou nor, mbuo haih hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh nyei fiem-fingx gauh camv deix. Ging-Sou bun mbuo hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh oix caux maiv oix nyei jauv, ninh hnangv haaix nor goux mienh, caux hnangv haaix nor mangc taux nyungc-nyungc sic.—Doqc Singx Nzung 103:7-10.

eng: 3. Does God have a name?
imn: 3. Tin-Hungh maaih mbuox nyei fai?

eng: Jesus said: “Our Father in the heavens, let your name be sanctified.” (Matthew 6:9) Although God has many titles, he has only one name. In each language, it is pronounced differently. In English it is usually pronounced “Jehovah.” But some people pronounce it “Yahweh.”—Read Psalm 83:18.
imn: Yesu gorngv: “Yie mbuo nyei tin-dorngh Diex aah! Tov bun mienh taaih meih nyei cing-nzengc mbuox.” (Matvˆtaai 6:9) Tin-Hungh maaih camv-nyungc heuc nyei, hnangv Ziouv fai Zangc Diex, mv baac ninh maaih nduqc norm mbuox hnangv. Yietc fingx maaih yietc fingx nyei waac heuc. Yiem Iu-Mienh waac nor, naaic norm mbuox se heuc “Yeˆhoˆwaa.” Mv baac maaih deix mienh heuc “Yaweh.”—Doqc Cuotv Iˆyipv 17:15, mangc sou-juoqv nzaangc; Singx Nzung 83:18.a

eng: God’s name has been taken out of many Bibles and replaced with the titles Lord or God. But when the Bible was written, it contained God’s name some 7,000 times. Jesus made God’s name known when he taught people about God.—Read John 17:26.
imn: Maaih camv-buonv Ging-Sou se maiv maaih Tin-Hungh nyei mbuox weic zuqc mienh zorqv cuotv mingh, liuz tiuv an Ziouv fai Tin-Hungh. Zinh ndaangc fiev daaih nyei Ging-Sou se maaih Tin-Hungh nyei mbuox fiev jienv camv zoqc se leih maiv go 7,000 nzunc. Dongh wuov zanc Yesu njaaux mienh Tin-Hungh nyei jauv wuov, ninh bun mienh hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh nyei mbuox nyei.—Doqc Yoˆhan 17:6, mangc sou-juoqv nzaangc; 17:26.

eng: 4. Does Jehovah care about us?
imn: 4. Yeˆhoˆwaa nzauh heix taux mbuo nyei fai?

eng: God has granted man the dignity of free will. Do we not appreciate our freedom to choose to serve God? (Joshua 24:15) But many choose to do bad things to others, so suffering abounds. It saddens Jehovah to see such injustice.—Read Genesis 6:5, 6.
imn: Tin-Hungh bun baamh mienh maaih leiz ginv oix zoux haaix nyungc, weic ninh maiv mangc faix mbuo. Naaic maiv zoux bun mbuo oix ginv fu-sux Tin-Hungh fai? (Yoˆsuˆwaa 24:15) Mv baac mienh camv nyei ginv zoux waaic bun dieh deix. Hnangv naaic, mienh cingx zuqc diev kouv. Yeˆhoˆwaa buatc maiv baengh fim nyei jauv se zoux bun ninh hnyiouv mun.—Doqc Tin Deic Douh 6:5, 6.

eng: Jehovah is a God who cares about us. He wants us to enjoy life. Soon he will eliminate suffering and those who cause it. Meanwhile, he has good reason to allow suffering for a limited time. In Lesson 8, we will learn what that reason is.—Read 2 Peter 2:9; 3:7, 13.
imn: Yeˆhoˆwaa se nzauh heix taux mbuo nyei. Ninh oix mbuo duqv njien-yiouh. Maiv lauh ninh zorqv guangc nzengc kouv naanc aqv, liemh jienv dongh zoux waaic wuov deix. Ih zanc se maaih diuc jauv ninh cingx maiv gaengh zorqv guangc baamh gen nyei kouv naanc. Yiem da’betv wuov ginc, mbuo cingx daaih hoqc taux naaic diuc jauv.—Doqc 2 Bide 2:9; 3:7, 13.

eng: 5. How can we draw closer to God?
imn: 5. Mbuo hnangv haaix nor haih gauh nitv Tin-Hungh?

eng: Jehovah invites us to draw close to him by speaking to him in prayer. He is interested in us individually. (Psalm 65:2; 145:18) He is willing to forgive. He recognizes our efforts to please him, even if we sometimes fail. So despite our imperfection, we really can enjoy a close relationship with God.—Read Psalm 103:12-14; James 4:8.
imn: Yeˆhoˆwaa heuc mbuo daux gaux weic bun mbuo haih mingh nitv ninh. Ninh mangc taux mbuo yietc dauh yietc dauh nyei. (Singx Nzung 65:2; 145:18) Ninh oix guangc mbuo nyei zuiz. Maiv gunv maaih ziangh hoc mbuo haih zoux dorngc ninh, ninh buatc dongh mbuo longc hnyiouv zoux horpc ninh wuov nyei. Ih zanc mbuo maiv dunh yunh, mv baac mbuo gengh haih mingh nitv ninh nyei, yaac haih caux ninh jiu tong.—Doqc Singx Nzung 103:12-14; Yaagorpc 4:8.

eng: Since Jehovah has given us life, we should love him more than we love anyone else. (Mark 12:30) As you express your love for God by learning more about him and doing as he asks, you will draw ever closer to him.—Read 1 Timothy 2:4; 1 John 5:3.
imn: Weic zuqc Yeˆhoˆwaa bun maengc mbuo, mbuo zuqc hnamv ninh gauh camv mbuo hnamv nyei mienh. (Maako 12:30) Se gorngv meih hoqc hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh camv jienv faaux yaac ei jienv ninh nyei waac zoux, bun ninh hiuv duqv meih hnamv ninh, meih ziouc haih nitv ninh gauh fatv.—Doqc 1 Tiˆmoˆtai 2:4; 1 Yoˆhan 5:3.

eng: Is the Good News Really From God?
imn: Wuov Kuv Fienx Se Gengh Zien Yiem Tin-Hungh Daaih Fai?

eng: 1. Who is the Author of the Bible?
imn: 1. Haaix dauh fiev Ging-Sou daaih?

eng: The good news that people will live forever on earth is written in the Bible. (Psalm 37:29) The Bible is made up of 66 smaller books. God used about 40 faithful men to write them. The first five books were written by Moses about 3,500 years ago. The last book was written by the apostle John over 1,900 years ago. Whose ideas did Bible writers present? God communicated with the Bible writers by means of his holy spirit. (2 Samuel 23:2) They wrote down the thoughts of God, not their own thoughts. So Jehovah is the Author of the Bible.—Read 2 Timothy 3:16; 2 Peter 1:20, 21.
imn: Kuv fienx gorngv taux mienh haih duqv yiem baamh gen yietc liuz se fiev jienv yiem Ging-Sou nyei. (Singx Nzung 37:29) Ging-Sou se 66 buonv sou-dorn gapv daaih. Tin-Hungh longc 40 dauh hnyiouv zingx nyei m’jangc dorn fiev naaiv deix sou-dorn. Ndaangc wuov deix biaa buonv sou-dorn se Mose fiev daaih, aav lamh duqv 3,500 hnyangx aqv. Nqa’haav wuov buonv se Yoˆhan wuov dauh gong-zoh fiev daaih, jiex ndaangc 1,900 hnyangx mi’aqc. Fiev Ging-Sou nyei mienh fiev njiec haaix dauh nyei za’eix? Tin-Hungh longc ninh nyei singx lingh caux fiev Ging-Sou nyei mienh jiu tong. (2 Saaˆmuˆen 23:2) Ninh mbuo fiev njiec Tin-Hungh hnamv cuotv nyei jauv, maiv zeiz ninh mbuo ganh hnamv cuotv nyei jauv. Hnangv naaic, Yeˆhoˆwaa se zoux dongh fiev Ging-Sou daaih wuov dauh.—Doqc 2 Tiˆmoˆtai 3:16; 2 Bide 1:20, 21.

eng: Watch the video Who Is the Author of the Bible?
imn: 2. Mbuo hnangv haaix nor haih hiuv duqv cing Ging-Sou se zien nyei?

eng: 2. How can we be sure the Bible is true?
imn: Mbuo hiuv duqv Ging-Sou se yiem Tin-Hungh daaih weic zuqc Ging-Sou jaa-ndaangc gorngv taux nqa’haav hingv nyei jauv-louc se gorngv daaih muonc nyei yaac zuqc nyei. Maiv maaih haaix dauh baamh mienh haih zoux hnangv naaic. (Yoˆsuˆwaa 23:14) Dongh Nernh Jiex nyei Tin-Hungh nduqc dauh ganh hnangv haih hiuv duqv cing baamh mienh nyei hoz nqaang.—Doqc Iˆsaaˆyaa 42:9; 46:10.

eng: We know that the Bible is from God because it accurately foretells the future in detail. No man can do that. (Joshua 23:14) Only Almighty God can accurately foresee mankind’s future.—Read Isaiah 42:9; 46:10.
imn: Yiem Tin-Hungh daaih nyei sou nor, mbuo hnamv daaih se benx lengc jeiv sou, ganh nyungc nyei, maiv hnangv dieh deix sou. Ging-Sou se m’daaih gengh hnangv naaic. Ziex ziepc waanc-waanc buonv Ging-Sou se duqv bun cuotv yiem ziex baeqv fingx waac mi’aqc. Ging-Sou se zinh ndaangc loz-hnoi fiev daaih nyei sou. Maiv gunv ndongc haaix loz, ih zanc baamh mienh koqv hiuv nyei ziex nyungc ga’naaiv yiem naaiv baamh gen se zungv maaih gorngv taux nyei yiem zinh ndaangc fiev daaih nyei Ging-Sou. Yaac maiv daan mbuoqc naaic, fiev Ging-Sou wuov deix mienh zungv fiev waac daaih doix-diuc nzengc. Jiex mingh, Ging-Sou gorngv taux hnamv mienh nyei jauv se bun mbuo hiuv cing waac se maiv zeiz yiem haaix dauh baamh mienh daaih, se yiem Tin-Hungh daaih, yaac maaih lingc maaih qaqv haih goiv yienc mienh nyei hnyiouv daaih zoux kuv sic bun ninh mbuo duqv gauh kuv-yiem. Naaiv deix zien jauv-louc bun ziex baeqv waanc dauh mienh hiuv duqv mengh Ging-Sou se Tin-Hungh nyei Waac.—Doqc 1 Teˆsaaˆloˆniˆgaa 2:13.

eng: We would expect a book from God to be unique, and it is. Billions of copies of the Bible have been distributed in hundreds of languages. Though ancient, the Bible is in harmony with proved science. Also, its 40 writers did not contradict one another.a Furthermore, the Bible bears the unmistakable stamp of God’s love, and it still has the power to change people’s lives for the better. These facts convince millions that the Bible is God’s Word.—Read 1 Thessalonians 2:13.
imn: 3. Ging-Sou gorngv taux haaix nyungc?

eng: Watch the video How Can We Be Sure the Bible Is True?
imn: Ging-Sou se maaih kuv fienx gorngv taux Tin-Hungh maaih longx haic nyei jauv bun baamh mienh. Ging-Sou nyei waac porv mengh baamh mienh zinh ndaangc hnangv haaix nor maiv duqv yiem orn-lorqc baamh gen, yaac porv mengh yiem nqa’haav hingv ninh mbuo hnangv haaix nor aengx haih duqv yiem orn-lorqc baamh gen.—Doqc Laauc Yaangh 21:4, 5.

eng: 3. What is the Bible about?
imn: Ging-Sou se maaih Tin-Hungh nyei doz-leiz, maaih zien leiz nyei gorn-ndoqv, yaac maaih waac njaaux mienh zoux longx, zoux horpc. Maiv daan mbuoqc naaic, zinh ndaangc Tin-Hungh hnangv haaix nor goux baamh mienh se zungv maaih fiev jienv yiem Ging-Sou nyei, bun mienh hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh nyei fiem-fingx se hnangv haaix. Hnangv naaic, Ging-Sou se haih tengx meih daaih hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh yaac se porv mengh meih hnangv haaix nor haih benx Tin-Hungh nyei doic.—Doqc Singx Nzung 19:7, 11; Yaagorpc 2:23; 4:8.

eng: The Bible centers on the good news that God has a loving purpose for mankind. The Scriptures explain how man’s privilege of living on a paradise earth was lost early in human history and how a paradise will finally be restored.—Read Revelation 21:4, 5.
imn: 4. Meih hnangv haaix nor haih bieqc hnyiouv Ging-Sou?

eng: God’s Word also contains laws, principles, and advice. In addition, the Bible records the history of God’s dealings with mankind—a history that reveals God’s personality. Thus, the Bible can help you to know God. It explains how you can become his friend.—Read Psalm 19:7, 11; James 2:23; 4:8.
imn: Longc naaiv buonv sou tengx meih bieqc hnyiouv Ging-Sou se hnangv Yesu longc nyei za’eix njaaux mienh wuov nor. Yesu ziux Ging-Sou nyei waac gorngv yaac porv mengh Ging-Sou nyei jioux-jioux waac.—Doqc Lugaa 24:27, 45.

eng: 4. How can you understand the Bible?
imn: Maiv maaih camv-nyungc ga’naaiv ndongc Tin-Hungh nyei kuv fienx kuv-muangx. Mv baac, maaih deix mienh maiv oix muangx Tin-Hungh nyei kuv fienx, maiv mangc taux, maiv guen taux. Maaih deix yaac zungv haiz yioux gau. Mv baac meih maiv dungx hnyiouv namx. Se gorngv meih oix duqv yietc liuz nyei maengc nor, meih m’daaih a’zuqc hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh.—Doqc Yoˆhan 17:3.

eng: This brochure will help you to understand the Bible by using the same method that Jesus used. He referred to one Bible text after another and explained “the meaning of the Scriptures.”—Read Luke 24:27, 45.
imn: Oix hiuv gauh camv deix nor, mingh mangc da’nyeic wuov zaang yiem What Can the Bible Teach Us? wuov buonv sou, se Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx zoux cuotv daaih nyei sou.

eng: Who Is Jesus Christ?
imn: Yesu Giduc Se Haaix Dauh?

eng: 1. How did Jesus’ life begin?
imn: 1. Yesu nyei maengc hnangv haaix nor jiez gorn?

eng: Unlike any other human, Jesus lived in heaven as a spirit person before he was born on earth. (John 8:23) He was God’s first creation, and he helped in the creation of all other things. He is the only one created directly by Jehovah and is therefore appropriately called God’s “only-begotten” Son. (John 1:14) Jesus served as God’s Spokesman, so he is also called “the Word.”—Read Proverbs 8:22, 23, 30; Colossians 1:15, 16.
imn: Yesu se maiv hnangv baamh mienh, weic zuqc dongh ninh maiv gaengh cuotv seix yiem baamh gen wuov, ninh se yietc dauh fin-mienh yiem tin-dorngh. (Yoˆhan 8:23) Ninh se Tin-Hungh zeix daaih nyei daauh nyungc ga’naaiv, liuz Tin-Hungh longc ninh tengx zeix cuotv maanc muotc. Dongh ninh ganh nduqc dauh hnangv Yeˆhoˆwaa Tin-Hungh ganh nyei buoz zeix daaih. Hnangv naaic, Ging-Sou cingx gorngv ninh se Tin-Hungh nyei “ndoqc” Dorn. (Yoˆhan 1:14) Yesu se zoux Tin-Hungh nyei Gorngv Waac Mienh. Weic naaiv, ninh se heuc “Doz.”—Doqc Cong-Mengh Waac 8:22, 23, 30; Koˆloˆsi 1:15, 16.

eng: 2. Why did Jesus come to the earth?
imn: 2. Weic haaix diuc Yesu njiec baamh gen?

eng: God sent his Son to earth by transferring his life from heaven to the womb of a virgin Jewess named Mary. So Jesus did not have a human father. (Luke 1:30-35) Jesus came to the earth (1) to teach the truth about God, (2) to set us an example in how to do God’s will even when we are in difficulties, and (3) to give his perfect life as “a ransom.”—Read Matthew 20:28.
imn: Tin-Hungh paaiv ninh nyei Dorn njiec baamh gen. Ninh fungx ninh nyei Dorn nyei maengc yiem tin-dorngh bieqc dauh maiv gaengh bueix jiex dorn nyei m’sieqv dorn nyei gu’nguaaz-mbuoqc. Naaic dauh m’sieqv dorn se Yiutai Mienh, mbuox heuc Maaˆliˆyaa. Wuov nyungc, Yesu nyei baamh mienh die se maiv zeiz ninh nyei zien die. (Lugaa 1:30-35) Yesu njiec baamh gen se weic (1) njaaux zien waac gorngv taux Tin-Hungh, (2) zoux nyungc zeiv bun mbuo hiuv duqv hnangv haaix nor zoux Tin-Hungh nyei eix maiv gunv mbuo buangh zuqc haaix nyungc kouv naanc, caux (3) siev ninh nyei dunh yunh maengc “zuoqc mienh camv nzuonx.”—Doqc Matvˆtaai 20:28.

eng: 3. Why do we need a ransom?
imn: 3. Weic haaix diuc mbuo a’zuqc longc dauh mienh zuoqc mbuo nzuonx?

eng: A ransom is the price paid to release a person from the threat of death. (Exodus 21:29, 30) Death and old age were not part of God’s original purpose for mankind. How do we know that? God told the first man, Adam, that if he committed what the Bible calls “sin,” he would die. So if Adam had not sinned, he would never have died. (Genesis 2:16, 17; 5:5) According to the Bible, death “entered” the world of mankind through Adam. Thus, Adam passed on to all his descendants sin and its penalty, death. We need a ransom to release us from the penalty of death that we inherited from Adam.—Read Romans 5:12; 6:23.
imn: Zuoqc mienh nzuonx nyei ga’naaiv se dongh a’zuqc jaauv nyei jaax-zinh weic bungx mienh leih ndutv zuqc daic nyei jauv. (Cuotv Iˆyipv 21:29, 30) Tin-Hungh jiez gorn nyei za’eix se maiv zeiz oix bun baamh mienh zuqc gox zuqc daic. Mbuo hnangv haaix nor hiuv duqv naaic? Tin-Hungh mbuox daauh dauh m’jangc dorn, Aaˆndam, se gorngv ninh zoux dorngc dongh Ging-Sou gorngv “baamz zuiz” wuov, ninh a’zuqc daic aqv. Hnangv naaic, se gorngv Aaˆndam maiv baamz zuiz nor, ninh zungv maiv zuqc daic. (Tin Deic Douh 2:16, 17; 5:5) Ziux Ging-Sou gorngv nyei waac, daic nyei jauv “bieqc” baamh gen se weic laaix Aaˆndam. Weic naaiv, Aaˆndam nyei zuiz ziouc linh jienv mingh bun ninh nyei fu’jueiv, fun-faqv, hoz doic zeiv-fun zuqc daic. Mbuo cingx daaih a’zuqc longc dauh mienh zoux zuoqc mbuo nzuonx nyei jaax-zinh bungx nqoi mbuo leih ndutv zuqc daic nyei jauv dongh yiem Aaˆndam baamz zuiz cuotv daaih hoic zuqc mbuo wuov.—Doqc Lomaa 5:12; 6:23.

eng: Who could pay the ransom to free us from death? When we die, we pay the penalty only for our own sins. No imperfect man can pay for the sins of others.—Read Psalm 49:7-9.
imn: Haaix dauh haih jaauv naaic norm jaax-zinh zuoqc mbuo nzuonx bun mbuo leih ndutv zuqc daic nyei jauv? Taux mbuo daic nyei ziangh hoc, mbuo jaauv duqv mbuo ganh nyei zuiz hnangv. Maiv dunh yunh nyei mienh maiv haih jaauv duqv dieh dauh mienh nyei zuiz.—Doqc Singx Nzung 49:7.

eng: 4. Why did Jesus die?
imn: 4. Weic haaix diuc Yesu daic?

eng: Unlike us, Jesus was perfect. So he did not need to die for his sins—he never committed any. Instead, Jesus died for the sins of others. God expressed extraordinary love for mankind by sending his Son to die for us. Jesus also showed love for us by obeying his Father and giving his life for our sins.—Read John 3:16; Romans 5:18, 19.
imn: Yesu se dunh yunh nyei, maiv hnangv mbuo nor maiv dunh yunh. Hnangv naaic, Yesu daic se maiv zeiz weic ninh zuqc jaauv ninh nyei zuiz. Ninh maiv baamz jiex haaix nyungc zuiz. Yesu daic se weic jaauv baamh mienh nyei zuiz. Tin-Hungh gengh hnamv haic baamh mienh cingx paaiv ninh nyei Dorn njiec daaih daic weic mbuo. Yesu mv baac gengh hnamv haic mbuo cingx ei jienv ninh nyei Die nyei waac zoux yaac siev ninh nyei maengc jaauv mbuo nyei zuiz.—Doqc Yoˆhan 3:16; Lomaa 5:18, 19.

eng: 5. What is Jesus doing now?
imn: 5. Ih zanc Yesu zoux haaix nyungc?

eng: When on earth, Jesus cured the sick, raised the dead, and rescued people in peril. He thereby demonstrated what he will do in the future for all obedient mankind. (Matthew 15:30, 31; John 5:28) After Jesus died, God restored him to life as a spirit person. (1 Peter 3:18) Jesus then waited at God’s right hand until Jehovah gave him power to rule as King over all the earth. (Hebrews 10:12, 13) Now Jesus is ruling as King in heaven, and his followers are announcing that good news worldwide.—Read Daniel 7:13, 14; Matthew 24:14.
imn: Wuov zanc dongh Yesu yiem naaiv baamh gen wuov, ninh zorc longx butv-baengc mienh, dorh daic nyei mienh aengx nangh daaih, yaac njioux mienh cuotv ninh mbuo nyei zeqc naanc. Ninh zoux naaiv deix sic se bun mbuo hiuv yiem nqa’haav hingv ninh oix hnangv naaiv nor zoux bun yietc zungv muangx waac nyei baamh mienh. (Matvˆtaai 15:30, 31; Yoˆhan 5:28) Yesu daic mingh, Tin-Hungh aengx bun ninh nangh daaih benx dauh henv haic nyei fin-mienh yiem tin-dorngh. (Eˆfeˆso 1:20) Yesu ziouc zueiz jienv Tin-Hungh nyei mbiaauc maengx bung zuov taux Yeˆhoˆwaa bun ninh maaih lingc zoux Hungh gunv baamh gen. (Hipvˆlu 10:12, 13) Ih zanc, Yesu yiem tin-dorngh zoux jienv Hungh nyei. Ninh nyei sai-gorx mbuo yaac zunh jienv naaic norm kuv fienx gormx naaiv lungh ndiev mbuox mienh camv.—Doqc Ndaaˆniˆen 7:13, 14; Matvˆtaai 24:14.

eng: Soon, Jesus will use his power as King to bring an end to all suffering and to those who cause it. All who exercise faith in Jesus by obeying him will enjoy life in a paradise on earth.—Read Psalm 37:9-11.
imn: Maiv lauh, Yesu longc ninh zoux Hungh nyei qaqv zorqv guangc nzengc yietc zungv kouv naanc caux zoux orqv zoux waaic nyei mienh. Haaix dauh sienx kaux Yesu yaac ei jienv ninh nyei waac zoux ziouc duqv njien-yiouh nyei maengc yiem orn-lorqc dorngx yiem naaiv baamh gen.—Doqc Singx Nzung 37:9-11.

eng: What Is God’s Purpose for the Earth?
imn: Tin-Hungh Zeix Baamh Gen Daaih Zoux Haaix Nyungc?

eng: 1. Why did God create the earth?
imn: 1. Weic haaix diuc Tin-Hungh zeix cuotv baamh gen?

eng: Jehovah gave the earth to men and women. It is our home. Therefore, the first human couple, Adam and Eve, were not created to populate the heavens—God had already created angels to live in heaven. (Job 38:4, 7) Rather, God took the first man and settled him in a delightful paradise called the garden of Eden. (Genesis 2:15-17) Jehovah gave him and his future offspring the prospect of enjoying life without end on earth.—Read Psalm 37:29; 115:16.
imn: Yeˆhoˆwaa zeix baamh gen daaih bun baamh mienh, se benx baamh mienh yiem nyei dorngx. Hnangv naaic, daauh doix baamh mienh, Aaˆndam caux Haawaa, se maiv zeiz zeix daaih bun ninh mbuo maaih fu’jueiv yiem tin-dorngh. Dongh Tin-Hungh maiv gaengh zeix baamh mienh wuov, ninh zungv zeix nzoih fin-mienh yiem jienv tin-dorngh aqv. (Yopv 38:4, 7) Tin-Hungh zeix daauh dauh m’jangc dorn daaih bun ninh yiem nzueic haic nyei orn-lorqc dorngx heuc Eˆnden Huingx. (Tin Deic Douh 2:15-17) Yeˆhoˆwaa bun ninh caux ninh oix duqv nyei fu’jueiv haih duqv njien-yiouh nyei yietc liuz yiem naaiv baamh gen.—Doqc Singx Nzung 37:29; 115:16.

eng: Originally, only the garden of Eden was a paradise. The first human couple were to fill the earth with their offspring. In time, they were to subdue the entire globe and make it a paradise. (Genesis 1:28) The earth will never be destroyed. It will always be mankind’s home.—Read Psalm 104:5.
imn: Dongh jiez gorn wuov, kungx Eˆnden Huingx se benx nzueic haic nyei orn-lorqc dorngx. Daauh doix baamh mienh a’zuqc maaih fu’jueiv hiaangx daaih buangv baamh gen. Hnangv naaic, ninh mbuo cingx haih gunv duqv mbuoqc baamh gen nyei ga’naaiv yaac tengx ceix ziangh norm baamh gen benx orn-lorqc huingx. (Tin Deic Douh 1:28) Naaiv norm baamh gen oix yietc liuz yiem yaac yietc liuz benx baamh mienh yiem nyei dorngx.—Doqc Gorngv Seix Zaangc 1:4.

eng: Watch the video Why Did God Create the Earth?
imn: 2. Weic haaix diuc baamh gen maiv benx orn-lorqc huingx?

eng: 2. Why is the earth not a paradise now?
imn: Aaˆndam caux Haawaa maiv ei Yeˆhoˆwaa, liuz Yeˆhoˆwaa zunc ninh mbuo cuotv Eˆnden Huingx. Naaic norm orn-lorqc huingx ziouc maiv maaih aqv, yaac maiv maaih baamh mienh haih ceix jiez naaic norm Eˆnden Huingx. Ging-Sou gorngv: “Tin-Hungh zorqv baamh gen jiu bun yiem orqv mienh nyei buoz-ndiev.”—Yopv 9:24.—Doqc Tin Deic Douh 3:23, 24.

eng: Adam and Eve disobeyed God, so Jehovah put them out of the garden. Paradise was lost, and no man has succeeded in restoring it. The Bible says: “The earth has been handed over to the wicked.”—Job 9:24.—Read Genesis 3:23, 24.
imn: Hnyungv, Yeˆhoˆwaa guangc ninh jiez gorn wuov norm za’eix mi’aqc fai? Maiv guangc lorqc! Ninh benx nernh jiex wuov dauh. Nyungc-nyungc ninh haih zoux duqv cuotv nyei. (Iˆsaaˆyaa 45:18) Ninh zungv oix zoux bun baamh mienh aengx benx hnangv loz yaac duqv hnangv dongh zinh ndaangc ninh oix bun ninh mbuo wuov nor.—Doqc Singx Nzung 37:11, 34.

eng: Has Jehovah abandoned his original purpose for mankind? No! He is almighty. He cannot fail. (Isaiah 45:18) He will restore the human race to the way he meant it to be.—Read Psalm 37:11, 34.
imn: 3. Baamh gen hnangv haaix nor haih benx orn-lorqc huingx?

eng: 3. How will Paradise be restored?
imn: Yiem Yesu benx Hungh gunv jienv baamh gen nyei ziangh hoc, baamh gen cingx daaih benx nzueic haic nyei orn-lorqc huingx. Taux mborqv jaax, heuc Aaˆlaaˆmaaˆgetcˆndon wuov, Yesu dorh Tin-Hungh nyei fin-mienh caux jienv ninh mingh mietc nzengc yietc zungv dongh caux Tin-Hungh doix-dekc wuov deix. Liuz, Yesu zorqv Saadaan wuonx 1,000 hnyangx. Tin-Hungh nyei mienh ziouc duqv njioux, maiv zuqc mietc weic zuqc Yesu goux jienv, beu jienv ninh mbuo. Ninh mbuo ziouc duqv yietc liuz nyei maengc yiem naaiv orn-lorqc baamh gen.—Doqc Laauc Yaangh 20:3, 7; 21:3, 4.

eng: Paradise will be restored on earth during Jesus’ rule as God’s King. In a battle called Armageddon, Jesus will lead God’s angels and destroy all who oppose God. Then Jesus will imprison Satan for 1,000 years. God’s people will survive the destruction because Jesus will guide and protect them. They will enjoy everlasting life in Paradise on earth.—Read Revelation 20:1-3; 21:3, 4.
imn: 4. Haaix zanc cingx maiv maaih kouv naanc?

eng: 4. When will suffering end?
imn: Haaix zanc Tin-Hungh cingx daaih zorqv guangc nzengc orqv nyei jauv? Yesu gorngv deix jauv-louc bun mbuo hiuv haaix zanc baamh gen nyei setv mueiz oix taux. Ih jaax lungh ndiev maaih ziex nyungc sic hoic jienv baamh mienh nyei maengc. Naaiv se bun mbuo hiuv cing ih zanc mbuo yiem jienv “baamh gen nyei setv mueiz” nyei ziangh hoc aqv.—Doqc Matvˆtaai 24:3, 7-14, 21, 22.

eng: When will God bring an end to evil on earth? Jesus gave a “sign” to indicate when the end would be near. Present world conditions threaten man’s survival and show that we now live in “the conclusion of the system of things.”—Read Matthew 24:3, 7-14, 21, 22.
imn: Taux dongh Yesu yiem tin-dorngh gunv njiec baamh gen 1,000 hnyangx wuov, ninh oix zorqv guangc nzengc yietc zungv kouv naanc. (Iˆsaaˆyaa 9:6, 7; 11:9) Yesu maiv daan benx Hungh, ninh yaac benx Domh Sai Mienh tengx zorqv guangc dongh hnamv Tin-Hungh wuov deix mienh nyei zuiz. Hnangv naaic, Tin-Hungh ziouc longc Yesu zorqv guangc nzengc baengc caux zuqc gox zuqc daic nyei jauv.—Doqc Iˆsaaˆyaa 25:8; 33:24.

eng: During Jesus’ 1,000-year rule from heaven over the earth, he will bring all suffering to an end. (Isaiah 9:6, 7; 11:9) Besides serving as King, Jesus will act as High Priest and will cancel the sins of those who love God. Thus, by means of Jesus, God will eliminate sickness, aging, and death.—Read Isaiah 25:8; 33:24.
imn: 5. Haaix dauh haih duqv yiem orn-lorqc huingx?

eng: 5. Who will live in the future Paradise?
imn: Haaix dauh ei Tin-Hungh se duqv yiem orn-lorqc huingx. (1 Yoˆhan 2:17) Yesu paaiv dongh gan ninh wuov deix mingh lorz nyiemc ganh zoux faix nyei mienh, njaaux ninh mbuo hnangv haaix nor haih zoux horpc Tin-Hungh. Ih jaax hnoi, Yeˆhoˆwaa njaaux jienv ziex baeqv waanc dauh mienh, mbenc ninh mbuo zipv maengc yiem orn-lorqc huingx yiem naaiv baamh gen. (Seˆfanˆyaa 2:3) Yiem Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx nyei Hungh Guoqv Dorngh, mienh hoqc hiuv duqv hnangv haaix nor haih benx kuv nqox, kuv auv, kuv die, kuv maa. Fu’jueiv caux jienv domh mienh lomh nzoih zangc Yeˆhoˆwaa, yaac hoqc hiuv duqv hnangv haaix nor dorh Tin-Hungh nyei kuv fienx daaih tengx ninh mbuo duqv longx.—Doqc Mikaa 4:1-4.

eng: People who obey God will live in Paradise. (1 John 2:17) Jesus sent his followers to search for meek people and to teach them how to become acceptable to God. Today, Jehovah is preparing millions of people for life in the future Paradise on earth. (Zephaniah 2:3) At Kingdom Halls of Jehovah’s Witnesses, people learn to be better husbands and fathers and to be better wives and mothers. Children and parents worship together and learn how to benefit from the good news.—Read Micah 4:1-4.
imn: Oix hiuv gauh camv deix nor, mingh mangc da’faam wuov zaang yiem What Can the Bible Teach Us? wuov buonv sou.

eng: What Hope Is There for the Dead?
imn: Daic Mingh Nyei Mienh Aengx Haih Duqv Haaix Nyungc Longx Nyei Fai?

eng: 1. What is the good news about the dead?
imn: 1. Maaih haaix nyungc kuv fienx gorngv taux daic mingh nyei mienh?

eng: When Jesus arrived at Bethany near Jerusalem, his friend Lazarus had been dead for four days. Jesus went to the burial place with Martha and Mary, sisters of the deceased man. Soon, a crowd gathered. Can you imagine the joy of Martha and Mary when Jesus raised Lazarus back to life?—Read John 11:21-24, 38-44.
imn: Dongh Yesu mingh taux Mbeˆtaaˆni Laangz wuov zanc, ninh nyei doic Laaˆsaaˆlatv daic mingh duqv biei hnoi mi’aqc. Mbeˆtaaˆni Laangz se leih Yeˆluˆsaaˆlem Zingh maiv go. Yesu caux jienv Laaˆsaaˆlatv nyei i dauh dorc, Maataa caux Maaˆliˆyaa, mingh Laaˆsaaˆlatv nyei zouv. Mingh taux wuov, maiv lauh, mienh camv nyei ziouc daaih souv jienv ga’hlen. Hnamv mangc gaax! Dongh Yesu dorh Laaˆsaaˆlatv aengx nangh daaih wuov zanc, Maataa caux Maaˆliˆyaa ndongc haaix nyei a’hneiv?—Doqc Yoˆhan 11:21-24, 38-44.

eng: Martha was already aware of the good news about the dead. She knew that Jehovah will resurrect the dead to live on earth again.—Read Job 14:14, 15.
imn: Maataa hiuv duqv wuov kuv fienx nyei, dongh gorngv taux daic mingh nyei mienh aengx haih nangh daaih wuov. Ninh hiuv duqv Yeˆhoˆwaa haih bun daic mingh nyei mienh aengx nangh daaih yiem naaiv baamh gen.—Doqc Iˆsaaˆyaa 26:19.

eng: 2. What is the condition of the dead?
imn: 2. Mienh daic mingh se benx hnangv haaix?

eng: Humans are made from dust. (Genesis 2:7; 3:19) We are not spirits living in a body of flesh. We are physical creatures, so no part of us survives death. When we die, our brain dies too, and our thoughts perish. Thus, Lazarus said nothing about his experience of death because the dead are unconscious.—Read Psalm 146:4; Ecclesiastes 9:5, 6, 10.
imn: Baamh mienh se nie-mbung benx daaih. (Tin Deic Douh 2:7; 3:19) Mbuo maiv maaih wuonh mbaeqv fai lingh wuonh yiem jienv mbuo nyei sin zaangc. Mbuo se benx mangc duqv buatc nyei ga’naaiv. Taux mbuo dangx qiex nyei ziangh hoc, mbuo maiv maaih wuonh mbaeqv fai lingh wuonh ca’lengc aengx ganh ziangh jienv mingh. Mbuo yietv daic mingh nor, mbuo nyei m’nqorngv-famv yaac caux jienv daic, maiv zoux gong aqv. Mbuo nyei hnyiouv hnamv nyei jauv yaac mietc nzengc mi’aqc. Hnangv naaic, Laaˆsaaˆlatv cingx maiv gorngv taux ninh daic nyei jauv se hnangv haaix nor weic zuqc daic mingh nyei mienh se maiv hiuv haaix nyungc aqv.—Doqc Singx Nzung 146:4; Gorngv Seix Zaangc 9:5, 6.

eng: Does God torment people with fire after death? Since the Bible shows that the dead are unconscious, hellfire is clearly a false teaching that slanders God. The very idea of tormenting people with fire disgusts him.—Read Jeremiah 7:31.
imn: Mienh yietv daic liuz, Tin-Hungh dorh douz buov ninh mbuo, zeix ninh mbuo, bun ninh mbuo diev mun siouc kouv fai? Ging-Sou gorngv daic mingh nyei mienh maiv hiuv haaix nyungc aqv. Hnangv naaic, haaix dauh njaaux gorngv maaih deic nyuoqc nyei douz buov jienv mienh yiem se njaaux daaih gorngv-baeqc gorngv hoic Tin-Hungh hnangv. Dorh douz buov mienh zeix mienh naaic norm za’eix Tin-Hungh zungv yioux haic, zungv maiv maaih yiem ninh nyei hnyiouv.—Doqc Yeˆleˆmi 7:31.

eng: 3. Can the dead speak to us?
imn: 3. Daic mingh nyei mienh haih caux mbuo gorngv waac nyei fai?

eng: The dead can neither speak nor hear. (Psalm 115:17) But some angels are wicked, and they may speak to people and pretend to be dead humans. (2 Peter 2:4) Jehovah forbids trying to inquire of the dead.—Read Deuteronomy 18:10, 11.
imn: Daic mingh nyei mienh maiv haih gorngv waac yaac maiv haih muangx haiz haaix nyungc. (Singx Nzung 115:17) Mv baac maaih deix fin-mienh se orqv haic, ninh mbuo duqv-eix zoux benx daic mingh nyei mienh daaih caux mienh gorngv waac. (Yiuˆndaa 6) Yeˆhoˆwaa hatc mbuo, heuc mbuo maiv dungx lorz taux daic mingh nyei mienh.—Doqc Nzamc Leiz-Latc 18:10, 11.

eng: 4. Who will return to life?
imn: 4. Haaix dauh aengx haih duqv maengc nangh daaih?

eng: Many millions of people who are dead in the grave will return to life on earth. Even some who did not know God and who practiced bad things will be resurrected.—Read Luke 23:43; Acts 24:15.
imn: Dongh daic mingh wuov deix ziex baeqv waanc dauh mienh aengx haih duqv maengc nangh daaih yiem naaiv baamh gen. Maaih deix se maiv hiuv Tin-Hungh yaac benx zoux waaic sic nyei mienh.—Doqc Gong-Zoh 24:15.

eng: Resurrected ones will be able to learn the truth about God and to exercise faith in Jesus by obeying him. (Revelation 20:11-13) Those who return to life and do good things will be able to enjoy life forever on earth.—Read John 5:28, 29.
imn: Aengx duqv nangh daaih wuov deix ziouc haih hoqc hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh se haaix dauh, ninh yaac hnangv haaix nor. Ninh mbuo yaac aengx haih maaih sienx fim kaux Yesu, ei ninh. (Laauc Yaangh 20:11, 12) Haaix deix aengx duqv nangh daaih yaac zoux kuv sic ziouc duqv yietc liuz njien-yiouh nyei yiem, yiem naaiv baamh gen.—Doqc Yoˆhan 5:28, 29.

eng: 5. What does the resurrection tell us about Jehovah?
imn: 5. Daic mingh aengx haih nangh daaih nyei jauv mbuox mbuo Yeˆhoˆwaa se hnangv haaix nor?

eng: God made hope for the dead possible by sending his Son to die for us. So the resurrection tells us about Jehovah’s love and undeserved kindness. When the dead return to life, whom do you especially want to see?—Read John 3:16; Romans 6:23.
imn: Tin-Hungh paaiv ninh nyei Dorn njiec daaih daic weic mbuo. Hnangv naaic, daic mingh nyei mienh cingx aengx haih duqv nangh daaih yiem nqa’haav hingv. Naaiv se bun mbuo hiuv duqv Yeˆhoˆwaa hnamv mienh nyei jauv caux ninh maaih nyei en, dongh mbuo zungv maiv zic duqv duqv nyei wuov nyungc en. Taux dongh daic mingh nyei mienh aengx duqv nangh daaih wuov zanc, meih oix duqv buatc haaix dauh?—Doqc Yoˆhan 3:16; Lomaa 6:23.

eng: What Is God’s Kingdom?
imn: Tin-Hungh Nyei Guoqv Se Haaix Nyungc?

eng: 1. What is God’s Kingdom?
imn: 1. Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv se haaix nyungc?

eng: The Kingdom of God is a heavenly government. It will replace all other governments and will cause God’s will to be done in heaven and on earth. The news about God’s Kingdom is good. Soon God’s Kingdom will satisfy man’s need for good government. It will unite everyone living on earth.—Read Daniel 2:44; Matthew 6:9, 10; 24:14.
imn: Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv se benx yietc norm hungh jaa yiem tin-dorngh. Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv oix zorqv guangc nzengc ih zanc maaih nyei yietc zungv hungh jaa yaac oix zoux ziangx Tin-Hungh nyei eix yiem tin-dorngh caux yiem baamh gen. Naaiv fienx gorngv taux Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv oix hnangv naaiv zoux se longx haic. Dongh baamh mienh oix maaih longx nyei hungh jaa wuov, maiv lauh Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv oix benx naaic norm hungh jaa bun ninh mbuo buangv hnyiouv nzengc. Naaic norm hungh jaa oix bun dauh dauh yiem naaiv baamh gen horpc fim dongh eix nyei yaac maaih dongh diuc hnyiouv.—Doqc Ndaaˆniˆen 2:44; Matvˆtaai 6:9, 10; 24:14.

eng: A kingdom must have a king. Jehovah appointed his Son, Jesus Christ, to be King of His Kingdom.—Read Revelation 11:15.
imn: Yietc norm guoqv m’daaih a’zuqc maaih dauh hungh gunv. Yeˆhoˆwaa orn ninh nyei Dorn, Yesu Giduc, zoux Hungh yiem Ninh nyei Guoqv.—Doqc Laauc Yaangh 11:15.

eng: 2. Why is Jesus the ideal King?
imn: 2. Weic haaix diuc Yesu zoux Hungh se horpc jiex?

eng: God’s Son is the ideal King because he is kind and he is firm for what is right. (Matthew 11:28-30) Also, he is powerful enough to help people because he will rule over the earth from heaven. After he was resurrected, he ascended to heaven and sat down to wait at Jehovah’s right hand. (Hebrews 10:12, 13) Finally, God gave him power to begin ruling.—Read Daniel 7:13, 14.
imn: Tin-Hungh nyei Dorn zoux Hungh se horpc jiex weic zuqc ninh se hnyiouv longx, ninh yaac dingc hnyiouv zoux baengh fim nyei jauv. (Matvˆtaai 11:28-30) Ninh maaih qaqv henv haih tengx duqv mienh weic zuqc taux ninh gunv nyei ziangh hoc, ninh ziouc yiem tin-dorngh gunv njiec baamh gen. Dongh ninh daic mingh aengx nangh daaih wuov, ninh faaux tin-dorngh mingh ziouc zueiz jienv Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei mbiaauc maengx bung zuov taux ninh duqv gunv. (Hipvˆlu 10:12, 13) Nqa’haav, Tin-Hungh ziouc bun ninh maaih lingc maaih hatc maaz jiez gorn gunv aqv.—Doqc Ndaaˆniˆen 7:13, 14.

eng: 3. Who will rule with Jesus?
imn: 3. Haaix dauh caux Yesu gunv?

eng: A group called “the holy ones” will rule with Jesus in heaven. (Daniel 7:27) The first holy ones chosen were Jesus’ faithful apostles. Jehovah has continued to select faithful men and women as holy ones until now. Like Jesus, they are resurrected with a spiritual body.—Read John 14:1-3; 1 Corinthians 15:42-44.
imn: Maaih guanh heuc “cing-nzengc baeqc fingx [“cing-nzengc mienh wuov deix,” NWT ]” duqv caux Yesu yiem tin-dorngh gunv. (Ndaaˆniˆen 7:27) Ginv ndaangc benx cing-nzengc mienh wuov deix se Yesu nyei hnyiouv zingx nyei gong-zoh mbuo. Yiem naaic daaih, taux ih zanc, Yeˆhoˆwaa ginv hnyiouv zingx nyei m’jangc dorn caux m’sieqv dorn benx cing-nzengc mienh wuov deix. Naaiv deix mienh daic mingh, ninh mbuo hnangv Yesu nor aengx duqv nangh daaih, sin maiv maaih orv maiv maaih nziaamv.—Doqc Yoˆhan 14:1-3; 1 Koˆlinˆto 15:42-44, 50.

eng: How many people go to heaven? Jesus called them a “little flock.” (Luke 12:32) They will finally number 144,000. They will rule over the earth with Jesus.—Read Revelation 14:1.
imn: Maaih mbuoqc ziex dauh mienh duqv faaux tin-dorngh? Maaih 144,000 dauh duqv faaux. Yesu heuc ninh mbuo zoux yietc “guanh zoqc nyei mienh.” (Lugaa 12:32) Ninh mbuo duqv caux Yesu gunv njiec baamh gen.—Doqc Laauc Yaangh 14:1.

eng: 4. What happened when Jesus began ruling?
imn: 4. Dongh Yesu jiez gorn gunv wuov, maaih haaix nyungc sic cuotv?

eng: God’s Kingdom began ruling in 1914.a Jesus’ first action as King was to throw Satan and his demons down to the earth. Satan became furious and began causing trouble throughout the earth. (Revelation 12:7-10, 12) Since then, mankind’s woes have severely increased. Wars, famines, epidemics, and earthquakes are all part of a “sign” that the Kingdom will soon take full charge of earth’s affairs.—Read Luke 21:7, 10, 11, 31.
imn: Yiem 1914 wuov norm hnyangx, Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv ziouc jiez gorn gunv aqv.a Yietv benx Hungh daaih, Yesu zoux nyei daauh nyungc gong se zorqv Saadaan caux Saadaan nyei fin-mienh waaic zoi njiec baamh gen. Saadaan ziouc qiex jiez haic, ziouc zoux cuotv ziex nyungc ciouv nyei sic gormx naaiv baamh gen. (Laauc Yaangh 12:7-10, 12) Yiem naaic daaih, ziex nyungc sic hoic mienh zuqc kouv se jaa camv jienv faaux. Deic-bung mborqv jaax nyei jauv, mienh ngorc hnaangx nyei jauv, butv domh wuon-baengc nyei jauv, caux ndau dongz nyei jauv yietc zungv se benx “jangx-hoc” bun cing maiv lauh Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv oix gunv nzengc baamh gen nyei sic aqv.—Doqc Lugaa 21:7, 10, 11, 31.

eng: 5. What does God’s Kingdom accomplish?
imn: 5. Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv zoux jienv haaix nyungc aqv?

eng: By means of a global preaching work, God’s Kingdom is already uniting a great crowd of people from all nations. Millions of meek people are becoming subjects of Jesus. God’s Kingdom will protect them when it destroys the present wicked system of things on earth. So all who want to benefit from God’s Kingdom should learn to be obedient subjects of Jesus.—Read Revelation 7:9, 14, 16, 17.
imn: Weic mienh ei Tin-Hungh nyei eix zunh jienv Tin-Hungh nyei doz gormx naaiv lungh ndiev, Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv bun mienh camv yiem norm-norm deic-bung maaih horpc fim dongh eix nyei jauv. Maaih ziex baeqv waanc dauh mienh nyiemc ganh zoux faix daaih benx Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv nyei baeqc fingx, bun Yesu gunv ninh mbuo. Taux Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv oix mietc nzengc naaiv norm baamh gen nyei orqv sic wuov zanc, Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv oix beu jienv ninh mbuo. Hnangv naaic, haaix dauh oix benx Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv nyei baeqc fingx nor, oix zuqc ei jienv Yesu nyei waac zoux.—Doqc Laauc Yaangh 7:9, 14, 16, 17.

eng: During a period of 1,000 years, the Kingdom will accomplish God’s original purpose for mankind. The earth will become a global paradise. Finally, Jesus will hand the Kingdom back to his Father. (1 Corinthians 15:24-26) Do you know someone whom you would like to tell about God’s Kingdom?—Read Psalm 37:10, 11, 29.
imn: Yiem 1,000 hnyangx gu’nyuoz, Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv oix zoux ziangx zinh ndaangc Tin-Hungh jiez gorn wuov norm za’eix dongh ninh oix bun baamh mienh wuov. Naaiv norm baamh gen oix benx yietc norm nzueic haic, kuv-yiem haic nyei domh orn-lorqc huingx. Nqa’haav, Yesu ziouc jiu naaic norm Guoqv daaux nqaang bun ninh nyei Die. (1 Koˆlinˆto 15:24-26) Meih maaih haaix dauh meih oix gorngv taux Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv bun muangx nyei fai?—Doqc Singx Nzung 37:10, 11, 29.

eng: Why Does God Allow Evil and Suffering?
imn: Weic Haaix Diuc Tin-Hungh Bun Maaih Kouv Naanc Caux Orqv Nyei Jauv?

eng: 1. How did evil begin?
imn: 1. Orqv nyei jauv hnangv haaix nor jiez gorn cuotv daaih?

eng: Evil began on earth when Satan told the first lie. Satan was originally a perfect angel, but “he did not stand fast in the truth.” (John 8:44) He developed a desire for worship that rightly belongs only to God. Satan lied to the first woman, Eve, and persuaded her to obey him instead of God. Adam joined Eve in disobeying God. Adam’s decision led to suffering and death.—Read Genesis 3:1-6, 19.
imn: Dongh Saadaan gorngv-baeqc daauh nzunc wuov, orqv nyei jauv se yiem naaic jiez gorn cuotv daaih. Zinh ndaangc, Saadaan se benx yietc dauh kuv fin-mienh, mv baac zoux gau “ninh . . . maiv a’hneiv zien leiz.” (Yoˆhan 8:44) Ninh hanc oix duqv mienh zangc ninh, dongh Tin-Hungh ganh nduqc dauh horpc zuqc duqv nyei wuov. Saadaan gorngv-baeqc nduov daauh dauh m’sieqv dorn, Haawaa, ei ninh, maiv ei Tin-Hungh. Aaˆndam yaac gan jienv Haawaa maiv ei Tin-Hungh. Laaix Aaˆndam hnangv naaic zoux, kouv naanc caux daic nyei jauv ziouc cuotv daaih aqv.—Doqc Tin Deic Douh 3:1-6, 19.

eng: When Satan suggested that Eve disobey God, he was starting a rebellion against God’s sovereignty, or position as Most High. The majority of mankind have joined Satan in rejecting God as their Ruler. Thus, Satan has become “the ruler of the world.”—Read John 14:30; 1 John 5:19.
imn: Dongh Saadaan nduov Haawaa maiv ei Tin-Hungh wuov, ninh jiez gorn caux Tin-Hungh doix-dekc. Ninh zoux daaih gorngv Tin-Hungh se maiv puix duqv gunv baamh mienh fai benx Zoux Hlo Jiex Wuov Dauh. Mienh camv yaac gan jienv Saadaan maiv oix longc Tin-Hungh gunv ninh mbuo. Hnangv naaic, Saadaan cingx benx “gunv baamh gen wuov dauh.”—Doqc Yoˆhan 14:30; 1 Yoˆhan 5:19.

eng: 2. Was God’s creation defective?
imn: 2. Tin-Hungh zeix daaih nyei ga’naaiv maiv dunh yunh fai?

eng: All of God’s works are perfect. The humans and angels whom God created were capable of obeying God perfectly. (Deuteronomy 32:4, 5) God created us with the freedom to choose between doing good and doing evil. That freedom gives us a way to express love for God.—Read James 1:13-15; 1 John 5:3.
imn: Yietc zungv Tin-Hungh zeix cuotv nyei ga’naaiv se dunh yunh nyei. Ninh zeix baamh mienh caux fin-mienh daaih haih dunh yunh nyei ei ninh. (Nzamc Leiz-Latc 32:4, 5) Ninh zeix mbuo daaih bun mbuo haih ginv oix zoux longx fai oix zoux orqv. Wuov nyungc, mbuo haih ginv oix zoux daaih gorngv mbuo hnamv Tin-Hungh.—Doqc Yaagorpc 1:13-15; 1 Yoˆhan 5:3.

eng: 3. Why has God allowed suffering until now?
imn: 3. Weic haaix diuc Tin-Hungh bun maaih kouv naanc taux ih zanc?

eng: For a limited time, Jehovah has allowed rebellion against his sovereignty. Why? To show that no effort to rule without him benefits people. (Ecclesiastes 7:29; 8:9) After 6,000 years of human history, the evidence is clear. Human rulers have failed to eliminate war, crime, injustice, or disease.—Read Jeremiah 10:23; Romans 9:17.
imn: Yiem zinh ndaangc daaih, Yeˆhoˆwaa bun mienh caux ninh doix-dekc, sueih ninh mbuo maiv longc ninh zoux hlo jiex gunv ninh mbuo. Weic haaix diuc Yeˆhoˆwaa bun hnangv naaic? Se bun mienh zieqv duqv maiv zeiz bun Tin-Hungh gunv baamh mienh nor, baamh mienh maiv haih duqv longx. (Gorngv Seix Zaangc 7:29; 8:9) Baamh mienh gunv baamh mienh duqv 6,000 hnyangx jiex daaih aqv. Ninh mbuo maiv haih zorqv guangc haengh orqv sic, baengc, mborqv jaax nyei jauv, fai maiv baengh fim nyei jauv.—Doqc Yeˆleˆmi 10:23; Lomaa 9:17.

eng: In contrast to human rulership, God’s rule benefits those who accept it. (Isaiah 48:17, 18) Soon, Jehovah will bring all human government to an end. Only people who choose to be ruled by God will inhabit the earth.—Isaiah 11:9; Read Daniel 2:44.
imn: Tin-Hungh gunv baamh mienh se maiv hnangv baamh mienh gunv baamh mienh. Haaix deix zipv Tin-Hungh gunv ninh mbuo nor, ziouc duqv longx. (Iˆsaaˆyaa 48:17, 18) Maiv lauh, Yeˆhoˆwaa zorqv guangc nzengc baamh mienh nyei hungh jaa aqv. Kungx dongh ginv oix Tin-Hungh gunv ninh mbuo wuov deix duqv yiem naaiv norm baamh gen hnangv.—Iˆsaaˆyaa 11:9.—Doqc Ndaaˆniˆen 2:44.

eng: Watch the video Why Does God Allow Suffering?
imn: 4. Tin-Hungh zoux hnyiouv ndaauv se bun mbuo haih zoux haaix nyungc?

eng: 4. What does God’s patience allow us to do?
imn: Saadaan gorngv mienh fu-sux Yeˆhoˆwaa se weic Yeˆhoˆwaa kungx ceix fuqv bun hnangv. Mbuo haih zoux bun cing ninh nyei waac se maiv zien. Meih oix hnangv naaic zoux nyei fai? Tin-Hungh zoux hnyiouv ndaauv se bun mbuo yietc zungv haih zoux daaih bun cing mbuo oix Tin-Hungh fai oix baamh mienh gunv mbuo. Se gorngv mbuo oix Tin-Hungh gunv nor, mbuo a’zuqc zoux horpc ninh.—Doqc Yopv 1:8-12; Cong-Mengh Waac 27:11.

eng: Satan claimed that no one would serve Jehovah out of unselfish love. Would you like to disprove that lie? You can! God’s patience allows all of us to show whether we favor rule by God or rule by man. We indicate our choice by the way we live.—Read Job 1:8-12; Proverbs 27:11.
imn: 5. Mbuo hnangv haaix nor haih ginv Tin-Hungh gunv mbuo?

eng: 5. How can we choose God as our Ruler?
imn: Mbuo haih ginv Tin-Hungh gunv mbuo se gorngv mbuo longc Ging-Sou hoqc hiuv duqv zien leiz yaac longc zien leiz zangc ninh. (Yoˆhan 4:23) Mbuo haih ginv maiv longc Saadaan gunv mbuo se gorngv mbuo hnangv Yesu nor simv mborqv jaax nyei jauv yaac maiv caux baamh mienh nyei hungh jaa maaih buonc zoux.—Doqc Yoˆhan 17:14.

eng: We can choose God as our Ruler by seeking out and practicing true worship based on God’s Word, the Bible. (John 4:23) We can reject Satan as our ruler by keeping out of politics and war, as Jesus did.—Read John 17:14.
imn: Saadaan longc ninh nyei qaqv kuinx mienh zoux maiv luonx horngc nyei sic, zoux mun mienh nyei sic lo haaix. Se gorngv mbuo maiv zoux naaiv nyungc sic nor, maaih deix mbuo nyei doic caux mienh muoz haih gorngv doqc jatv doqc mbuo fai caux mbuo doix-dekc. (1 Bide 4:3, 4) Hnangv naaic, mbuo a’zuqc ginv oix hnangv haaix zoux. Mbuo oix caux dongh hnamv Yeˆhoˆwaa Tin-Hungh wuov deix nziaauc nyei fai? Mbuo oix ei dongh Tin-Hungh bun daaih longx haic nyei leiz nyei fai? Se gorngv oix nyei nor, mbuo zoux nyei sic se bun cing Saadaan gorngv-baeqc hnangv, weic zuqc ninh gorngv maiv maaih haaix dauh oix gan Tin-Hungh nyei waac zoux se gorngv ninh mbuo buangh zuqc kouv nyei sic nor.—Doqc 1 Koˆlinˆto 6:9, 10; 15:33.

eng: Satan uses his power to promote immoral, harmful practices. When we reject such practices, some friends and relatives may ridicule or oppose us. (1 Peter 4:3, 4) So we face a choice. Will we associate with people who love God? Will we obey his wise and loving laws? If we do, we prove that Satan lied when he claimed that under pressure no one would do as God says.—Read 1 Corinthians 6:9, 10; 15:33.
imn: Weic Tin-Hungh hnamv baamh mienh, ninh laengz jienv zorqv kouv naanc caux orqv nyei jauv guangc nzengc. Haaix deix zoux daaih sienx kaux Tin-Hungh gorngv nyei waac nor, ninh mbuo ziouc duqv njien-yiouh baengh orn maengc yiem naaiv baamh gen yietc liuz.—Doqc Yoˆhan 3:16.

eng: God’s love for mankind guarantees that evil and suffering will end. Those who demonstrate that they believe this will enjoy life on earth forever.—Read John 3:16.
imn: Oix hiuv gauh camv deix nor, mingh mangc ziepc yietv wuov zaang yiem What Can the Bible Teach Us? wuov buonv sou.

eng: 1. Why is marriage vital to family happiness?
imn: 1. Weic haaix diuc a’zuqc tong leiz nyei dorng jaa hmuangv-doic cingx njien-yiouh?

eng: The good news comes from Jehovah, the happy God, who wants families to be happy. (1 Timothy 1:11) He originated marriage. Legal marriage is vital to family happiness because it provides a secure environment in which to raise children. Christians should respect local laws concerning the registration of marriage.—Read Luke 2:1, 4, 5.
imn: Wuov kuv fienx se yiem Yeˆhoˆwaa daaih. Yeˆhoˆwaa se njien-yiouh orn-lorqc nyei Tin-Hungh. (1 Tiˆmoˆtai 1:11, NWT ) Ninh oix hmuangv-hmuangv mienh duqv njien-yiouh orn-lorqc. Ninh se dongh liepc dorng jaa nyei jauv daaih bun baamh mienh wuov dauh. M’jangc caux m’sieqv tong leiz nyei dorng jaa se jienv haic bun hmuangv-doic njien-yiouh. Hnangv naaic zoux se bun naaic deix i hmuangv caux ninh mbuo nyei fu’jueiv haiz gauh maaih dorngx kaux. Giduc Mienh oix zuqc ziux ninh mbuo yiem nyei deic-bung nyei dorng jaa leiz zoux cai-doix sou.—Doqc Lugaa 2:1, 4, 5.

eng: How does God view marriage? He wants it to be a permanent union between a man and a woman. Jehovah wants husbands and wives to be loyal to each other. (Hebrews 13:4) He hates divorce. (Malachi 2:16) But he does allow Christians to divorce and remarry if their spouse commits adultery.—Read Matthew 19:3-6, 9.
imn: Tin-Hungh hnangv haaix nor mangc taux dorng jaa nyei jauv? Dorng jaa nyei jauv se m’jangc dorn dorh m’sieqv dorn daaih zoux auv caux ninh puix seix, yietc seix bun ninh mbuo benx cai-doix. Yeˆhoˆwaa oix dauh dauh nqox caux auv zoux hnyiouv zingx nyei bun doic. (Hipvˆlu 13:4) Ninh nzorng leih cai-doix nyei jauv. (Maaˆlaaˆki 2:16) Mv baac se gorngv nqox fai auv mingh hienx zuqc ganh dauh nor, Tin-Hungh bun maiv zoux dorngc wuov dauh haih leih nqoi aengx ganh longc siang.—Doqc Matvˆtaai 19:3-6, 9.

eng: 2. How should a husband and a wife treat each other?
imn: 2. Nqox caux auv oix zuqc maaih haaix nyungc eix-leiz?

eng: Jehovah created men and women to fill complementary roles in marriage. (Genesis 2:18) As family head, a husband should take the lead in providing materially for his family and in teaching them about God. He should be self-sacrificing in his love for his wife. Husbands and wives should love and respect each other. Since all husbands and wives are imperfect, learning to forgive is a key to happiness in marriage.—Read Ephesians 4:31, 32; 5:22-25, 33; 1 Peter 3:7.
imn: Yeˆhoˆwaa zeix m’jangc dorn caux m’sieqv dorn daaih puix doic zoux cai-doix. (Tin Deic Douh 2:18) Nqox se benx hmuangv-doic nyei ziouv. Ninh oix zuqc liuc leiz goux hmuangv-doic bun ninh mbuo maaih dongh ninh mbuo qiemx zuqc longc nyei ga’naaiv yaac njaaux ninh mbuo Tin-Hungh nyei leiz. Ninh oix zuqc hnamv ninh nyei auv gauh camv hnamv ninh ganh. Nqox caux auv oix zuqc hnamv doic, taaih doic. Weic nqox caux auv maiv dunh yunh, ninh mbuo a’zuqc laanh hoqc guangc laanh nyei zuiz. Naaiv se zoux gorn-ndoqv bun ninh mbuo i hmuangv haih njien-yiouh baengh orn nyei yiem.—Doqc Eˆfeˆso 4:31, 32; 5:22-25, 33; 1 Bide 3:7.

eng: 3. Should you abandon an unhappy marriage?
imn: 3. Dorng jaa liuz haiz maiv a’hneiv nor, nqox caux auv horpc zuqc leih doic aqv fai?

eng: If you experience difficulties in your marriage, both of you should work at treating each other with love. (1 Corinthians 13:4, 5) God’s Word does not recommend separation as a way to resolve problems that are common in marriage.—Read 1 Corinthians 7:10-13.
imn: Se gorngv meih mbuo nqox caux auv ganh maaih dorngx maiv horpc doic nor, meih mbuo ganh i hmuangv oix zuqc zoux laanh hnamv laanh nyei eix-leiz. (1 Koˆlinˆto 13:4, 5) Tin-Hungh nyei Waac maiv zeiz njaaux cai-doix bun nqoi yiem daaih zorc ninh mbuo nyei pou-tong sic.—Doqc 1 Koˆlinˆto 7:10-13.

eng: 4. Children, what does God want for you?
imn: 4. Fu’jueiv aac, Tin-Hungh oix meih duqv haaix nyungc?

eng: Jehovah wants you to be happy. He gives you the best advice on how to enjoy your youth. He wants you to benefit from the wisdom and experience of your parents. (Colossians 3:20) Jehovah also wants you to experience the joy of doing things for your Creator and his Son.—Read Ecclesiastes 11:9–12:1; Matthew 19:13-15; 21:15, 16.
imn: Yeˆhoˆwaa oix bun meih nyei hnyiouv njien-yiouh. Ninh bun meih longx jiex nyei waac-njaaux tengx meih lunx nyei ziangh hoc duqv kuv-yiem. Ninh oix meih nyei diex maac zoux jiex daaih nyei kuv nyungc zeiv caux maaih nyei cong-mengh njaaux meih duqv longx. (Koˆloˆsi 3:20) Yeˆhoˆwaa yaac oix meih duqv njien-yiouh weic zoux nyungc-nyungc bun Zeix meih daaih Wuov Dauh caux ninh nyei Dorn.—Doqc Gorngv Seix Zaangc 11:9–12:1; Matvˆtaai 19:13-15; 21:15, 16.

eng: 5. Parents, how can your children find happiness?
imn: 5. Diex maac aac, meih nyei fu’jueiv hnangv haaix nor haih lorz duqv buatc njien-yiouh nyei jauv?

eng: You should work hard to provide food, housing, and clothing for your children. (1 Timothy 5:8) But for your children to find happiness, you also need to teach them to love God and to learn from him. (Ephesians 6:4) Your example in showing love for God can deeply influence your child’s heart. When based on God’s Word, your instruction can mold your child’s thinking in a positive way.—Read Deuteronomy 6:4-7; Proverbs 22:6.
imn: Meih oix zuqc longc hnyiouv zoux gong daaih maaih lai hnaangx bun meih nyei fu’jueiv nyanc, maaih biauv bun ninh mbuo yiem, caux maaih lui houx bun ninh mbuo zuqv. (1 Tiˆmoˆtai 5:8) Mv baac oix bun meih nyei fu’jueiv haih lorz duqv buatc njien-yiouh nyei jauv nor, meih yaac a’zuqc njaaux ninh mbuo hnamv Tin-Hungh caux muangx ninh nyei waac. (Eˆfeˆso 6:4) Meih zoux nyei nyungc zeiv hnamv Tin-Hungh se gengh haih tengx meih nyei fu’jueiv aengx haih hnamv Tin-Hungh. Haaix zanc meih ziux Tin-Hungh nyei Waac njaaux, meih nyei waac haih tengx meih nyei fu’jueiv hnamv longx nyei jauv.—Doqc Nzamc Leiz-Latc 6:4-7; Cong-Mengh Waac 22:6.

eng: Children benefit when you encourage and commend them. They also need correction and discipline. Such training protects them from conduct that could rob them of happiness. (Proverbs 22:15) Yet, discipline should never be harsh or cruel.—Read Colossians 3:21.
imn: Haaix zanc meih ceng yaac kuinx meih nyei fu’jueiv nor se tengx duqv ninh mbuo. Ninh mbuo mv baac a’zuqc longc mienh gorngv-njaaux caux mborqv njaaux ninh mbuo nyei. Hnangv naaic njaaux ninh mbuo se beu jienv ninh mbuo, maiv bun ninh mbuo sueih ganh nyei hnyiouv zoux ziouc maiv haih duqv njien-yiouh orn-lorqc. (Cong-Mengh Waac 22:15) Mv baac, gorngv-njaaux fai mborqv njaaux nyei ziangh hoc, maiv dungx zoux ba’laqc ciouv jiex ndaangc fai zoux doqc bun.—Doqc Koˆloˆsi 3:21.

eng: Jehovah’s Witnesses publish several books that are especially written to help parents and children. These books are based on the Bible.—Read Psalm 19:7, 11.
