imn: Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx yienx duqv maaih camv-buonv sou lengc jeiv nyei fiev daaih njaaux diex maac caux fu’jueiv. Naaiv deix sou se ziux Ging-Sou nyei jauv fiev daaih.—Doqc Singx Nzung 19:7, 11.

eng: How Can You Recognize True Worship?
imn: Meih Hnangv Haaix Nor Haih Zieqv Duqv Zien Buoqc Zangc Nyei Jauv?

eng: 1. Is there only one true religion?
imn: 1. Se kungx maaih nduqc nyungc zien buoqc zangc nyei jauv hnangv fai?

eng: Jesus taught his followers only one religion, the true religion. It is like a road leading to everlasting life. Jesus said: “Few are finding” that road. (Matthew 7:14) God accepts only worship based on his Word of truth. All true worshippers are united in one faith.—Read John 4:23, 24; 14:6; Ephesians 4:4, 5.
imn: Yesu njaaux ninh nyei sai-gorx mbuo nduqc nyungc buoqc zangc nyei jauv hnangv, se dongh zien buoqc zangc nyei jauv. Naaiv se hnangv diuh jauv dorh mienh mingh duqv yietc liuz nyei maengc. Yesu gorngv: “Lorz buatc [naaiv] diuh jauv nyei mienh zoqc” nyei. (Matvˆtaai 7:14) Tin-Hungh kungx zipv dongh ziux ninh nyei zien Waac zangc ninh wuov deix hnangv. Yietc zungv longc zien leiz zangc Tin-Hungh wuov deix se dongh diuc hnyiouv yaac juangc nduqc nyungc doz sienx.—Doqc Yoˆhan 4:23, 24; 14:6; Eˆfeˆso 4:5, 6.

eng: Watch the video Does God Accept All Forms of Worship?
imn: 2. Yesu gorngv jaav Giduc Mienh se hnangv haaix?

eng: 2. What did Jesus say about false Christians?
imn: Yesu jaa-ndaangc donv maaih jaav douc waac mienh oix zoux bun Giduc Mienh zangc nyei jauv lunc. Ninh mbuo beih zangc zoux hnangv zien Tin-Hungh nyei mienh. Ninh mbuo nyei leiz-baaix dorngh nyei mienh nyiemc ninh mbuo ganh benx Giduc Mienh. Mv baac ninh mbuo zoux nyei sic bun meih haih zieqv duqv ninh mbuo se haaix nyungc mienh. Hnangv haaix nor haih zieqv duqv? Kungx zien buoqc zangc nyei jauv haih maaih zien Giduc Mienh hnangv. Zien Giduc Mienh nyei fiem-fingx caux ninh mbuo zoux nyei sic se mangc duqv hiuv nyei.—Doqc Matvˆtaai 7:13-23.

eng: Jesus warned that false prophets would corrupt Christianity. Outwardly, they seem like true worshippers. Their churches claim to be Christian. But you can recognize such people for what they really are. How? Only true worship produces genuine Christians with recognizable qualities and ways.—Read Matthew 7:13-23.
imn: 3. Meih hnangv haaix nor haih zieqv duqv zien Tin-Hungh nyei mienh?

eng: 3. How can you recognize true worshippers?
imn: Mangc taux naaiv deix biaa diuc jauv:

eng: Consider these five identifying marks:
imn: Zien Tin-Hungh nyei mienh taaih Ging-Sou benx Tin-Hungh nyei Waac. Ninh mbuo longc hnyiouv gan longx Ging-Sou njaaux nyei jauv. Zien buoqc zangc nyei jauv caux baamh mienh longc ganh nyei cong-mengh liepc daaih nyei se maiv fih hnangv. (Matvˆtaai 15:7-9) Zien Tin-Hungh nyei mienh maiv zeiz njaaux mienh yietc nyungc, liuz mingh zoux ganh nyungc.—Doqc Yoˆhan 17:17; 2 Tiˆmoˆtai 3:16, 17.

eng: True worshippers respect the Bible as God’s Word. They strive to live by its principles. So true religion differs from religion that is based on men’s ideas. (Matthew 15:7-9) True worshippers do not preach one thing and practice another.—Read John 17:17; 2 Timothy 3:16, 17.
imn: Ninh mbuo taaih Tin-Hungh nyei mbuox, Yeˆhoˆwaa. Yesu taaih Tin-Hungh nyei mbuox dongh ninh gorngv cuotv bun mienh hiuv duqv wuov. Ninh tengx mienh daaih hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh yaac njaaux ninh mbuo daux gaux bun mienh taaih Tin-Hungh nyei cing-nzengc mbuox. (Matvˆtaai 6:9) Dongh meih yiem nyei dorngx wuov, meih buatc haaix nyungc buoqc zangc nyei jauv zunh Tin-Hungh nyei mbuox bun mienh hiuv?—Doqc Yoˆen 2:32; Cuotv Iˆyipv 17:15, mangc sou-juoqv nzaangc.

eng: Jesus’ true followers honor God’s name, Jehovah. Jesus honored God’s name by making it known. He helped people to know God and taught them to pray that God’s name be sanctified. (Matthew 6:9) Where you live, which religion makes God’s name known?—Read John 17:26; Romans 10:13, 14.
imn: Zien Giduc Mienh zunh doz gorngv taux Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv. Tin-Hungh paaiv Yesu daaih zunh Tin-Hungh zoux Ziouv gunv mienh nyei kuv fienx. Kungx Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv hnangv se haih tengx baamh mienh. Yesu zunh jienv naaic norm Guoqv nyei kuv fienx mingh taux ninh daic nyei hnoi. (Lugaa 4:43; 8:1; 23:42, 43) Ninh gorngv ninh nyei sai-gorx mbuo yaac oix zunh gorngv taux naaic norm Guoqv. Se gorngv maaih laanh mienh buangh zuqc meih yaac gorngv taux Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv nor, meih hnamv daaih ninh yiem haaix nyungc buoqc zangc nyei jauv?—Doqc Matvˆtaai 24:14.

eng: True Christians preach about God’s Kingdom. God sent Jesus to preach the good news of the Kingdom. God’s Kingdom is the only hope for mankind. Jesus continued speaking about it until his dying day. (Luke 4:43; 8:1; 23:42, 43) He said that his followers would preach about it. If someone approaches you to speak about God’s Kingdom, to which religion does he likely belong?—Read Matthew 24:14.
imn: Yesu nyei sai-gorx maiv guei naaiv norm orqv nyei lungh ndiev. Meih haih zieqv duqv ninh mbuo weic zuqc ninh mbuo maiv caux baamh mienh nyei hungh jaa maaih buonc zoux fai maaih nzaeng hingh nyei jauv. (Yoˆhan 17:16; 18:36) Ninh mbuo yaac maiv zuotc lungh ndiev mienh nyei eix-leiz caux maiv zoux mun mienh nyei sic.—Doqc Yaagorpc 4:4.

eng: Jesus’ followers are no part of this wicked world. You can recognize them by the way they take no part in politics or social conflicts. (John 17:16; 18:36) Also, they do not imitate the world’s harmful practices and attitudes.—Read James 4:4.
imn: Zien Giduc Mienh za’gengh maaih hnyiouv laanh hnamv laanh. Ninh mbuo hoqc hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh nyei Waac daaih, ninh mbuo maiv mangc faix haaix fingx mienh. Maiv gunv jaav buoqc zangc nyei jauv a’loc njiec qaqv bouc deic-bung mborqv jaax nyei sic, zien zangc Tin-Hungh nyei mienh maiv laengz hnangv naaic zoux. (Mikaa 4:1-3) Zien Giduc Mienh zungv guangc jienv ninh mbuo ganh nyei ziangh hoc yaac longc ninh mbuo ganh maaih nyei ga’naaiv daaih tengx mienh, kuinx mienh.—Doqc Yoˆhan 13:34, 35; 1 Yoˆhan 4:20.

eng: True Christians have outstanding love for one another. From God’s Word, they learn to respect all ethnic groups. Although false religions have often strongly supported the wars of the nations, true worshippers refuse to do so. (Micah 4:1-3) Rather, true Christians unselfishly use their time and resources to help and encourage others.—Read John 13:34, 35; 1 John 4:20.
imn: 4. Meih haih zieqv duqv zien buoqc zangc nyei jauv nyei fai?

eng: 4. Can you identify the true religion?
imn: Haaix nyungc buoqc zangc nyei jauv njaaux nyei nyungc-nyungc se ziux Tin-Hungh nyei Waac njaaux, taaih Tin-Hungh nyei mbuox, caux zunh mbuox mienh camv kungx Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv hnangv haih tengx baamh mienh? Haaix guanh mienh zoux hnamv mienh nyei jauv caux simv nqoi deic-bung mborqv jaax nyei sic? Meih hnangv haaix nor hnamv?—Doqc 1 Yoˆhan 3:10-12.

eng: Which religion bases all its teachings on God’s Word, honors God’s name, and proclaims God’s Kingdom as mankind’s only hope? Which group practices love and shuns war? What do you think?—Read 1 John 3:10-12.
imn: Oix hiuv gauh camv deix nor, mingh mangc ziepc hmz wuov zaang yiem What Can the Bible Teach Us? wuov buonv sou.

eng: How Can You Draw Close to God?
imn: Meih Hnangv Haaix Nor Haih Mingh Nitv Jienv Tin-Hungh?

eng: 1. Does God listen to all prayers?
imn: 1. Tin-Hungh muangx yietc zungv daux gaux nyei waac nyei fai?

eng: God invites people of all sorts to draw close to him in prayer. (Psalm 65:2) Yet, he does not listen to, or accept, all prayers. For example, the prayers of a man who mistreats his wife can be hindered. (1 Peter 3:7) Also, when the Israelites persisted in badness, God refused to hear their prayers. Clearly, prayer is a privilege. Nevertheless, God will accept the prayers of even gross sinners if they repent.—Read Isaiah 1:15; 55:7.
imn: Tin-Hungh heuc ziex nyungc mienh daaih daux gaux nitv ninh. (Singx Nzung 65:2) Mv baac, ninh maiv muangx, fai maiv zipv, yietc zungv daux gaux nyei waac. Gorngv nyungc zeiv se hnangv naaiv, se gorngv m’jangc dorn zoux waaic bun ninh nyei auv nor, ninh zoux waaic nyei sic se haih laanh jienv ninh daux gaux nyei waac. (1 Bide 3:7) Dongh wuov zanc Iˆsaaˆlaaˆen Mienh maiv kangv goiv ninh mbuo zoux waaic nyei sic wuov, Tin-hungh maiv muangx ninh mbuo daux gaux nyei waac. Mv baac, Tin-Hungh zungv oix zipv zuiz-mienh daux gaux nyei waac se gorngv ninh mbuo maaih hnyiouv guangc zuiz nor, maiv gunv ninh mbuo duqv baamz jiex zuiz ndongc haaix hlo. Naaiv bun mbuo hiuv duqv cing, daux gaux nyei jauv se yietc nyungc Tin-Hungh bun nyei lengc jeiv zingh nyeic.—Doqc Iˆsaaˆyaa 1:15; 55:7.

eng: Watch the video Does God Listen to All Prayers?
imn: 2. Mbuo horpc zuqc hnangv haaix nor daux gaux?

eng: 2. How should we pray?
imn: Mbuo zangc Tin-Hungh se oix zuqc daux gaux. Mbuo horpc zuqc kungx daux gaux bun Yeˆhoˆwaa nduqc dauh ganh hnangv weic zuqc ninh se dongh Zeix mbuo daaih Wuov Dauh. (Matvˆtaai 4:10; 6:9) Mv baac weic mbuo maiv dunh yunh, mbuo a’zuqc dengv jienv Yesu nyei mbuox daux gaux weic zuqc ninh daic nzaaux mbuo nyei zuiz. (Yoˆhan 14:6) Yeˆhoˆwaa maiv oix mbuo dorh mienh fiev daaih nyei waac fai kungx jiangx jienv haaix deix waac daaih paan mingh puoqv nzuonx daux gaux kungx gorngv dingc naaic deix waac hnangv. Ninh oix mbuo maaih haaix nyungc yiem hnyiouv se ziux naaic gorngv.—Doqc Matvˆtaai 6:7; Fiˆlipvˆpoi 4:6, 7.

eng: Prayer is part of our worship, so we should pray only to our Creator, Jehovah. (Matthew 4:10; 6:9) Also, since we are imperfect, we should pray in the name of Jesus because he died for our sins. (John 14:6) Jehovah does not want us to repeat memorized or written prayers. He wants us to pray from our heart.—Read Matthew 6:7; Philippians 4:6, 7.
imn: Zeix mbuo daaih Wuov Dauh zungv haih muangx duqv haiz mbuo yiem hnyiouv gu’nyuoz daux gaux nyei waac nyei. (1 Saaˆmuˆen 1:12, 13) Ninh oix mbuo maaih haaix nyungc yaac daux gaux bun ninh. Mbuo haaix zanc daux gaux yaac duqv, hnangv lungh ndorm, lungh muonz, nyanc hnaangx nyei ziangh hoc, caux haaix zanc mbuo buangh zuqc haaix nyungc sic.—Doqc Singx Nzung 55:22; Matvˆtaai 15:36.

eng: Our Creator can hear even silent prayers. (1 Samuel 1:12, 13) He invites us to pray on every occasion, such as at the beginning and the end of the day, at mealtimes, and when we face problems.—Read Psalm 55:22; Matthew 15:36.
imn: 3. Weic haaix diuc Giduc Mienh gapv zunv buangh?

eng: 3. Why do Christians meet together?
imn: Mingh nitv fatv Tin-Hungh se maiv hec weic zuqc mbuo yiem zuqc maiv sienx Tin-Hungh nyei mienh mbu’ndongx. Ninh mbuo yaac oix guatc doqc jatv Tin-Hungh laengz nyei waac oix bun naaiv norm lungh ndiev baengh orn daaih. (2 Tiˆmoˆtai 3:1, 4; 2 Bide 3:3, 13) Hnangv naaic, mbuo cingx a’zuqc caux dongh caux mbuo juangc nyungc sienx nyei mienh nziaauc, bun mbuo lomh nzoih laanh kuinx laanh.—Doqc Hipvˆlu 10:24, 25.

eng: Drawing close to God is not easy because we live among people who lack faith in God and ridicule his promise of peace on earth. (2 Timothy 3:1, 4; 2 Peter 3:3, 13) So we need the encouraging association of fellow believers, and they need us.—Read Hebrews 10:24, 25.
imn: Caux hnamv Tin-Hungh nyei mienh nziaauc se tengx mbuo haih mingh nitv Tin-Hungh. Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx mbuo nyei wuic se zoux daaih bun mienh maaih kuv ziangh hoc duqv buatc dieh deix nyei sienx fim ziouc haih tengx ninh mbuo ganh sienx aengx gauh henv.—Doqc Lomaa 1:11, 12.

eng: Associating with people who love God helps us to draw close to God. The meetings of Jehovah’s Witnesses provide fine opportunities to benefit from the faith of others.—Read Romans 1:11, 12.
imn: 4. Meih hnangv haaix nor haih mingh nitv jienv Tin-Hungh?

eng: 4. How can you draw close to God?
imn: Meih haih mingh nitv jienv Yeˆhoˆwaa se gorngv meih fei-fangv taux dongh meih duqv hoqc taux nyei jauv yiem Ging-Sou wuov. Oix zuqc hnamv taux Yeˆhoˆwaa zoux nyei sic, ninh njaaux nyei jauv, caux ninh laengz nyei waac. Se gorngv mbuo daux gaux bun Yeˆhoˆwaa yaac fei-fangv taux ninh nyei jauv nor, naaic se tengx mbuo haih maaih hnyiouv zongc zingh haic Tin-Hungh nyei cong-mengh caux ninh hnamv mienh nyei jauv.—Doqc Yoˆsuˆwaa 1:8; Singx Nzung 1:1-3.

eng: You can draw close to Jehovah by meditating on what you have learned from his Word. Contemplate his activities, his advice, and his promises. Prayerful meditation builds appreciation in our heart for God’s love and wisdom.—Read Joshua 1:8; Psalm 1:1-3.
imn: Meih haih mingh nitv jienv Tin-Hungh se gorngv meih kaux ninh, se gorngv meih maaih sienx fim. Mv baac sienx fim nyei jauv se beiv hnangv taux ga’naaiv-zuangx nor zanc-zanc a’zuqc fuqv wuom goux jienv yiem cingx daaih haih hlo. Meih a’zuqc zanc-zanc zaah mangc meih sienx nyei jauv weic tengx meih goux jienv meih nyei sienx fim bun meih haih sienx wuonv.—Doqc Matvˆtaai 4:4; Hipvˆlu 11:1, 6.

eng: You can be close to God only if you trust in him, if you have faith in him. But faith is like a living thing that needs feeding. You must constantly feed your faith by reviewing the basis for your beliefs.—Read Matthew 4:4; Hebrews 11:1, 6.
imn: 5. Mingh nitv jienv Tin-Hungh nor, naaic hnangv haaix nor haih tengx meih?

eng: 5. How will drawing close to God benefit you?
imn: Yeˆhoˆwaa korv-lienh dongh hnamv ninh wuov deix mienh. Ninh haih beu jienv ninh mbuo nyei sienx fim caux dongh ninh mbuo hnamv jienv oix duqv yietc liuz nyei maengc wuov. (Singx Nzung 91:1, 2, 7-10) Ninh donv mbuo haaix nyungc jauv haih zoux bun mbuo maiv wangc yaac maiv njien-yiouh. Yeˆhoˆwaa njaaux mbuo hnangv haaix nor yiem se gauh longx jiex.—Doqc Singx Nzung 73:27, 28; Yaagorpc 4:4, 8.

eng: Jehovah cares for those who love him. He can protect them from anything that could jeopardize their faith and their hope of everlasting life. (Psalm 91:1, 2, 7-10) He warns us against ways of life that threaten our health and happiness. Jehovah teaches us the best way of life.—Read Psalm 73:27, 28; James 4:4, 8.
imn: Oix hiuv gauh camv deix nor, mingh mangc ziepc cietv wuov zaang yiem What Can the Bible Teach Us? wuov buonv sou.

eng: What Is the Good News About Religion?
imn: Kuv Fienx Hnangv Haaix Nor Gorngv Taux Buoqc Zangc Nyei Jauv?

eng: 1. Is all religion good?
imn: 1. Yietc zungv buoqc zangc nyei jauv se longx bun mbuo nyei fai?

eng: There are sincere people in all religions. It is good news that God sees those people and cares about them. But sadly, religion has been used for evil ends. (2 Corinthians 4:3, 4; 11:13-15) According to news reports, some religions are even involved in terrorism, genocide, war, and child abuse. How this saddens sincere believers in God!—Read Matthew 24:3-5, 11, 12.
imn: Yietc zungv buoqc zangc nyei jauv se maaih hnyiouv longx nyei mienh nyei. Se longx haic, Tin-Hungh hiuv duqv naaic deix mienh nyei yaac nzauh heix taux ninh mbuo. Mv baac maaih mienh dorh buoqc zangc nyei jauv daaih zoux orqv sic. (2 Koˆlinˆto 4:3, 4; 11:13-15) Ziux lungh ndiev nyei fienx gorngv daaih, maaih deix buoqc zangc nyei jauv zungv caux jienv maaih buonc zoux doqc zoux ciouv haeqv mienh gamh nziex, njiec hnyiouv daix mienh camv weic oix mietc nzengc ninh mbuo, mborqv jaax weic nzaeng deic-bung fai nzaeng zoux hlo, caux zoux doqc fu’jueiv fai caa fu’jueiv bueix. Hnangv naaic zoux daaih, zien sienx Tin-Hungh wuov deix haiz nzauh haic!—Doqc Matvˆtaai 24:3-5, 11, 12.

eng: Whereas true religion glorifies God, false religion displeases him. It teaches ideas that are not in the Bible, including false teachings about God and about the dead. But Jehovah wants people to know the truth about him.—Read Ezekiel 18:4; 1 Timothy 2:3-5.
imn: Zien buoqc zangc nyei jauv zoux daaih se ceng Tin-Hungh, mv baac jaav buoqc zangc nyei jauv zoux daaih ninh maiv a’hneiv. Jaav buoqc zangc nyei jauv njaaux nyei jauv-louc se maiv yiem Ging-Sou, liemh jienv dongh ninh mbuo njaaux jaav leiz gorngv taux Tin-Hungh caux daic nyei jauv. Mv baac Yeˆhoˆwaa oix mienh hiuv duqv zien ninh se hnangv haaix.—Doqc Eˆseˆken 18:4; 1 Tiˆmoˆtai 2:3-5.

eng: 2. What is the good news about religion?
imn: 2. Kuv fienx hnangv haaix nor gorngv taux buoqc zangc nyei jauv?

eng: Happily, God is not deceived by religions that claim to love him but are really in love with Satan’s world. (James 4:4) God’s Word refers to all false religion as “Babylon the Great.” Babylon was the ancient city where false religion began after the Flood of Noah’s day. Soon, God will bring a sudden end to religion that deceives and oppresses mankind.—Read Revelation 17:1, 2, 5, 16, 17; 18:8.
imn: Jaav buoqc zangc nyei jauv se maiv haih nduov duqv zuqc Tin-Hungh. Ninh mbuo nyiemc ninh mbuo hnamv Tin-Hungh, mv baac zien nor, ninh mbuo nyei hnyiouv zungv bun nzengc Saadaan nyei lungh ndiev mi’aqc. (Yaagorpc 4:4) Tin-Hungh nyei waac yiem Ging-Sou bun mbuo hiuv duqv yietc zungv jaav buoqc zangc nyei jauv se heuc “Mbaaˆmbiˆlon Domh Zingh.” Mbaaˆmbiˆlon Zingh se loz-hnoi nyei zingh. Dongh jiex liuz mbiungc-suiv-imx yiem Noyaa nyei ziangh hoc wuov, jaav buoqc zangc nyei jauv se yiem Mbaaˆmbiˆlon Zingh jiez gorn cuotv daaih. Maiv lauh, Tin-Hungh oix liemh zeih mietc nzengc buoqc zangc nyei jauv dongh zoux nduov mienh caux zoux doqc zoux hoic mienh wuov.—Doqc Laauc Yaangh 17:1, 2, 5, 16, 17; 18:8.

eng: There is more good news. Jehovah has not forgotten the sincere people who are scattered among false religions worldwide. He is uniting such people by teaching them the truth.—Read Micah 4:2, 5.
imn: Kuv fienx nyei waac se aengx maaih gauh camv naaiv. Yeˆhoˆwaa maiv la’kuqv hnyiouv longx nyei mienh gormx naaiv lungh ndiev dongh faatv nzaanx mingh yiem zuqc jaav buoqc zangc nyei jauv wuov. Ninh njaaux naaic nyungc mienh zien leiz, bun ninh mbuo daaih gapv zunv gan ninh nyei jauv yangh.—Doqc Mikaa 4:2, 5.

eng: 3. What should sincere people do?
imn: 3. Hnyiouv longx nyei mienh horpc zuqc hnangv haaix nor zoux?

eng: Jehovah cares about people who love what is true and good. He urges them to leave false religion behind. People who love God are willing to make changes to please him.—Read Revelation 18:4.
imn: Dongh hnamv zien leiz yaac oix zoux longx nyei mienh, Yeˆhoˆwaa oix hnamv ninh mbuo. Ninh heuc ninh mbuo oix zuqc gaanv deix yiem jaav buoqc zangc nyei jauv cuotv daaih. Dongh maaih hnyiouv hnamv Tin-Hungh wuov deix oix a’hneiv nyei goiv daaih zoux horpc ninh.—Doqc Laauc Yaangh 18:4.

eng: In the first century, when sincere people heard the good news from the apostles, they responded with joy. They learned a new way of life from Jehovah, a happier way of life with a purpose and a hope. They are a good example for us today because they responded to the good news by putting Jehovah first in their lives.—Read 1 Thessalonians 1:8, 9; 2:13.
imn: Yiem gu’nyuoz daauh baeqv hnyangx nyei ziangh hoc wuov, hnyiouv longx nyei mienh duqv haiz liuz gong-zoh mbuo zunh nyei kuv fienx, ninh mbuo ziouc a’hneiv nyei zipv. Ninh mbuo duqv Yeˆhoˆwaa njaaux ninh mbuo siang-jauv hnangv haaix nor yiem, se gauh longx nyei jauv weic bun ninh mbuo maaih nyungc zoux caux maaih lamh hnamv dorngx. Ninh mbuo se zoux kuv nyungc zeiv bun mbuo ih jaax hnoi nyei mienh weic zuqc ninh mbuo zipv wuov kuv fienx dorh Yeˆhoˆwaa daaih ndaangc nyungc-nyungc.—Doqc 1 Teˆsaaˆloˆniˆgaa 1:8, 9; 2:13.

eng: Jehovah welcomes into his family of worshippers those who turn away from false religion. If you accept Jehovah’s warm invitation, you will receive his friendship, a new and loving family of fellow worshippers of Jehovah, and everlasting life.—Read Mark 10:29, 30; 2 Corinthians 6:17, 18.
imn: Haaix deix biaux cuotv jaav buoqc zangc nyei jauv, Yeˆhoˆwaa a’hneiv nyei zipv ninh mbuo bieqc daaih benx zangc ninh nyei biauv zong mienh. Se gorngv meih zipv Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei cingv fienx nor, meih duqv ninh benx meih nyei doic yaac duqv dongh zangc Yeˆhoˆwaa wuov deix benx meih nyei siang-hmuangv-doic. Ninh mbuo maaih hnyiouv hnamv mienh. Maiv daan mbuoqc naaic, meih yaac duqv yietc liuz nyei maengc.—Doqc Maako 10:29, 30; 2 Koˆlinˆto 6:17, 18.

eng: 4. How will God bring joy to every land?
imn: 4. Tin-Hungh hnangv haaix nor dorh njien-yiouh nyei jauv bieqc norm-norm deic-bung?

eng: The coming judgment of false religion is good news. It will bring worldwide relief from oppression. Never again will false religion mislead and divide mankind. Everyone living will be united in the worship of the only true God.—Read Revelation 18:20, 21; 21:3, 4.
imn: Taux ziangh hoc siemv jaav buoqc zangc nyei jauv se benx kuv fienx. Naaic se bungx nqoi mienh maanh gormx naaiv baamh gen, bun ninh mbuo duqv kuv-yiem, maiv zuqc maaih haaix nyungc daaih zoux doqc zoux hoic ninh mbuo. Yiem naaic mingh maiv aengx maaih jaav buoqc zangc nyei jauv daaih nduov mienh maanh bun nqoi benx ziex guanh aqv. Dauh dauh yiem njiec se dongh diuc hnyiouv kungx zangc nduqc dauh zien Tin-Hungh hnangv.—Doqc Laauc Yaangh 18:20, 21; 21:3, 4.

eng: Why Does God Have an Organization?
imn: Weic Haaix Diuc Tin-Hungh Maaih Norm Gorn Zangc?

eng: 1. Why did God organize ancient Israel?
imn: 1. Weic haaix diuc zinh ndaangc Tin-Hungh dorh Iˆsaaˆlaaˆen Mienh gapv zunv maaih dongh diuc hnyiouv?

eng: God organized descendants of the patriarch Abraham into a nation and gave them a body of laws. He called the nation “Israel” and made it the custodian of true worship and of his word. (Psalm 147:19, 20) So people of all nations could benefit from Israel.—Read Genesis 22:18.
imn: Zinh ndaangc maaih dauh m’jangc dorn heuc Apcˆlaaˆham. Ninh ei Yeˆhoˆwaa Tin-Hungh nyei waac zoux. Tin-Hungh dorh Apcˆlaaˆham nyei zeiv-fun gapv zunv benx yietc guoqv mienh, liuz bun doz-leiz ninh mbuo. Ninh cuotv mbuox heuc naaiv norm guoqv “Iˆsaaˆlaaˆen,” liuz bun ninh mbuo zoux gorn dorh zien buoqc zangc nyei jauv yaac dorh ninh nyei waac. (Singx Nzung 147:19, 20) Hnangv naaic, maanc guoqv nyei mienh oix duqv longx weic Iˆsaaˆlaaˆen.—Doqc Tin Deic Douh 22:18.

eng: God chose the Israelites to be his witnesses. Their ancient history provides a demonstration of how people benefit by obeying God’s laws. (Deuteronomy 4:6) Thus, through the Israelites, others could get to know the true God.—Read Isaiah 43:10, 12.
imn: Tin-Hungh ginv Iˆsaaˆlaaˆen Mienh benx ninh nyei zorng-zengx mienh. Ninh mbuo zinh ndaangc zoux jiex nyei jauv-louc bun hiuv mienh hnangv haaix nor duqv longx se gorngv ei Tin-Hungh nyei leiz nor. (Nzamc Leiz-Latc 4:6) Hnangv naaic, weic Iˆsaaˆlaaˆen Mienh, dieh deix cingx daaih haih hiuv duqv zien wuov dauh Tin-Hungh.—Doqc Iˆsaaˆyaa 43:10, 12.

eng: 2. Why are true Christians organized?
imn: 2. Weic haaix diuc zien Giduc Mienh gapv zunv maaih dongh diuc hnyiouv?

eng: In time, Israel lost God’s favor, and Jehovah replaced that nation with the Christian congregation. (Matthew 21:43; 23:37, 38) Now, in place of the Israelites, true Christians serve as Jehovah’s witnesses.—Read Acts 15:14, 17.
imn: Zoux gau, Iˆsaaˆlaaˆen Mienh maiv kangv zoux horpc Tin-Hungh, liuz Yeˆhoˆwaa guangc naaic guoqv mienh ziouc dorh Giduc Mienh daaih div ninh mbuo. (Matvˆtaai 21:43; 23:37, 38) Guangc liuz Iˆsaaˆlaaˆen Mienh, Yeˆhoˆwaa dorh zien Giduc Mienh daaih benx ninh nyei zorng-zengx mienh.—Doqc Gong-Zoh 15:14, 17.

eng: Jesus organized his followers to preach and make disciples in all nations. (Matthew 10:7, 11; 24:14; 28:19, 20) This work is reaching its climax now, in the conclusion of the present system of things. For the first time in history, Jehovah has united millions from all nations in true worship. (Revelation 7:9, 10) True Christians are organized to encourage and help one another. Worldwide, they enjoy the same program of Bible instruction at their meetings.—Read Hebrews 10:24, 25.
imn: Wuov zanc, Yesu heuc dongh gan ninh nyei mienh mingh zunh doz njaaux maanc fingx mienh benx ninh nyei sai-gorx. (Matvˆtaai 10:7, 11; 24:14; 28:19, 20) Ih zanc se yiem zuqc setv mueiz nyei hnoi-nyieqc. Zunh doz nyei gong se aav lamh ziangx aqv. Yiem zinh ndaangc daaih taux ih zanc, naaiv se daauh nzunc Yeˆhoˆwaa dorh ziex baeqv waanc dauh mienh yiem norm-norm guoqv daaih gapv zunv dongh hnyiouv nyei longc zien leiz zangc ninh. (Laauc Yaangh 7:9, 10) Zien Giduc Mienh se dongh diuc hnyiouv nyei laanh kuinx laanh yaac tengx doic. Gormx naaiv lungh ndiev, ninh mbuo nyei wuic njaaux taux Ging-Sou nyei jauv-louc se fih hnangv nyei.—Doqc Hipvˆlu 10:24, 25.

eng: 3. How did the modern-day organization of Jehovah’s Witnesses begin?
imn: 3. Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx nyei ih jaax hnoi gorn zangc se hnangv haaix nor jiez gorn daaih?

eng: In the 1870’s a small group of Bible students began rediscovering long-lost Bible truths. They knew that Jesus had organized the Christian congregation to preach, so they began an international Kingdom-preaching campaign. In 1931 they adopted the name Jehovah’s Witnesses.—Read Acts 1:8; 2:1, 4; 5:42.
imn: Yiem gu’nyuoz 1870 mingh taux 1879 wuov deix hnyangx, maaih guanh zoqc nyei horqc saeng hoqc taux Ging-Sou ziouc jiez gorn lorz buatc Ging-Sou nyei zien leiz. Ninh mbuo hiuv duqv wuov zanc Yesu njaaux Giduc Mienh mingh zunh doz, liuz ninh mbuo ziouc jiez gorn mingh ziex norm deic-bung zunh doz gorngv taux Tin-Hungh nyei Guoqv. Taux 1931 wuov hnyangx, ninh mbuo duqv Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx naaiv norm mbuox daaih longc.—Doqc Gong-Zoh 1:8; 2:1, 4; 5:42.

eng: 4. How are Jehovah’s Witnesses organized?
imn: 4. Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx hnangv haaix nor gapv zunv maaih dongh diuc hnyiouv?

eng: In the first century, the Christian congregations in many lands benefited from a central governing body that recognized Jesus as the Head of the congregation. (Acts 16:4, 5) Similarly today, Jehovah’s Witnesses worldwide benefit from a Governing Body of experienced elders. It oversees branch offices of Jehovah’s Witnesses that translate, print, and distribute Bible-study material in over 600 languages. Thus the Governing Body can provide Scriptural encouragement and direction for over 100,000 congregations worldwide. In each congregation, qualified men serve as elders, or overseers. These men lovingly care for God’s flock.—Read 1 Peter 5:2, 3.
imn: Yiem gu’nyuoz daauh baeqv hnyangx, maaih guanh mienh gox se zoux gorn goux Giduc Mienh nyei yietc zungv jiu-bang yiem ziex norm deic-bung, se dorh jauv bun jiu-bang maaih sienx fim longx. Naaic deix mienh gox zieqv duqv Yesu se zoux jiu-bang nyei m’nqorngv. (Gong-Zoh 16:4, 5) Ih jaax hnoi yaac fih hnangv nyei, maaih guanh mienh gox se zoux gorn goux yietc zungv jiu-bang gormx naaiv lungh ndiev, se dorh jauv bun Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx maaih sienx fim longx. Naaiv guanh mienh gox se goux Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx nyei gorn zangc dongh faan waac, yienx sou, caux fungx cuotv longc hoqc Ging-Sou nyei ga’naaiv wuov. Longc hoqc Ging-Sou nyei ga’naaiv se faan daaih maaih jiex ndaangc 800 nyungc waac. Hnangv naaic, naaiv guanh mienh gox haih bun cuotv Ging-Sou nyei waac daaih kuinx caux dorh jauv jiex ndaangc 120,000 norm jiu-bang gormx naaiv lungh ndiev. Yiem yietc norm yietc norm jiu-bang, puix duqv benx mienh gox nyei m’jangc dorn wuov deix se zoux mienh gox. Naaic deix jiu-bang nyei mienh gox se longc hnyiouv goux longx jiu-bang nyei mienh.—Doqc 1 Bide 5:2, 3.

eng: Jehovah’s Witnesses are organized to preach the good news and make disciples. Like the apostles, we preach from house to house. (Acts 20:20) We also offer to study the Bible with sincere lovers of truth. But Jehovah’s Witnesses are not just an organization. We are a family with a loving Father. We are brothers and sisters who care for one another. (2 Thessalonians 1:3) Since Jehovah’s people are organized to please God and to help others, they form the happiest family on earth.—Read Psalm 33:12; Acts 20:35.
imn: Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx mbuo mbenc jauv daaih zunh kuv fienx caux njaaux mienh benx Yesu nyei sai-gorx. Yie mbuo yiem yietc norm biauv zunh jiex yietc norm biauv, m’daaih hnangv zinh ndaangc gong-zoh mbuo zoux wuov nor. (Gong-Zoh 20:20) Yie mbuo yaac tengx dongh maaih hnyiouv oix gan zien leiz wuov deix hoqc bieqc hnyiouv Ging-Sou. Mv baac Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx se maiv zeiz kungx benx norm gorn zangc hnangv. Yie mbuo se benx yietc hmuangv mienh juangc dauh hnamv haic dorn-jueiv nyei Die. Yie mbuo se benx gorx-yiouz dorc muoc, laanh hnamv laanh. (2 Teˆsaaˆloˆniˆgaa 1:3) Weic Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei mienh mbenc jauv daaih zoux horpc Tin-Hungh caux tengx dieh deix mienh, ninh mbuo gapv daaih se benx a’hneiv jiex nyei mienh hmuangv yiem naaiv baamh gen.—Doqc Singx Nzung 33:12; Gong-Zoh 20:35.

eng: Why Should You Continue?
imn: Weic Haaix Diuc Meih Horpc Zuqc Hoqc Jienv Mingh?

eng: 1. How will continuing your Bible study benefit you?
imn: 1. Hoqc jienv Ging-Sou mingh se hnangv haaix nor haih tengx duqv meih?

eng: No doubt, this overview of basic Bible teachings has strengthened your love for Jehovah. This love needs constant cultivation. (1 Peter 2:2) Your hope of everlasting life depends on whether you continue to draw close to God by studying his Word.—Read John 17:3; Jude 21.
imn: Hnamv daaih naaiv deix Ging-Sou nyei jauv-louc se tengx meih aengx gauh haih hnamv Yeˆhoˆwaa. Hnamv Tin-Hungh se a’zuqc zanc-zanc longc hnyiouv zoux nyungc-nyungc daaih bun meih hnamv ninh aengx gauh wuonv. (1 Bide 2:2) Meih hnamv jienv oix duqv yietc liuz nyei maengc wuov, naaic se m’daaih yiem meih ganh aengx hoqc jienv Ging-Sou mingh, tengx meih mingh nitv jienv Tin-Hungh.—Doqc Yoˆhan 17:3; Yiuˆndaa 21.

eng: As you grow in knowledge of God, your faith will become stronger. Faith will help you to please God. (Hebrews 11:1, 6) It will move you to repent and to make beneficial changes in your way of life.—Read Acts 3:19.
imn: Meih hoqc hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh camv jienv faaux nor, meih ziouc haih maaih sienx fim aengx gauh longx. Maaih sienx fim se haih tengx meih zoux horpc Tin-Hungh, bun Tin-Hungh a’hneiv. (Hipvˆlu 11:1, 6) Meih ziouc oix goiv hnyiouv guangc zuiz yaac oix yienc daaih zoux longx nyei jauv.—Doqc Gong-Zoh 3:19.

eng: 2. How could your knowledge of God benefit others?
imn: 2. Hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh se bun meih hnangv haaix nor haih tengx duqv dieh deix?

eng: Naturally, you will want to share with others what you have learned—we all enjoy telling good news. As you continue studying the Bible, you will learn how to use the Bible to explain your faith in Jehovah and the good news.—Read Romans 10:13-15.
imn: Hnamv daaih meih hoqc hiuv duqv haaix nyungc liuz, meih ziouc oix bun dieh deix caux jienv hiuv weic zuqc mbuo yietc zungv m’daaih oix mbuox mienh kuv fienx nyei. Hoqc jienv Ging-Sou mingh nor, meih haih hiuv duqv hnangv haaix nor longc Ging-Sou porv mengh Tin-Hungh nyei kuv fienx caux meih sienx Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei jauv.—Doqc Matvˆtaai 28:19, 20.

eng: Most people start by sharing the good news with friends or relatives. Be tactful. Rather than saying that their religion is mistaken, tell them about God’s promises. Also, remember that people are often more impressed by your kind behavior than by what you say.—Read 2 Timothy 2:24, 25.
imn: Jiez gorn nor, mienh nzengc-nzengc dorh Tin-Hungh nyei kuv fienx mbuox taux ninh mbuo nyei a’nziaauc doic caux mienh muoz cien-ceqv ndaangc. Mv baac, a’zuqc dorh leiz nyei mbuox. Gorngv taux Tin-Hungh laengz jienv nyei jauv bun ninh mbuo muangx, maiv zeiz yietc zaqc ziang naaic gorngv ninh mbuo sienx nyei jauv se dorngc mi’aqc. Jiangx jienv, mienh nzengc-nzengc gauh oix taaih meih weic meih zoux eix-leiz longx, maiv zeiz weic meih gorngv nyei waac.—Doqc 2 Tiˆmoˆtai 2:24, 25.

eng: 3. What relationship with God can you enjoy?
imn: 3. Meih hnangv haaix nor haih a’hneiv nyei duqv benx Tin-Hungh nyei?

eng: Studying God’s Word will help you to grow spiritually. Eventually, you may enter into a very special relationship with Jehovah. You can actually belong to him as a member of his family.—Read 2 Corinthians 6:18.
imn: Hoqc Ging-Sou se tengx meih haih hiuv duqv Tin-Hungh nyei jauv camv jienv faaux bun meih haih nitv ninh. Jiex mingh, meih yaac haih duqv lengc jeiv haic nyei buonc caux Yeˆhoˆwaa horpc hnyiouv. Meih zungv gengh haih benx dauh ninh nyei biauv zong mienh, benx ninh nyei buonc.—Doqc 2 Koˆlinˆto 6:18.

eng: 4. How can you continue to progress?
imn: 4. Meih hnangv haaix nor haih bieqc hnyiouv camv jienv mingh?

eng: You can advance spiritually by continuing to study God’s Word. (Hebrews 5:13, 14) Ask one of Jehovah’s Witnesses to study the Bible with you using the book What Does the Bible Really Teach? The more you learn from God’s Word, the more successful your life will be.—Read Psalm 1:1-3; 73:27, 28.
imn: Hoqc jienv Tin-Hungh nyei Waac mingh, maiv guangc nor, meih haih bieqc hnyiouv camv jienv mingh. (Hipvˆlu 5:13, 14) Heuc dauh Yeˆhoˆwaa nyei Zorng-Zengx longc What Can the Bible Teach Us? wuov buonv sou tengx meih bieqc hnyiouv Ging-Sou. Hoqc bieqc hnyiouv Tin-Hungh nyei Waac aengx gauh camv se tengx meih aengx gauh haih duqv longx.—Doqc Singx Nzung 1:1-3; 73:27, 28.

eng: The good news comes from Jehovah, the happy God. You can draw closer to him by drawing closer to his people. (Hebrews 10:24, 25) By continuing your efforts to please Jehovah, you are reaching out for the real life—everlasting life. Drawing close to God is truly the best thing that you can do.—Read 1 Timothy 1:11; 6:19.
imn: Wuov kuv fienx se yiem Yeˆhoˆwaa daaih, se njien-yiouh orn-lorqc nyei Tin-Hungh. Oix nitv ninh aengx gauh fatv nor, oix zuqc mingh nitv ninh nyei mienh aengx gauh fatv. (Hipvˆlu 10:24, 25) Zanc-zanc longc hnyiouv zoux horpc Yeˆhoˆwaa nor, meih haih duqv zien maengc, se yietc liuz nyei maengc. Mingh nitv jienv Tin-Hungh se gengh longx haic nyei jauv meih haih zoux.—Doqc 1 Tiˆmoˆtai 1:11; 6:19.
